### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-01
For those that don't know what Ubuntu Ultimate is you can learn about the distro from my [website]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/faq.html#ultimate").

I will be editing this post to keep people informed:

**10/25/07**  I will no longer be updating this post it is now being updated on my [News & Events]("http://forumubuntusoftware.info/viewforum.php?f=2") section of my forum.  Just to give a heads up U.U.E. 1.6 is really close to "official" release.  Those that are registered on my forum will get it even before it has a webpage even written, you can learn the benefits of registering [here]("http://forumubuntusoftware.info/viewtopic.php?f=14&t=27").  So please take the time and [register]("http://forumubuntusoftware.info/ucp.php?mode=register"), before it is too late.

**10/22/07** I don't have time to update both forums, I suggest you read the [News & Events]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/posting.php?mode=edit&f=2&p=3") on my forum, you will want to register as soon as you see yesterdays post.  I am uploading U.U.E. 1.6 as I type this.  Only registered users will be able to see when it goes "Hot for release" & download it directly from a hidden post (viewable only by registered users) on my forum.  It will be posted to the main site later after I write a webpage for it.

**10/20/07** Work on Ubuntu Ultimate 1.6 is going slow however I am posting from the Live DVD, which works perfectly thus far  about 300MB of nothing but updates.  Thought I would toss down a few screenshots (can be viewed in [News & Events]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/posting.php?mode=edit&f=2&p=3") section on my forum).  Wallpaper login etc. are subject to change based on your [vote](http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/viewforum.php?f=16).

**10/19/07** Yes, I know Gusty Gibson has been released as final and have been building from it to make 1.6 no easy [task]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/viewtopic.php?f=8&t=43").  If you would like to learn how the release cycle works with U.U.E. you can learn about that [here]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/viewtopic.php?f=8&t=30&p=79&hilit=Happy+harry#p79").  I highly suggest you take the time and register on my forum, those that do will be able to view a thread that guests cannot see.  It will be under [News & Events]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/viewforum.php?f=2") a release before even a webpage is written for 1.6, most likely days in advance.

**10/16/07**  [Our forum is now online]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/forum/") and open for registration and posts, please bare in mind there will be some initial growing pains... Enjoy !!!

**10/06/07** Got talent?  Graphic artists wanted, would you like to have your work seen by 100's of thousands of people?  I have set up a poll read more about that [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=568585").

**10/04/07** I know it has been a long time since I posted, got a job takes up alot of my time not that I have not been busy pre-building what will be 1.6 (Gusty Gibson based), I have made a new grub splash, Usplash theme, GDM theme, wallpaper and theme for it, you can read more about that [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3473502&postcount=1286").  Been setting up our own forum as well not ready for mainstream traffic yet, will keep you informed.

**09/22/07**  [1.5 has been released]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.5/")!!!  I will not post it internally until torrent is brought online, so as it sits right now a ubuntuforum exclusive enjoy.  Funny an admin moved my thread today, an admin told me to post it where it was.

**09/20/06**  WOW an idiot I am.. .  I may have openly said so as far as corruption jfg69, brought it to my attention (Think that is his nick).  Perhaps as I indeed have said in the past, the upload is corrupted seen it first 2nd hand, sorry I will now name snaple he took the "Pepsi challenge" and is no new comer to traffic.  I am re-uploading it as I type this.  Did not want you to think I was[ B.S. ing]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3398400&postcount=1107") you.

**09/18/06** It simply blows my mind the pounding going on across the board, I have spoken with a gentleman this eve (no names mentioned) he holds the "key" on the release of 1.5, currently all servers including mine are at max capacity with 1.4 (2.6 TB this week), this guy if he is up to the "Pepsi" challenge wiill feel a huge punch from the snap.  Even if he does not accept, we will still have a release but it will be slow (Irregardless I suggest you torrent to help others).
1 of 10 *can you imagine what ccal has seen, or other mirrors?:
```
6135179.23 MB of 250000.00 MB (2454.07%)
```

**09/15/07** Due to the many repeated questions I have decided to create a Forum based [F.A.Q.]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3370885&postcount=1041")  This will take some time to complete perhaps your question is already answered  there.  I should be spending my time writing a webpage for 1.5 it is uploaded and ready for release.

**09/14/07** I have discussed it with ccal and he does not care (he has unlimited bandwidth and pays the same no matter how many people "hit" him") there will be a 1.5 release (uploading it now), however I will be unable to host my own release, a lot of weight will be on his shoulders.  Ccal deserves a huge thanks, he is about to be at max capacity for a long time to come, I will adjust the script to remove his repo from your list 200 people at a time is more then anyone should ask for.  After traffic calms down I will update the script and put it back in, the script update will be no time soon, obviously I have "bigger fish to fry", I wish people could see what I do.

**09/12/07** I have completed and am currently running UUE 1.5 (Gnome, KDE, Enlightenment, Window Maker & XFCE included) each environment 9 in all are selectable from the login window.  I have made a short [screencast]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/screencasts/kde.avi") of the KDE environment & posted a few [screenshots]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3354935&postcount=994") of Gnome environment.  With the current amount of [traffic]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3355692&postcount=996") it is unknown when / if it will even be released the servers are at maximum capacity, if traffic slows down perhaps we will have a release; those of you that run 1.4+ will want to wait for the script update, this will allow you to upgrade to 1.5 w/o having to download the 2.2 Gig 1.5 release.  Will post more info as it becomes available.

**09/07/07** Updated [Super Tweaking Ultimate]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Howto_tweak_ubuntu_ultimate.html") page, now have UUE 1.4 Gamers booting into gnome in 15 secs ;)  Bonus tip added for those with dual core CPU's.  If and only if you like the end results, please provide me a few [kudos.]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Super_Tweaking_Ultimate_Feisty").  Probably by the time you read it fully a thing of the past, a large webpage ;)

**09/04/07** Paid for the repo & download server to be put back online, repo problems should now be a thing of the past.  The download server however will take at least 24 hours to refill (unless ccal helps me out).

**08/30/07** Due to the great work of Ccal as always.  Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 Gamers is now available for [download]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4_Gamers/"), [screencast]("http://youtube.com/watch?v=duhAtZT11vw").  Enjoy!!!  His repo is now online as well downloading from it now ;)  If you have time I'd appreciate a few [diggs]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1_4_Gamers_Edition_i386_DVD_Now_Available_at_LinuxTracker").  Lets see if ccal can handle the digg effect ;)

**08/29/07** Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4  Gamers edition (64 Bit) is now uploaded, 18 hours to upload 1.4 Gamers X86 (16 1/2 left).

**08/28/07** Repo is now mirrored via ccal, 2 standard repo mirrors and one on the way ;)  Will be set in motion in a few days.  UUE 1.4 Gamers Edition (AMD64 or EM64T architecture (e.g., Athlon64, Opteron, EM64T Xeon) has been built and is uploading as I type this for those of you with 64 bit CPU's.  There is also a standard X86 release (already done and ready for upload) for those with intel or don't want to run a 64 bit O/S, a huge download most games are integrated on the DVD.   You can read more about it [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3266670&postcount=862").  If you would like to see the list of games included you can read more about that from my [website]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4_Gamers/") please note this page is under construction as of time of this post, don't expect the download links to work yet, screenshots and screencast are from 1.4 DVD not Gamers yet.

**08/21/07** Repo and download server are down here is [why]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3225468&postcount=725"), please don't click on the upgrade Icon until they are back up, will take days in just re-uploading, thanks for your patience and sorry for the inconvenience.

**08/18/07** Even though a re-built Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers 1.4 (smaller & windows users happy), I will not [release]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3212131&postcount=699") anything that cant be downloaded, no matter the OS you run; release has been uploaded to main server, there will be no "official" release until ccal comes back from Italy (1 week and & 5 days), or someone PM's me with unlimited bandwidth and ability to serve a hungry crowd.

Example:
```
Bandwidth:  	 4579333.41 MB of 2000000.00 MB (228.97%)
```

This release will exceed that manifold...  Not just in size (4GB) but demand.  If you have 4GB of space and unlimited bandwidth (what you see above is Terabytes not Gigabytes of bandwidth, thought I would shut down the PM's before started), please message me.

**08/14/07** I have written a [How to Super Tweak Ultimate]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Howto_tweak_ubuntu_ultimate.html") page, even though it is written specifically with Ubuntu Ultimate in mind I see no reason why a Feisty Fawn user can't use it as well.  Enjoy!!!

**08/12/07**  Sorry to inform you that we may not have a release exempt a ubuntuforum exclusive.  You most likely will not be able to download Gamers unless you run linux.  Due to the limitation in [Windows]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3175230&postcount=543")
 this is the best case situation, I may have to re-build it all.

**08/10/07 Gamers Edition is done!!!** You can read more about it that [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3168828&postcount=511").  Will probably be 2 days before you can download it between uploading & mirroring a release of this size.

**08/07/07** I wrote a brief [howto]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") covering installation of UUE DVD & Nvidia Driver installation.  Testing a build of Gamers Edition a bit over 4GB!!!  Download server has exceed it's bandwidth limits after almost 400,000 downloads of UUE 1.4 DVD and almost 200,000 of 1.4 CD, simply crazy and done in short order.

**08/04/07** I thought I would shttp://hare with you all the making of UUE 1.4 Gamers, most wont be able to view it unless you run UUE 1.4 or have the codecs installed, first [beginning]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/test/starting"), and [midstride]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/test/midstride") along.  Please note no extension *nix doesn't need them ;)  Going quite well thus far.  245,517 downloads of 1.4 DVD from main download server only.  Not bad for 2 days ;)  Massive [traffic]("http://ubuntuforums.org/attachment.php?attachmentid=39812&d=1186257394").  Progress on [UUE 1.4 Gamers Edition]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3134528&postcount=423").  We really could use some mirrors, post if you can help.

**08/03/07** Mirror & torrent now setup.  Please feel free to give me a few "[diggs]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Ubuntu_Ultimate_Edition_1_4_DVD_Gnarly_Gnome_Released")", I dugg my own website I want to see if it remains up, the propose of having 3 to begin with.  Thanks.

**08/02/07**  [Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 DVD]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4/") is available for download enjoy!!!.  [Screencast]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vLUXjXo7MuU").  Mirrors & torrents will be set up tomorrow.

**07/31/07** [Ubuntu Ultimate Edition 1.4 DVD]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4/") is now uploading ~2GB will take nothing short of 9 hours to upload, mirrors please stand by.  Many [wireless issues]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3063468") have been addressed and integrated onto the DVD ([screenshots]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3111424&postcount=379")).

I would like to apologize if I do not immediately get back with you, I hope you can understand I am a very busy Mahn :), I do try and respond to each and every post.

**Looking for help or answers please view our old [thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=309704").  Still can't find an answer?  Please feel free to post.**

Below are screen shots of 1.4 DVD version...  And a [screencast]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vLUXjXo7MuU"), a bit choppy sorry about that happened when converted from oog / vorbis to avi..

### Post by Scruffynerf on 2007-06-03
Interesting to hear that the Ultimate based off Feisty is around.

Whilst I am here, I'd just like to thank TheeMahn2003 for this release - vanilla ubuntu's never installed on my system, just used to hang on install. Ultimate (1.3 - Edgy release) has been a dream - as a newbie, I've never had to do a re-install, and most things worked out of the box.

Would highly recommend this project for those looking to migrate from windows. 

cheers

Scruffy.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-03
> **adewale said:**
> hello,
its a relief to hear that the project isn't dead. i would like to know that would this only come with Gnome, why not include KDE also and something to help install the nvidia driver from the cd or better still pre-installed. Thanks

KDE has not been included **YET**, however when 1.4 goes final it will be included, the nvidia driver or ati can be installed from the live cd or after installation using a tool called [envy]("http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html") this is automatically ran for you if you choose to install video drivers see screenshot [here]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ultimate_Beta/Screenshots/Screenshot-1.png").

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-03
> **Scruffynerf said:**
> Interesting to hear that the Ultimate based off Feisty is around.

Whilst I am here, I'd just like to thank TheeMahn2003 for this release - vanilla ubuntu's never installed on my system, just used to hang on install. Ultimate (1.3 - Edgy release) has been a dream - as a newbie, I've never had to do a re-install, and most things worked out of the box.

Would highly recommend this project for those looking to migrate from windows. 

cheers

Scruffy.

Thank you for your kind comments, this release (running it currently) by being based off Feisty has been a real chore to build, it is no where near complete the script I need to finish to allow it to fit on a cd is a lot of work.  IMHO I feel Ultimate 1.2 to be the best release I have put out it was very stable and everything just worked w/o having to add this or that.  I am glad to hear you have had such great success with 1.3, the main enhancements added to 1.3 was security, however I think I overdid it I bought a book yes I do find time to read on occasion ;)  called Hardning Linux a great book if you are looking to secure your box. One of the security enhancements in 1.3 encrypted a disk I burnt for a buddy and he couldn't read it when I put it back in my system it read fine, all though some may call that a security enhancement I call it an annoyance.

Thanks again for your comments

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-04
> **lukeo said:**
> Feisty 7.04 installs fine on my PC, basic specs Nforce 4 ultra, 4800+, 2GB ram, 8800gts. Ultimate gives me a X failed to start and it will not boot off the DVD. Shame really.

```
envy -t
```

and select nvidia.

I would like to start off by saying this is a beta, many bugs and omissions, but work in progress.  In the past I have released only what I consider stable, however I feel, I should allow people a chance to participate in creating what will be Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4, codename: Gnarly Gnome.  I may go back to the ways of the past depending how this works out.

I have thought about scraping Feisty Based all together it has been a huge problem to build from the snap and going back to edgy, where I seem to have a lot less problems.  I installed the beta w/o a hitch, it is a beta, I fully expect people as well as myself to have problems on my homepage I clearly state it is not for everyone, I'm sorry your mileage was not as great as mine.

### Post by spitingcamel on 2007-06-05
I normally rely on Ultimate Ubuntu as I find it excellent and has all the necessary programs I need so after the recent upgrade I thought I'd wait for the Ultimate edition,so a bit disappointed  to see this is a beta as I am a complete newbie to linux and prefer to have everything handed to me on a plate like windows,at the moment am running Windows Vista Basic on a Sony Viao IGB Ram ATI Graphics card which of course I cant upgrade the graphics card to get the glass effects of vista,but with Ubuntu and Beryl I find it a thousands times better than vista,strange how I have no problem with 3D effects with Beryl but denied it with vista,yes I know vista basic does not have aero,I also have vista premium,yes I like to waste money,anyway tried your Ultimate Ubuntu Beta from the DVD and was very much impressed,was even able to play my videos from the windows folder all from the DVD,wont install yet untill the stable version is released,so please don't give up,your doing a great job!

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-05
> **spitingcamel said:**
> I normally rely on Ultimate Ubuntu as I find it excellent and has all the necessary programs I need so after the recent upgrade I thought I'd wait for the Ultimate edition,so a bit disappointed  to see this is a beta as I am a complete newbie to linux and prefer to have everything handed to me on a plate like windows,at the moment am running Windows Vista Basic on a Sony Viao IGB Ram ATI Graphics card which of course I cant upgrade the graphics card to get the glass effects of vista,but with Ubuntu and Beryl I find it a thousands times better than vista,strange how I have no problem with 3D effects with Beryl but denied it with vista,yes I know vista basic does not have aero,I also have vista premium,yes I like to waste money,anyway tried your Ultimate Ubuntu Beta from the DVD and was very much impressed,was even able to play my videos from the windows folder all from the DVD,wont install yet untill the stable version is released,so please don't give up,your doing a great job!

Thank you, it is indeed far from final all I was looking for is a chance to let others "test it out" just as you have done.  I think I would be hesitant to install the beta as well, however I have no choice ;) Scripting it to the CD version is a long way away, but I look for it to be the best solution allowing the end user to pick and choose what they would like installed, grabbing all the packages & prerequisites from the internet & playing music as it does so; if the end user opts for it.  By the time it's done the music it plays now as it downloads drivers etc. "Halloween" may be fitting.  I have downloaded 120,000 mods, s3m etc. so I may have a poll for what you would like to hear as it "takes care of business" this also will be in the future.

I would have to agree with you on the beryl thing it is really something to see as it has much lower requirements then for vista, I have recently purchased 2 nvidia geforce 7800gts (SLI mode) with tripple view (probably when I have the $ gonna buy 3 of [these]("http://www.antaresdigital.com/customer/product.php?productid=16758&cat=359&page=2") to go with it), have yet to hook up a third monitor; it impresses everyone who has seen it and makes vista look like a joke.

I'm sorry to announce that I have gotten another job (roofing) now 4 jobs if you include building Ubuntu a job so progress will be much slower unless someone else would like to pay my bills ;)

Thanks again

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-05
I thought I would share a bit of the scripts progress this will only work currently with the Ubuntu Ultimate Beta please feel free to try it yourself.  I will soon attach screenshots.  Below is the code for the bash script currently Gamers Edition and Custom only work.

```
#!/bin/bash
# Ubuntu Ultimate Edition Easy upgrade script Via TheeMahn
#
#
# Copyright (c) 2006  Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
if [ ! -e "/usr/bin/zenity" ]; then
	gksudo apt-get install -y zenity
fi

#Check for update to script here


#is it running off the Live CD?
if [ $USERNAME = "ubuntu" ]; then
    `zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text="This upgrade scipt can only be ran after hard disk installation." --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition"`;
    exit 1
fi

# Are we running on Feisty?
if ( ! grep "Ubuntu 7.04" /etc/issue >/dev/null 2>&1); then
     `zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text="This scipt can only be ran on Feisty based distros." --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition"`;
    exit 1
fi

timidity -Os -A130 -p3 -Ei5 -module=2 -q20 -T140 /usr/share/ultimate/halloween_theme.s3m&choice="$(zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png  --width=400 --height=180 --title "Ubuntu Ultimate Edition" --text "Choose distro path" --list --radiolist --column "Install" --column "Distro" true 'Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers Edition' false 'Ubuntu Ultimate Edition' false 'Custom')"

warn() {
	if ! zenity --question --title "Distro selected" --text "${choice}
	Do you wish to grab the default packages for the above distro?"; then
		exit 0
	fi
}

baseinstall() {
gksudo apt-get update | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Updating source list"
gksudo apt-get upgrade -y --allow-unauthenticated | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Upgrading base distro..."
}

debinstall() {
INSTALLED=$(apt-cache policy $1 | grep '^ *Installed: ' | sed -e 's/ *Installed: *//')
echo "INSTALLED = \"$INSTALLED\""
if [ $INSTALLED = "(none)" ]; then
echo "installing $1"
#download it
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/$2 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading $3..."
#install it
sudo dpkg -i $2 2>&1 | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing $3..."
gksudo rm $2
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabing $3 Dependancies"
else
echo "$3 Already installed"
fi
}

gamers() {
Action_Cube
Alien_Arena
Open_Arena
Scourge
Glest
Boswars
Sauerbraten
Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
Vdrift
UFO_AI
Warsow
Mania_Drive
Supertux
Astromenace
Blob_and_conquer
Dark_Oberon

killall timidity
}

######################################################## GAMEZ ##############################################

Action_Cube() {
#actioncube
debinstall actioncube actioncube_0.92-1getdeb1_i386.deb Action_cube
}

Alien_Arena() {
#alien Arena
debinstall alienarena2007 alienarena2007_6.04-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Alien_Arena
}

Open_Arena() {
#open arena
debinstall openarena openarena_0.6.0-1getdeb1_i386.deb Open_Arena
}

Glest() {
#glest
debinstall glest-data glest-data_2.0.0-0~getdeb1_all.deb Glest_Data
debinstall glest glest_2.0.0-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Glest
}

Scourge() {
debinstall scourge scourge_0.18-1~getdeb1_i386.deb Scourge
debinstall scourge-data scourge-data_0.18-1~getdeb1_all.deb Scourge_Data
}

Boswars() {
debinstall boswars boswars-data_2.3-0~getdeb1_all.deb Boswars
debinstall boswars-data boswars_2.3-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Boswars_Data
}

Sauerbraten() {
debinstall sauerbraten-data sauerbraten-data_0.0.20070415-0~getdeb1_all.deb Sauerbraten_Data
debinstall sauerbraten sauerbraten_0.0.20070415-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Sauerbraten
}

Secret_Maryo_Chronicles() {
debinstall smc-data smc-data_0.99.6-0~getdeb1_all.deb Secret_Maryo_Chronicles_Data
debinstall smc smc_0.99.6-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
}

Vdrift() {
debinstall vdrift-full vdrift-full_0.0.2007.03.23-0~getdeb1_all.deb Vdrift_Data
debinstall vdrift vdrift_0.0.2007.03.23-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Vdrift
}

UFO_AI(){
debinstall ufoai ufoai-data_2.1-0~getdeb1_all.deb UFO_AI
}

Warsow() {
debinstall warsow-data warsow-data_0.3-1~getdeb1_all.deb Warsow_Data
debinstall warsow warsow_0.3-1~getdeb1_i386.deb Warsow
}

Mania_Drive() {
debinstall maniadrive-data maniadrive-data_1.2-0~getdeb1_all.deb Maniadrive_Data
debinstall maniadrive maniadrive_1.2-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Mania_Drive
}

Supertux() {
debinstall supertux-data supertux-data_0.3.0-1getdeb1_all.deb Supertux_Data
debinstall supertux supertux_0.3.0-1getdeb1_i386.deb Supertux
}

Astromenace() {
debinstall astromenace astromenace_0.46b-1getdeb1_i386.deb Astromenace
}

Blob_and_conquer() {
debinstall blobandconquer-data blobandconquer-data_0.90-1~getdeb1_all.deb Blob_and_conquer_data
debinstall blobandconquer blobandconquer_0.90-1~getdeb1_i386.deb Blob_and_conquer
}

Dark_Oberon() {
debinstall dark-oberon-data dark-oberon-data_1.0.1+1.0.2RC1-0~getdeb1_all.deb Dark_Oberon_Data
debinstall dark-oberon dark-oberon_1.0.1+1.0.2RC1-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Dark_Oberon
}

Wesnoth() {
debinstall wesnoth-music wesnoth-music_1.2.4-0~getdeb1_all.deb Wesnoth_Music
debinstall wesnoth-data wesnoth-data_1.2.4-0~getdeb1_all.deb Wesnoth_Data
debinstall wesnoth wesnoth_1.2.4-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Wesnoth
}


case "${choice}" in
	"Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers Edition" )
		warn
		#begin gamers script
		baseinstall
		gamers


	;;
	"Custom" )
		ans=$(zenity   --width=630 --height=460 --list  --text "Custom Installation" --checklist  --column "Install" --column "Program / Game" --column "Description" FALSE "Action_Cube" "ActionCube is a total conversion of Wouter van Oortmerssen´s FPS Cube." FALSE "Alien_Arena" "This game combines some of the very best aspects of such games as Quake III and Unreal Tournament" FALSE "Open_Arena" "Open-source content package for Quake III Arena." FALSE "Glest" "Glest is a 3D real-time strategy game." FALSE "Scourge" "S.C.O.U.R.G.E. is a rogue-like game in the fine tradition of NetHack and Moria It sports a graphical front-end, similar to glHack or the Falcon's eye." FALSE "Boswars" "Bos Wars is a futuristic real-time strategy game. It is possible to play against human opponents over LAN, internet, or against the computer." FALSE "Sauerbraten" "Singleplayer and multiplayer 12 game modes first person shooter, built as a major redesign of the Cube FPS." FALSE "Secret_Maryo_Chronicles" "Secret Maryo Chronicles is an Open Source two-dimensional platform game designed for Linux and Windows developed in C++. Smiliar to classic side-scrollers." FALSE "Vdrift" "A cross-platform, open source driving simulation made with drift racing in mind." FALSE "UFO_AI" "UFO:AI is a 3d turn based strategy game inspired by the X-Com series by Microprose." FALSE "Warsow" "Warsow, is a multiplayer first-person shooter computer and video game." FALSE "Mania_Drive" "A great game for driving fans. Good physics system, 3D engine, and fun are all integrated in this driving simulator." FALSE "Supertux" "SuperTux is a classic 2D jump and run arcade sidescroller game." FALSE "Astromenace" "Collect money and upgrade your spaceship to destroy your enemies." FALSE "Blob_and_conquer" "Blob and Conquer is a 3rd person action shooter." FALSE "Dark_Oberon" "Dark Oberon is an open source real-time strategy game similar to Warcraft II released under GPL." FALSE "Wesnoth" "Polished turn based strategy game featuring fantasy characters and modular plots."); 

#parse choices made
if echo "$ans" | grep "Alien_Arena" ; then
        Alien_Arena
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Action_Cube" ; then
        Action_Cube
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Open_Arena" ; then
        Open_Arena
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Glest" ; then
        Glest
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Scourge" ; then
        Scourge
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Boswars" ; then
        Boswars
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Sauerbraten" ; then
        Sauerbraten
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Secret_Maryo_Chronicles" ; then
        Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Vdrift" ; then
        Vdrift
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "UFO_AI" ; then
        UFO_AI
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Warsow" ; then
        Warsow
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Mania_Drive" ; then
        Mania_Drive
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Supertux" ; then
        Supertux
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Astromenace" ; then
        Astromenace
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Blob_and_conquer" ; then
        Blob_and_conquer
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Dark_Oberon" ; then
        Dark_Oberon
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Wesnoth" ; then
        Wesnoth
      fi
killall timidity
;;
esac

exit 0


```

It will install the following games:
```
Action_Cube
Alien_Arena
Open_Arena
Scourge
Glest
Boswars
Sauerbraten
Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
Vdrift
UFO_AI
Warsow
Mania_Drive
Supertux
Astromenace
Blob_and_conquer
Dark_Oberon
```

I will also attach the script for download.  This script is far from complete I will provide options for apps and more games.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-09
> **adewale said:**
> hmm i hear about this double view now tripple view what is about can you plz explain i've haven't seen one. what are the advantages of having double views or evn tripple. Thanks

I have 2 video cards (nvidia 7600GT's) running in [SLI]("http://www.techreport.com/reviews/2004q4/sli/index.x?pg=1") mode 16X PCI Express X2 the mainboard supports 32X in SLI mode each video card has 2 heads both are dvi . In essence I can hook up to 4 monitors up to it, I have 3 hooked up now (2 X 17" and a 22" widescreen in the center), I can drag a window from one to the other and onto the last 1.  This allows for much greater productivity, once you have tasted twinview it is really hard to go back to one monitor.  3 monitors so far to me seems to be overkill it will probably be that way until I get used to it.  Here is an example of truely [overkill]("http://www.9xmedia.com/pages-Build_a_system/X-Top---5over5.html").  [This]("http://www.cinemassivedisplays.com/Trio_21X.php") is about the same setup as I have.

If you would like to learn more about how they increase productivity please review [this]("http://chris.pirillo.com/2007/05/31/dual-monitor-display-increases-productivity/") article.

dd if=/dev/cd of=NAMEOFIMAGE.ISO

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-10
_BEFORE INSTALLATION LIVE CD_:

I'm burning another CD version of what will be Ubuntu Ultimate 1.31 or perhaps 1.4, it appears from testing prior to installation to be almost flawless.  Let's hope the end results are the same.  :)  697MB and no windows apps, before it is over with I'll fill the other 3MB ;)  As a live cd works almost perfect, however does not play music while it downloads currently but otherwise good to go testing installation and upgrade script.

_AFTER INSTALLATION_:

Still **NO MUSIC** why?  No timidity first screenshot, will be fixed prior to availability for download, the terminal will be gone for every installation except those that require a license agreement, I plan on wiping the terminal out completely before I am done.  I prefer the end user not to have to know cryptic commands in a terminal.  Screenshots are taken on a widescreen monitor; BTW the distro picked it up and used at max resolution off the rip no envy even needed even though it is built in for those that may need it, nor any intervention at all on my part.

From screenshot to screenshot the games list gets larger ;) apps list will be the same way as I continue to work upon it.  All will be end user selectable when finished.  You build your distro ;) really brings the light of day in calling it ULTIMATE!!!

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-14
> **adewale said:**
> This is my xorg

where would i fix this
Option         "NvAgp" "1"

```
Section "Device"
    Identifier     "NVIDIA Corporation NV11 [GeForce2 MX/MX 400]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option 	   "NvAgp" "1"
EndSection
```

I have modified if for you see attachment.  If you plan on using beryl (I have seen it work and quite well on a 400) please modify your screen section add the following line just as done above:
```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
```

with your xorg it should look like this if you want beryl:
```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "NVIDIA Corporation NV11 [GeForce2 MX/MX 400]"
    Monitor        "Generic Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
```

Let me know if you need anymore help.

the latest is 1.31 it is a [beta]("http://downloadubuntusoftware.info/ubuntu-ultimate-beta.iso") based on feisty expect problems as I work them out, I would like to let people know the beta CD version is going better then expected and may be finalized before being released (it will be 1.4).  It has the ability to be directly upgraded to the "Gamers Edition" for those that play games.  It also has a custom section allowing the end user to select what he/she may want installed (games / apps etc) the script is centrally located on one of my servers and will allow upgrade to 1.5 etc. when completed, instead of having to download a whole cd or dvd again.  Currently it allows installation of drivers for Nvidia as well as ATI based video cards via [envy]("http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html") which is integrated in the CD version in works.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-18
> **TS28 said:**
> So if I were to install the beta cd, I could keep that install with all my settings and stuff when I upgrade to 1.4 and so forth?

If so, intalling now!

I'm working on that now, you may want to wait for 1.4 to go final be it as a cd or as a dvd, both will have the same results. Once 1.4 goes final it is all handled by a script I posted for all to try.  As of today when the script is executed (an icon on the desktop that says upgrade) it checks the repo for a newer script if it is newer it will download it and replace it with the current version, inform the end user a newer version has been released and ask you to re-execute it.

For those concerned how I did it below is the code for version checking:

```

SCRIPT_VERSION=1.31
##Check for zenity

if [ ! -e "/usr/bin/zenity" ]; then
	gksudo apt-get install -y zenity
fi

update() {
 wget -O /tmp/gamers.sh http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
 REMOTE_VERSION=`grep SCRIPT_VERSION /tmp/gamers.sh |head -n1 |sed 's/.*=//'`
 if [ "$REMOTE_VERSION" != "$SCRIPT_VERSION" ]; then
  if [[ -n $DIALOG ]]
  then
   dialog --yesno "Newer version of Ubuntu Ultimate has been found\n\nDo you wish to install it?" 0 0
   DIALOG_EXIT_CODE=$?
   if [[ $DIALOG_EXIT_CODE = 0 ]]
   then
    cp /tmp/gamers.sh $0
    echo "Ubuntu Ultimate script has been updated to v $REMOTE_VERSION"
    echo "Please re-run the script"
    zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text='Newer version of script detected '$REMOTE_VERSION'.  It has been upgraded please re-run script.' --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition";
    exit
   fi
  else
   cp /tmp/gamers.sh $0
   echo "Newer version detected: $REMOTE_VERSION"
   zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text='Newer version of script detected '$REMOTE_VERSION'.  It has been upgraded please re-run script.' --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition";
   exit
  fi
 fi
 rm /tmp/gamers.sh 2>/dev/null
}


```

### Post by Slicer101 on 2007-06-19
tovarichtch

This would indicate that it is not really installed. 

rc gaim 2.0.0+beta6-1ubuntu4 multi-protocol instant messaging client

What I would do is check AutoMatix to make sure that it is not listed as an installed App. If it is, uninstall it via AutoMatix as well. 

You can then use dpkg --purge gaim to clean things up a bit. 

Then when you do a list command again you should see it listed as: 

pn  gaim           <none>         (no description available)


Hope this helps, 

Slicer

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-19
> **Slicer101 said:**
> tovarichtch

This would indicate that it is not really installed. 

rc gaim 2.0.0+beta6-1ubuntu4 multi-protocol instant messaging client

What I would do is check AutoMatix to make sure that it is not listed as an installed App. If it is, uninstall it via AutoMatix as well. 

You can then use dpkg --purge gaim to clean things up a bit. 

Then when you do a list command again you should see it listed as: 

pn  gaim           <none>         (no description available)


Hope this helps, 

Slicer

Slicer I greatly appreciate you trying to help others, especially in an area I don't have time to do.  Gaim was installed in Ultimate using a script I wrote for reconstructor below is the code:

```
#!/bin/sh 
#
# Reconstructor Module - Gaim Beta Installer
#	Copyright (c) 2006  Reconstructor Team <http://reconstructor.aperantis.com>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

RMOD_ENGINE=1.0
RMOD_CATEGORY='Software'
RMOD_SUBCATEGORY='Networking'
RMOD_NAME='Gaim Beta 6 Build'
RMOD_AUTHOR='TheeMahn'
RMOD_VERSION=0.9
RMOD_DESCRIPTION='Builds and installs Gaim beta 7 & Plugins'
RMOD_RUN_IN_CHROOT=True
RMOD_UPDATE_URL='http://reconstructor.aperantis.com/update/modules/'
echo Running $RMOD_NAME...

#grab and install build tools and prerequisites
apt-get install -y build-essential subversion gcc autoconf libtool automake1.4 automake1.9 libxcomposite-dev libxdamage-dev libstartup-notification0-dev libwnck-dev x11proto-gl-dev librsvg2-dev intltool libgnutls-dev 

#Grab extract and build
wget http://easynews.dl.sourceforge.net/sourceforge/pidgin/pidgin-2.0.0beta7.tar.bz2
tar xvf pidgin-2.0.0beta7.tar.bz2
cd pidgin-2.0.0beta7
./configure --enable-ssl
make
make install

# clean cache
cd ..
rm -R pidgin*
apt-get clean
apt-get autoclean
echo $RMOD_NAME Finished...
```


This is not the exact module used in 1.3.  I have re-written it for pidgin but the jest is there. when an app is installed using a package manager such as Synaptic it is added to their database and can be removed likewise.  When an app is downloaded as source and compiled into the OS... I think in here lies the problem and the reason he is having problems upgrading (no longer exists) or removing (not in the database).  I have solved this from ever happening again by "The Script", being built now.  The script currently downloads nothing but debs and installs them using dpkg.  I do have plans for those that would like to try "cutting edge" beryl for example all driven by the script, this is a ways down the road stability is my first priority.

This is just for 1 program can you imagine how much time I have spent writing a module for each app included in 1.3?  Irregardless of the situation I do appreciate you trying to help others, and so a **KUDOS to Slicer**, I feel the same way about ccal and snapel for helping the linux community.  When I get time after 1.4 finalizes I will install 1.3 and find a way to purge it from the system.  Thanks for your help.

I just thought I'd edit and add that I'm testing what I hope to be my last build of the cd version of Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4.  :)

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-26
> **philbo said:**
> Sorry Mate, I must be missing something.

First the was no /usr/share/ultimate/ folder.

Second I don't think I was clear, I am currently running Ultimate gamers 1.3 (ubuntu Edgy 6.10), I have downloaded the Ulitmate 1.4 (Ubuntu fiesty 7.04) DVD and would like to upgrade the installed ultimate 1.3 to 1.4.

I'm a bit of a linux Newb so assume the worst when relaying instructions!!!

When I try to run the script that you suggested, it stated that I required feisty to be installed first, is it possible to upgrade using the dvd rather than across the net via synaptics?

Thanks in anticipation

Philbo

Don't quote me on this but I do not think that is possible 1.3 is a edgy based distro 1.4 is feisty based.  If you would like to attempt a upgrade first add 1.4's repo to your sources.list.  By DVD is probably not possible but by the net the following should work.

```
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

remove edgy based repos and add:

```
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty main
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security main restricted
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security main restricted
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty restricted
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty universe
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security universe
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security universe
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty multiverse
deb http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty-updates main restricted
deb http://repoubuntusoftware.info/ feisty all
```

save and close.

If you would like to be the first 1 to try a upgrade to 1.4 open a terminal and enter the following...

```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade
sudo apt-get dist-upgrade && sudo apt-get -f install && sudo dpkg --configure -a
```

After and **IF** it finishes graciously reboot and perform the following in a terminal...

```
mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
rm -R *
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
chmod +x gamers.sh
mkdir /etc/skel/Desktop
apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy
#create Shortcut
echo "Creating Upgrade shortcut..."
# create desktop shortcut for Upgrade
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

I would not attempt any of the above unless you are willing to accept a possible failure (this could cause a re-install or clean install of 1.4 however you want to view it).  **So please use at your own risk.**  I would not do so on a production machine.

I hope I have been of some help.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-06-27
> **ice60 said:**
> it looks good, i like more minimal stuff though, but after looking through the added apps you might be interested with a qemu frontend. i have it on another computer, but i think this is the home page -
[http://qemulator.createweb.de/](http://qemulator.createweb.de/)
[http://qemulator.createweb.de/index.php4?mengroup=main&haupt=Screenshots&typ=richdoc&doccount=214](http://qemulator.createweb.de/index.php4?mengroup=main&haupt=Screenshots&typ=richdoc&doccount=214)

I have seen many qemu front ends, none of which I see that take care of business as I see it and add Qemu acceleration, I will integrate this into your OS, please be patient as I write the script.  By all means I encourage someone else to write it.  I am 1 mahn most companies which I am not, have plenty of time on their hands.  I have written a rmod that does just that, I build for the people.  Please excuse me when I say I have been down that road and I present you the code:

```
#!/bin/sh
#
# Reconstructor Module - QEMU & KQEMU Accelerator
#	Copyright (c) 2006  Reconstructor Team <http://reconstructor.aperantis.com>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

RMOD_ENGINE=1.0
RMOD_CATEGORY='Software'
RMOD_SUBCATEGORY='Virtualization'
RMOD_NAME='QEMU & KQEMU Accelerator'
RMOD_AUTHOR='TheeMahn'
RMOD_VERSION=0.4
RMOD_DESCRIPTION='Installs QEMU & KQEMU Accelerator'
RMOD_RUN_IN_CHROOT=True
RMOD_UPDATE_URL='http://reconstructor.aperantis.com/update/modules/'
echo Running $RMOD_NAME...
#apt-get update
apt-get install -y wget
wget http://fabrice.bellard.free.fr/qemu/qemu-0.9.0.tar.gz
tar xvf qemu-0.9.0.tar.gz
wget http://fabrice.bellard.free.fr/qemu/kqemu-1.3.0pre11.tar.gz
tar xvf kqemu-1.3.0pre11.tar.gz
cd qemu-0.9.0
./configure
cd kqemu-1.3.0pre9
./configure
make
./install.sh

# All good mount it up

modprobe kqemu

echo Installing kqemu accelerator module ...
# Load kqemu module on each bootup so that you don’t have to manually load the module
# every time you start qemu:

#load the kqemu kernel module
echo /sbin/modprobe kqemu >> miscboot.sh 

#create thekqemu device

echo mknod /dev/kqemu c 250 0 >> miscboot.sh 

#grant rw permissions to all users

echo chmod 666 /dev/kqemu >> miscboot.sh

# Install to init.d

install -c -m 0755 miscboot.sh /etc/init.d/miscboot.sh

echo Cleaning up ...
# clean up

rm miscboot.sh
cd .. 
rm -R kqemu-1.3.0pre9
rm kqemu-1.3.0pre9.tar.gz
# clean cache
apt-get clean
apt-get autoclean
echo $RMOD_NAME Finished...
```

The same can be executed as a bash script.  We can argue till we are blue in the face but results are the same. Probably does not work anymore, I have focused my attention in developing an OS that suits the people and when I say the people I mean broad based.  I do take in consideration what you have said and is within something I should add with in my main script.  A GUI based QEMU I could write for you, but why "re-create the wheel", a theory I live by.  I have written many a program for windows utilizing the API, which is BTW a joke.

I will try to integrate software as far as qemu is concerned, however not top on my list.  I wish you could see what I do.

### Post by joe.turion64x2 on 2007-06-27
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> I would like to post to let all know, I have not died and Ubuntu Ultimate is still in the works as a matter of fact I'm here to post that a new "beta" will soon be available for download, uploading now.  This post will change to keep people informed of current works in progress.  If you would like to learn more about the beta distro please view this [page]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ultimate_Beta/").   Details etc. as it is finalized will be updated here.  The distro is based on Feisty Fawn.

It is now uploaded, enjoy.

For those that don't know what Ubuntu Ultimate is you can learn about the distro from my [website]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/ultimate/").

I will be editing this post to keep people informed:

**06/26/07** Great news... Ubuntu Software now has a dedicated webmaster, something I don't have time for.  Look for pages to start appearing that were "under construction" soon & those not finished to be completed.  Everyone give a big thanks to [rakansen]("http://ubuntuforums.org/member.php?u=284994").  We are also looking for a wikipedia writer for Ubuntu Ultimate, I will provide most of the information.  If you can help please post or pm me.

**06/25/07** A huge stumbling block has been hit in trying to remove gaim and compiz from the live cd (was going to replace them with pidgin and beryl) due to dependencies with the ubuntu-desktop metapackage, I have so far failed.  I am working on resolving this issue.  There is no bug report listed as it is not seen as a bug unless you are building it as a live cd.  Once built it will ask for a login and password from the live cd (should not do this, should auto login).  This issue can be seen by attempting to remove gaim it will also remove ubuntu-desktop which is an essential package for the live cd.  Adding it back after removal will also install gaim.

**06/18/07** The [script]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh") is now good enough to start building for release.  When executed it will compare its version to the version on the server if the one on the server is newer it will download it overwriting it with the new one and inform the end user to re-start the upgrade process.  Works like a charm ;)  Currently set to version 1.31 at time of posting.

**06/10/07** just a bit of an [update]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2819407&postcount=52") on the progress of the CD version.

**06/09/07** I have updated the script and am now using what will be the CD version please be forewarned there are bugs in the script, that will be ironed out well prior to any release be it as a beta or as final, I will post progress.

**06/06/07** For those that are currently running it (Ultimate 1.31 beta installed) please try this [script]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh") it is work in progress, I have purchased a separate hosting account, domain & the packages to take the load off the main server as it is being tested.  The script will constantly be changing as I work on it.

I would like to apologize if I do not immediately get back with you, I hope you can understand I am a very busy Mahn :), I do try and respond to each and every post.

I would like to post the source for all to view of the current script in works:

```
#!/bin/bash
# Ubuntu Ultimate Edition Easy upgrade script Via TheeMahn
#
#
# Copyright (c) 2007  Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
SCRIPT_VERSION=1.32
##Check for zenity

if [ ! -e "/usr/bin/zenity" ]; then
	gksudo apt-get install -y zenity
fi

#Music?
zenity --question --title "Ubuntu Ultimate Edition" --text "Would you like to listen to music as Ubuntu Ultimate downloads and installs software for you?  Not exactly killer music but free, OK to listen, cancel to install without.";
if [ "$?" = "0" ]; then
		sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes --allow-unauthenticated timidity | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Timidity Music Player..."
TIMIDITY="1";
fi


update() {
 wget -O /tmp/gamers.sh http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
 REMOTE_VERSION=`grep SCRIPT_VERSION /tmp/gamers.sh |head -n1 |sed 's/.*=//'`
 if [ "$REMOTE_VERSION" != "$SCRIPT_VERSION" ]; then
  if [[ -n $DIALOG ]]
  then
   dialog --yesno "Newer version of Ubuntu Ultimate has been found\n\nDo you wish to install it?" 0 0
   DIALOG_EXIT_CODE=$?
   if [[ $DIALOG_EXIT_CODE = 0 ]]
   then
    cp /tmp/gamers.sh $0
    echo "Ubuntu Ultimate script has been updated to v $REMOTE_VERSION"
    echo "Please re-run the script"
    zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text='Newer version of script detected '$REMOTE_VERSION'.  It has been upgraded please re-run script.' --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition";
    exit
   fi
  else
   cp /tmp/gamers.sh $0
   echo "Newer version detected: $REMOTE_VERSION"
   zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text='Newer version of script detected '$REMOTE_VERSION'.  It has been upgraded please re-run script.' --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition";
   exit
  fi
 fi
 rm /tmp/gamers.sh 2>/dev/null
}




#is it running off the Live CD?
if [ $USERNAME = "ubuntu" ]; then
    `zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text="This upgrade scipt can only be ran after hard disk installation." --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition"`;
    exit 0
fi

# Are we running on Feisty?
if ( ! grep "Ubuntu 7.04" /etc/issue >/dev/null 2>&1); then
     `zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text="This scipt can only be ran on Feisty based distros." --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition"`;
    exit 0
fi

#Check for update to script here
update

if [ $TIMIDITY = "1" ]; then
timidity -Os -A130 -p3 -Ei5 -module=2 -q20 -T140 /usr/share/ultimate/halloween_theme.s3m&choice="$(zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png  --width=400 --height=220 --title "Ubuntu Ultimate Edition" --text "Choose distro path" --list --radiolist --column "Install" --column "Distro" true 'Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers Edition' false 'Ubuntu Ultimate Edition' false 'Custom')"
else
choice="$(zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png  --width=400 --height=220 --title "Ubuntu Ultimate Edition" --text "Choose distro path" --list --radiolist --column "Install" --column "Distro" true 'Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers Edition' false 'Ubuntu Ultimate Edition' false 'Custom')";
fi


warn() {
	if ! zenity --question --title "Distro selected" --text "${choice}
	Do you wish to grab the default packages for the above distro?"; then
		exit 0
	fi
}

baseinstall() {
gksudo apt-get update | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Updating source list"
sudo apt-get upgrade -y --allow-unauthenticated | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Upgrading base distro..."
}

debinstall() {
INSTALLED=$(apt-cache policy $1 | grep '^ *Installed: ' | sed -e 's/ *Installed: *//')
echo "INSTALLED = \"$INSTALLED\""
if [ $INSTALLED = "(none)" ]; then
echo "installing $1"
#download it
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/$2 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading $3..."
#install it
sudo dpkg -i $2 2>&1 | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing $3..."
sudo rm $2
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabing $3 Dependancies"
else
echo "$3 Already installed"
fi
}

webinstall() {
INSTALLED=$(apt-cache policy $1 | grep '^ *Installed: ' | sed -e 's/ *Installed: *//')
echo "INSTALLED = \"$INSTALLED\""
if [ $INSTALLED = "(none)" ]; then
echo "installing $4"
#download it
wget -c $2 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading $3..."
#install it
sudo dpkg -i $3 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing $4..."
sudo rm $3
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabing $4 Dependancies"
else
echo "$3 Already installed"
fi
}

terminstall() {
INSTALLED=$(apt-cache policy $1 | grep '^ *Installed: ' | sed -e 's/ *Installed: *//')
echo "INSTALLED = \"$INSTALLED\""
if [ $INSTALLED = "(none)" ]; then
echo "installing $1"
#download it
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/$2 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=400 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading $3..."
#install it
sudo xterm -e gdebi $2 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing $3..."
sudo rm $2
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabbing $3 dependencies"
else
echo "$3 Already installed"
fi
}

prog() {
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes --allow-unauthenticated $1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing $2..."
echo $2 " installed."
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabbing $2 dependencies"
}

Finish_upgrade() {
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install hal linux-headers-generic linux-image-generic linux-restricted-modules-generic | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Finalizing distro"

Inform() {
zenity --window-icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png --info --text="It is highly recomended you restart your computer after installing video drivers." --title="Ubuntu Ultimate Edition"; }
if [ $? ! 0 ]; then
exit 0
else
envy -g
fi
}

Ultimate() {
Adobe_Acroread
GFTP
KSnapshot
K3b
IM_Clients
Music_Players
Web_Editors
Codecs
DVD_RIP_ENCODE
Kvirc
Archiving_Tools
NTFS
Themes
P2P_Tools
Beryl
SUM_BUM
System_Info
Picasa
EasyTag
Video_Players
}

gamers() {
Action_Cube
Alien_Arena
Open_Arena
Scourge
Glest
Boswars
Sauerbraten
Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
Vdrift
UFO_AI
Warsow
Mania_Drive
Supertux
Astromenace
Blob_and_conquer
Dark_Oberon
Wesnoth
Advanced_Armagetron
Amoebax
Nexuiz
Enemy_Territory
Tremulous
Americas_Army
BZFlag
Chess_Games
killall timidity
}

##################### GAMEZ ##################
Action_Cube() {
#actioncube
debinstall actioncube actioncube_0.92-1getdeb1_i386.deb Action_cube
}

Alien_Arena() {
#alien Arena
debinstall alienarena2007 alienarena2007_6.04-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Alien_Arena
}

Amoebax() {
debinstall amoebax amoebax_0.1.1-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Amoebax
}

Open_Arena() {
#open arena
debinstall openarena openarena_0.6.0-1getdeb1_i386.deb Open_Arena
}

Glest() {
#glest
debinstall glest-data glest-data_2.0.0-0~getdeb1_all.deb Glest_Data
debinstall glest glest_2.0.0-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Glest
}

Advanced_Armagetron() {
debinstall armagetron armagetronad_0.2.8.2.1-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Advanced_Armagetron
}

Scourge() {
debinstall scourge scourge_0.18-1~getdeb1_i386.deb Scourge
debinstall scourge-data scourge-data_0.18-1~getdeb1_all.deb Scourge_Data
}

Boswars() {
debinstall boswars boswars-data_2.3-0~getdeb1_all.deb Boswars
debinstall boswars-data boswars_2.3-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Boswars_Data
}

Sauerbraten() {
debinstall sauerbraten-data sauerbraten-data_0.0.20070415-0~getdeb1_all.deb Sauerbraten_Data
debinstall sauerbraten sauerbraten_0.0.20070415-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Sauerbraten
}

Secret_Maryo_Chronicles() {
debinstall smc-data smc-data_0.99.6-0~getdeb1_all.deb Secret_Maryo_Chronicles_Data
debinstall smc smc_0.99.6-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
}

Vdrift() {
debinstall vdrift-full vdrift-full_0.0.2007.03.23-0~getdeb1_all.deb Vdrift_Data
debinstall vdrift vdrift_0.0.2007.03.23-0~getdeb1_i386.deb Vdrift
}

UFO_AI(){
debinstall ufoai ufoai-data_2.1-0~getdeb1_all.deb UFO_AI
}

Warsow() {
debinstall warsow-data warsow-data_0.3-1~getdeb1_all.deb Warsow_Data
debinstall warsow warsow_0.3-1~getdeb1_i386.deb Warsow
}

Mania_Drive() {
debinstall maniadrive-data maniadrive-data_1.2-0~getdeb1_all.deb Maniadrive_Data

}

Enemy_Territory () {
if test -f et-linux-2.60.x86.run
then
sudo sh ./et-linux-2.60.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Enemy Territory..."
else
wget -c http://ftp.games.skynet.be/pub/wolfenstein/et-linux-2.60.x86.run 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading Enemy Territory..."
sudo chmod +x et-linux-2.60.x86.run
sudo sh ./et-linux-2.60.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Enemy Territory..."
fi
#update
if test -f et-linux-2.60-update.x86.run
then
sudo sh ./et-linux-2.60-update.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Enemy Territory Update..."
else
wget -c http://ftp.games.skynet.be/pub/wolfenstein/et-linux-2.60-update.x86.run 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading Enemy Territory Update..."
sudo chmod +x et-linux-2.60-update.x86.run
sudo sh ./et-linux-2.60-update.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Enemy Territory Update..."
fi
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabbing Enemy Territory dependencies"
}

Tremulous () {
if test -f tremulous-1.1.0-installer.x86.run
then
sudo sh ./tremulous-1.1.0-installer.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Tremulous..."
else
wget -c http://prdownloads.sourceforge.net/tremulous/tremulous-1.1.0-installer.x86.run?download 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading Tremulous..."
sudo chmod +x tremulous-1.1.0-installer.x86.run
sudo sh ./tremulous-1.1.0-installer.x86.run 2>&1 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Tremulous..."
fi
sudo apt-get -f --force-yes -y install | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Grabbing Tremulous dependencies"
}

Archiving_Tools() {

NTFS() {
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes --allow-unauthenticated ntfs-3g libfuse2 fuse-utils ntfstools ntfs-config | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing NTFS Support..."
echo fuse | sudo tee -a /etc/modules
sudo addgroup ntfs
modprobe fuse
}

Themes() {
sudo apt-get install -y gnome-art blubuntu-look comixcursors crystalcursors edgy-gdm-themes gcursor gdm-themes metacity-themes peace-look tropic-look artwiz-cursor gdm-themes gnome-themes-extras industrial-cursor-theme industrialtango-theme peace-look metacity-themes gnome-icon-theme-* | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Extra Themes..."
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated ubuntu-artwork outdoors-theme gtk-theme-switch gtk-theme-switch gtk-clearlooks-* enlightenment-theme-* gtk-engines-* gtk2-engines-* | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Theme Engines..."
sudo apt-get remove -y --purge gtk-engines-eazel | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Purging Bad Engine..."
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated ttf-bitstream-vera ttf-freefont ttf-arphic-bkai00mp gsfonts-x11 ttf-devanagari-fonts ttf-farsiweb ttf-sjfonts ttf-punjabi-fonts ttf-indic-fonts ttf-kochi-gothic-naga10 ttf-thai-tlwg ttf-kochi-mincho ttf-bangla-fonts ttf-bengali-fonts ttf-mgopen ttf-nafees ttf-kannada-fonts ttf-xfree86-nonfree-syriac ttf-unfonts ttf-bpg-georgian-fonts ttf-uralic t1-xfree86-nonfree ttf-fossfonts ttf-f500 ttf-isabella ttf-larabie-deco ttf-larabie-straight ttf-larabie-uncommon ttf-staypuft ttf-summersby ttf-ubuntu-title xfonts-artwiz xfonts-intl-european ttf-dustin | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Extra Fonts..."
}

Nautilus_Scripts() {
cd ~/.gnome2/nautilus-scripts/
wget -c http://ubuntusoftware.info/scripts/Nautilus_scripts.tar.gz 2>&1 | sed -u 's/.*\ \([0-9]\+%\)\ \+\([0-9.]\+\ [KMB\/s]\+\)$/\1\n# Downloading \2/' | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --auto-close --title="Downloading Nautilus Scripts..."
tar xvf Nautilus_scripts.tar.gz
rm Nautilus_scripts.tar.gz
sudo chmod +x *
##Grab scripts
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated nautilus-script-* nautilus-open-terminal lame

#enable them
nautilus-script-manager enable ConvertAudioFile
nautilus-script-manager enable Subversion
cd ~
}

Screensavers(){
sudo apt-get install -y  --force-yes --allow-unauthenticated brightside kdescreensaver-aasaver kscreensaver kscreensaver-xsave* ktux xscreensaver xscreensaver-gl-extra screensave* kcometen3 | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Extra Screensavers..."
}

Build_Tools(){
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes build-essential subversion gcc autoconf libtool automake1.4 automake1.9 libxcomposite-dev libxdamage-dev libstartup-notification0-dev libwnck-dev x11proto-gl-dev fakeroot debhelper librsvg2-dev intltool libmetacity-dev libgnome-window-settings-dev libgnomeui-dev libgnome-desktop-dev | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Subversion & Build tools..."
}

Emulation() {
prog wine Wine
prog qemu Qemu
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes --allow-unauthenticated wget build-essential gcc-3.4 libsdl1.2-dev checkinstall zlib1g-dev linux-headers-$(uname -r) | zenity --width=600 --height=100 --progress --pulsate --auto-close --title "Installing Qemu dependencies..."
}

##Process choice
case "${choice}" in
	"Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers Edition" )
		warn
		#begin gamers script
		baseinstall
		gamers
		Finish_upgrade
		Inform

	;;
	"Ubuntu Ultimate Edition" )
		warn
		baseinstall
		Ultimate
		Finish_upgrade
		Inform
	;;

	"Custom" )
		ans=$(zenity   --width=630 --height=460 --list  --text "Custom Installation" --checklist  --column "Install" --column "Program / Game" --column "Description" FALSE "Action_Cube" "ActionCube is a total conversion of Wouter van Oortmerssen´s FPS Cube." FALSE "Advanced_Armagetron" "Multiplayer game in 3d that attempts to emulate and expand on the lightcycle sequence from the movie Tron. It's an old school arcade game slung into the 21st century." FALSE "Americas_Army" "America's Army, the Official US Army Game (AA), provides young Americans with a virtual web-based environment in which they can explore Army career.  PLEASE NOTE THIS GAME IS HUGE Could take hours to download." FALSE "Alien_Arena" "This game combines some of the very best aspects of such games as Quake III and Unreal Tournament" FALSE "Amoebax" "Amoebax is a free Puyo Puyo-style puzzle game for up to two players." FALSE "BZFlag" "Battle Zone capture the Flag is a three dimensional first person tank shooting game." FALSE "Open_Arena" "Open-source content package for Quake III Arena." FALSE "Chess_Games" "Installs various Chess and board games." FALSE "Enemy_Territory" "Wolfenstein: Enemy Territory is an online multiplayer game, where the players interact with each other over a network in two teams (axis and allies) to defend or destroy mission objectives." FALSE "Glest" "Glest is a 3D real-time strategy game." FALSE "Scourge" "S.C.O.U.R.G.E. is a rogue-like game in the fine tradition of NetHack and Moria It sports a graphical front-end, similar to glHack or the Falcon's eye." FALSE "Boswars" "Bos Wars is a futuristic real-time strategy game. It is possible to play against human opponents over LAN, internet, or against the computer." FALSE "Sauerbraten" "Singleplayer and multiplayer 12 game modes first person shooter, built as a major redesign of the Cube FPS." FALSE "Tremulous" "Tremulous is a stand-alone game based on the ioquake3 source which draws inspiration from the popular Quake2 mod Gloom." FALSE "Secret_Maryo_Chronicles" "Secret Maryo Chronicles is an Open Source two-dimensional platform game designed for Linux and Windows developed in C++. Smiliar to classic side-scrollers." FALSE "Vdrift" "A cross-platform, open source driving simulation made with drift racing in mind." FALSE "UFO_AI" "UFO:AI is a 3d turn based strategy game inspired by the X-Com series by Microprose." FALSE "Warsow" "Warsow, is a multiplayer first-person shooter computer and video game." FALSE "Mania_Drive" "A great game for driving fans. Good physics system, 3D engine, and fun are all integrated in this driving simulator." FALSE "Supertux" "SuperTux is a classic 2D jump and run arcade sidescroller game." FALSE "Astromenace" "Collect money and upgrade your spaceship to destroy your enemies." FALSE "Blob_and_conquer" "Blob and Conquer is a 3rd person action shooter." FALSE "Dark_Oberon" "Dark Oberon is an open source real-time strategy game similar to Warcraft II released under GPL." FALSE "Wesnoth" "Polished turn based strategy game featuring fantasy characters and modular plots." FALSE "Warzone_2100" "Warzone 2100 is a real-time strategy game, comparable to Earth 2150." FALSE "The_Goonies" "Adventure game in which teams compete by solving puzzles." TRUE "Adobe_Acroread" "Adobe PDF files Reader." TRUE "Music_Players" "Amarok, audacious and songbird music players and plugins.." TRUE "Web_Editors" "Installs a large suite of web editors."  TRUE "Codecs" "Installs commonly used codecs." TRUE "Burning" "K3b and Gnomebaker are a comfortable CD/DVD writing suite for Linux/KDE." TRUE "GFTP" "gFTP is a free multithreaded ftp client for *NIX based machines running X11R6 or later." TRUE "IM_Clients" "Multi-protocol Instant Messaging clients that allows you to use all of your IM accounts at once." TRUE "KSnapshot" "KSnapshot is a simple applet for taking screenshots." TRUE "DVD_RIP_ENCODE" "Dvd ripping and encoding software." TRUE "Kvirc" "Internet relay chat program." TRUE "Archiving_Tools" "Installs virtually all archive handlers." TRUE "NTFS" "Installs support for reading / writing to NTFS partitions." TRUE "Themes" "Installs extra themes and cursors." TRUE "P2P_Tools" "Installs several Peer to peer tools." TRUE "Beryl" "Beryl is an OpenGL accelerated desktop that seeks to provide a free, open source desktop experience to the community that reflects the wishes of the users." TRUE "SUM_BUM" "Start-up and Bootup manager, allows customization and control of services." TRUE "System_Info" "Various system information tools." TRUE "Picasa" "Installs Google Picasa photo re-touching software." TRUE "EasyTag" "EasyTAG is an utility for viewing, editing and writing tags of your MP3, MP2, FLAC, Ogg Vorbis, MusePack and Monkey's Audio files." TRUE "Video_Players" "VLC Player and Mplayer movie players" FALSE "IPOD_Software" "Software for conectivity and handling IPODS." TRUE "Google_Earth" "Provides directions, interactive maps, and satellite/aerial imagery of the United States." FALSE "Capture_Card_Software" "Installs Prograps for watching TV on your PC for capture cards." TRUE "GDesklets" "Pretty System Info tools rss etc. and plugins." FALSE "Financial" "Installs GNU cash similar to MS Money." TRUE "File_Sharing" "Installs support for File sharing across a network." TRUE "Nautilus_Scripts" "Various Nautilus scripts that help in productivity." TRUE "Screensavers" "Installs Extra Screensavers." FALSE "Build_Tools" "Installs subversion and tools necessary to build your software." TRUE "Emulation" "Installs Qemu and wine Emulators."); 

#Base Installation
baseinstall

#parse choices if an app
if echo "$ans" | grep "Adobe_Acroread" ; then
        Adobe_Acroread
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Emulation" ; then
        Emulation
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "GDesklets" ; then
        GDesklets
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Build_Tools" ; then
        Build_Tools
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Screensavers" ; then
        Screensavers
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "File_Sharing" ; then
        File_Sharing
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Nautilus_Scripts" ; then
        Nautilus_Scripts
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Financial" ; then
        Financial
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Capture_Card_Software" ; then
        Capture_Card_Software
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "IPOD_Software" ; then
        IPOD_Software
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Google_Earth" ; then
        Google_Earth
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Music_Players" ; then
        Music_Players
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Video_Players" ; then
        Video_Players
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "EasyTag" ; then
        EasyTag
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "GFTP" ; then
        GFTP
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Burning" ; then
        Burning
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Web_Editors" ; then
        Web_Editors
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Codecs" ; then
        Codecs
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "IM_Clients" ; then
        IM_Clients
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Picasa" ; then
        Picasa
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "KSnapshot" ; then
        KSnapshot
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "System_Info" ; then
        System_Info
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "DVD_RIP_ENCODE" ; then
        DVD_RIP_ENCODE
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Kvirc" ; then
        Kvirc
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Archiving_Tools" ; then
        Archiving_Tools
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "NTFS" ; then
        NTFS
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Themes" ; then
        Themes
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "P2P_Tools" ; then
        P2P_Tools
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Beryl" ; then
        Beryl
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "SUM_BUM" ; then
        SUM_BUM
      fi


#parse choices made if a game
if echo "$ans" | grep "Alien_Arena" ; then
        Alien_Arena
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Amoebax" ; then
        Amoebax
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Action_Cube" ; then
        Action_Cube
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "BZFlag" ; then
        BZFlag
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Chess_Games" ; then
        Chess_Games
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Americas_Army" ; then
        Americas_Army
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Advanced_Armagetron" ; then
	Advanced_Armagetron
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Enemy_Territory" ; then
        Enemy_Territory
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Open_Arena" ; then
        Open_Arena
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Glest" ; then
        Glest
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Scourge" ; then
        Scourge
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Tremulous" ; then
        Tremulous
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Boswars" ; then
        Boswars
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Sauerbraten" ; then
        Sauerbraten
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Secret_Maryo_Chronicles" ; then
        Secret_Maryo_Chronicles
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Vdrift" ; then
        Vdrift
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "UFO_AI" ; then
        UFO_AI
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Warsow" ; then
        Warsow
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Mania_Drive" ; then
        Mania_Drive
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Supertux" ; then
        Supertux
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Astromenace" ; then
        Astromenace
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Blob_and_conquer" ; then
        Blob_and_conquer
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Dark_Oberon" ; then
        Dark_Oberon
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Wesnoth" ; then
        Wesnoth
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "Warzone_2100" ; then
        Warzone_2100
      fi
if echo "$ans" | grep "The_Goonies" ; then
        The_Goonies
      fi
#Inform the user

;;
esac

killall timidity
exit 0



```

I wait for the final release!!

I would like to thank you personally, it feels great to know my work has not gone unnoticed.  This I find funny my [profile]("http://ubuntuforums.org/member.php?u=74770") has pagerank ;)  My site is powerful at returning the favor, a 3 but no other site I know of points to that page.  My page rank is about to go up to 6, internal pages have nothing less then a 4, unless new.  Pretty impressive to me, I have had sub 10,000 traffic rank.  Think about this for a few, there are billions of websites and of all those billions I have achieved at peak above top 10,000 in the world (million+ hits in 2 days).  Done by 1 Mahn ;)

I understand this has nothing to do with my work other then the website, but gives me chills.  I provide information on how to do it yourself, welcome aboard [linux mint]("http://linuxmint.com/").  I am all about education, If I have time.  Time is not a commodity I have.  I wish I could help everyone in the world.

My Ultimate goal is to make NIX easier then windows, even as much as I love the terminal I am going to kill it, or so make it optional.  Even though this feature has not been implemented as of yet an example of it can be seen [here]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/ubuntu_games/RTCW_Enemy_Territory.html").  Notice the script installer, eventually will be MIME type; one click and done.  Will write and integrate it into the OS, the extention will change but results yielded will be the same.  I understand this has been done before "Click & run" software, however they charge for this feature, I have no intention of charging for anything I offer.  

I do however intend to offer a donation page and a sponsor section.  This money is used to help absorb the costs of bandwidth, BTW I have seen 1 1/2 TB, yes thats TB not MB ;) smoked in a day, and becomes quite expensive.  That was peak and boils down to $1,500 a day, imagine that, thanks to ccal, torrent and others, I would like to say at this point welcome aboard our new African mirror.  These people are truly responsible for you being able to obtain it to begin with.  My costs have gone down due to this.  The purchasing of other sites download and repo has trippled my cost overall, when download gets close to exceeding the 2TB limit I cancel and pay for a new one, 2TB for $15 is much better then $1.49 per gig, if I catch it in time.

This was probably information overload, I know you didn't ask about the other info, I just wanted it off my chest.  Thanks again.

Best regards,

TheeMahn
