Actually 5 websites one not posted to the public.  Will let all know I have updated the script.  Overkill in linux is called an added feature there is no such thing as bloat as there is in the windows world, more you add the slower it gets in windows, has no effect on *NIX.  So it should not matter if there is 6 music players etc. unless of course space is limited.  In linux no matter how many programs you install does not slow down overall performance, minus services of course.
[URL="http://ubuntusoftware.info/bum.html"]
BUM[/URL] is included in any release to allow removal of unnecessary services, what may be necessary to one may not to be to you, please review carefully before removing any service.  I have been working hard to allow users the ability to allow **you**, yes you and everyone else to build your own distro, selecting only what you would like with descriptions of what those apps / games are; welcome aboard the CD version running it now, currently spits me errors and fixes them at the end, however can not have that as some may click cancel & bork their system and blame me ;)

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-03
> **xguy2gk said:**
> i had went to the beryl website and got the repo for feisty from there, i installed it and ran it it...then the window borders disappeared. and i didn't have that problem before.
but anyway when 1.4 comes out i'll re-format and start fresh with compiz-fusion,thanks

Brother here is your answer... open a terminal  applications>accessories>terminal
```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

goto the "screen section and add the following:

```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals"  "True"
```

It should look similar to this:
```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals"  "True"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7600 GT]"
    Monitor        "CMC 22 W"
    DefaultDepth    24
```

Make sure default depth is 24

save and exit press ctrl-alt-backspace to restart xserver Beryl should work fine;)  Enjoy!!!

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-03
> **Scruffynerf said:**
> Um, what? Sorry TheeMahn, but I've not used you repo's. At least, not after a week or so. Ages ago they errored when re-building my sources.list, and I removed them. I tend to run off Internode's repos, which are my local ISP's mirrors of the official repo's. (My ISP does this for pretty much all the linux distro's - but only Internode customers can access them.)

I was suggesting Fusion to the other fellow, based on what I read about it on various fora, and what I thought was an educated guess as to what the problem may have been.

cheers

Scruffy

Speaking of [Compiz Fusion]("http://video.google.com/videoplay?docid=1405505248277861212&q=compiz+fusion&total=53&start=0&num=10&so=0&type=search&plindex=0")... It is now on Ultimate 1.31+ [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/#1.4") (feisty based) hosted on a separate server, however 1 package was missing and is now fixed prior to "official release.  For those running Ultimate 1.31+ or just feisty and would like to try it below is how to get compiz fusion running.  **Please test at your own risk**, just because it works on mine is no guarantee it will work on yours.

**Step 1** remove old compiz and desktop effects:
```
sudo apt-get remove compiz-core desktop-effects
```

**Step 2** with Ultimate 1.4+ [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/#1.4") enabled (does not exist on Ultimate edgy based repo) update and install compiz fusion.
```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade
sudo apt-get install compiz compiz-gnome compizconfig-settings-manager compiz-fusion-plugins-extra libcompizconfig-backend-gconf

```

**Step 3** Fire it up... In a terminal
```
compiz --replace
```

Just some notes of interest as with previous posts adding the following to your screen section may be necessary to get "window decorations" to work, typically NVIDIA cards:

open a terminal  applications>accessories>terminal
```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

goto the "screen section and add the following:

```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals"  "True"
```

It should look similar to this:
```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals"  "True"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7600 GT]"
    Monitor        "CMC 22 W"
    DefaultDepth    24
```

Make sure default depth is 24

save and exit press ctrl-alt-backspace to restart xserver.  Compiz fusion should now work fine;)  Enjoy!!!

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-05
> **gabeyg said:**
> Hey, where can I find source code of Ubuntu Ultimate? I was searching the ubuntu ultimate site for 1 month... 
**By the way, how did you manage to make musics to play in installer?

The source of what makes Ultimate current edition based on feisty is a [script]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh") I wrote.  For 1.3 or less I used a standard Ubuntu disk you can obtain all source from their [website]("http://www.ubuntu.com/products/WhatIsUbuntu/desktopedition") and [reconstructor]("http://reconstructor.aperantis.com/").  If you would like to learn how I made it please review my [FAQ]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/faq.html#distro").

Music... I used software called timidity to play it.  I pre-injected the music onto the disk they are not mp3's they are .mods and s3m's similar to, but better then a midi, but not copy written.

the statement in the script that plays the music is:

```
timidity /usr/share/ultimate/playlist.m3u
```

Currently just works with the cd version.  Will work with the official 1.4 DVD as well.  Or perhaps in your case:

```
timidity /usr/share/ultimate/halloween_theme.s3m
```

The script now dynamically updates itself from my repo by version checking the one on the server, unfortunately I had not written that section until after release of the beta dvd.  So any build / release from here on out will be able to be upgraded without downloading 2 GB each time, however I will still offer a DVD version of both Ultimate as well as gamers edition.  Look for the gamers edition to fill an entire DVD, [America's Army]("http://www.americasarmy.com/") for example is 775.8 MB and thats just 1 of the many games that will be included.

I can not count how many times I have built the CD version and currently hangs at either 54% or 69% of being installed.  This issue will be resolved before release.

If you are running the beta DVD below is a way to force it to update the script... once done 1 time will not have to be done again as the script will do it for you in a terminal:

```
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh

```

Click on the upgrade icon on the desktop and enjoy your new features.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-05
> **abrichr said:**
> I apologize if the answers to my questions have been posted somewhere, but I haven't been able to find them.

1) Is there a 64-bit version of Ubuntu Ultimate?  Are there plans for one in the future?

2) Are you still maintaining Ubuntu Ultimate Gamer's?  Will there be one based on Feisty or Gutsy? 

I'm interested in testing/using Ubuntu Ultimate or Ubuntu Ultimate Gamer's, but I like to know that I'm getting the most out of my hardware and the best software available.

Thanks in advance.

I have made valiant attempts at making a 64 bit version, which failed to build more times then I can count, this was in the edgy days, I have not however tried to build it in feisty.  I have my hands full and do not currently have time to attempt another build, may be something I re-attempt in the future.

Ubuntu Gamers Edition is 3 full releases away, CD version which has been a bear to me followed by a final build of 1.4.  Then Gamers Edition, for those that don't want to wait for the Gamers Edition DVD the CD Version is upgradeable to any version including grabbing the games off the net and installing them for you, fully customizable.  Main reasons for creating the CD version are: 

1.  Script able engine allowing enhancements, adding apps /games from a centrally served script (not having to download 2GB when Ultimate goes to 1.5 etc.)
2.  Not everyone has a DVD burner
3.  Customization (hand pick what you would like installed)

Negatives:

1.  Each person that installs it sucks a ton of bandwidth from my repo currently hosts most packages,  Some packages (games) are huge.
2.  If someone screws up their OS and reinstalls see #1 ;)

Gusty?  I currently have no intention, each release I have released in the past were built off stable Ubuntu images and meant to be stable, and have stood up to it.  Perhaps time permitting in the future that may happen, but I have quite a workload ahead of me currently.

### Post by gabeyg on 2007-07-08
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> The source of what makes Ultimate current edition based on feisty is a [script]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh") I wrote.  For 1.3 or less I used a standard Ubuntu disk you can obtain all source from their [website]("http://www.ubuntu.com/products/WhatIsUbuntu/desktopedition") and [reconstructor]("http://reconstructor.aperantis.com/").  If you would like to learn how I made it please review my [FAQ]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/faq.html#distro").

Music... I used software called timidity to play it.  I pre-injected the music onto the disk they are not mp3's they are .mods and s3m's similar to, but better then a midi, but not copy written.

the statement in the script that plays the music is:

```
timidity /usr/share/ultimate/playlist.m3u
```

Currently just works with the cd version.  Will work with the official 1.4 DVD as well.  Or perhaps in your case:

```
timidity /usr/share/ultimate/halloween_theme.s3m
```

The script now dynamically updates itself from my repo by version checking the one on the server, unfortunately I had not written that section until after release of the beta dvd.  So any build / release from here on out will be able to be upgraded without downloading 2 GB each time, however I will still offer a DVD version of both Ultimate as well as gamers edition.  Look for the gamers edition to fill an entire DVD, [America's Army]("http://www.americasarmy.com/") for example is 775.8 MB and thats just 1 of the many games that will be included.

I can not count how many times I have built the CD version and currently hangs at either 54% or 69% of being installed.  This issue will be resolved before release.

If you are running the beta DVD below is a way to force it to update the script... once done 1 time will not have to be done again as the script will do it for you in a terminal:

```
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh

```

Click on the upgrade icon on the desktop and enjoy your new features.

HI, And I understand, but.. How can I compile your .sh file?

ATI All-in-Wonder TV capture software
The General ATI TV and Overlay Software (GATOS) suite for

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-08
> **CrankyFilipino said:**
> Lot's of posts on this thread. I just had one question hoping someone could answer. Can you install this over your current Ubuntu install? Or does it have to be brand new?

Have never tried... However even if you did would not get login screen, and other customizations.  Software yes but things like compiz fusion would not be there.  If you would like to try please run the script attached to this post.  For those concerned below is the source code:

```
#!/bin/sh
#
# Feisty to Ultimate Script 
#
# Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
rm -R *
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
chmod +x gamers.sh
cd ~/Desktop/
apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy
#create Shortcut
echo "Creating Upgrade shortcut..."
# create desktop shortcut for Upgrade
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

You will have to have the [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/") enabled before executing this script.  It will create a shortcut on your desktop that says Upgrade.  Enjoy!!!  ;)

---

### Post by CrankyFilipino on 2007-07-08
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> Have never tried... However even if you did would not get login screen, and other customizations.  Software yes but things like compiz fusion would not be there.  If you would like to try please run the script attached to this post.  For those concerned below is the source code:

```
#!/bin/sh
#
# Feisty to Ultimate Script 
#
# Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
rm -R *
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
chmod +x gamers.sh
cd ~/Desktop/
apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy
#create Shortcut
echo "Creating Upgrade shortcut..."
# create desktop shortcut for Upgrade
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

You will have to have the [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/") enabled before executing this script.  It will create a shortcut on your desktop that says Upgrade.  Enjoy!!!  ;)

### Post by misGnomer on 2007-07-09
Interesting project but the nomenclature confuses me a little with its Ubuntu name and a new version numbering.

Did you get a permission from Canonical to use their trademarked "Ubuntu" name for your releases, domains and the logo too. The presentation of your project certainly lookss quite official (e.g. in terms of security), especially for the uninitiated.

What about the hosting of 3rd party proprietary packages on your (unauthenticated) ubuntusoftware.info site?

Your website's "contact" and "about" pages were nonexisting links so I'm kind of wondering who's actually vouching for the software on offer and its  security.

If it was simply "TheeMahn's Ultimate Linux distro (based on Ubuntu xx)", or even "TheeMahn's Ultimate Automatix-on-steroids Script for Ubuntu xx", it probably wouldn't have piqued my interest. But since it's "Ubuntu" from a "ubuntusoftware.info" site...

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-09
> **misGnomer said:**
> Interesting project but the nomenclature confuses me a little with its Ubuntu name and a new version numbering.

Did you get a permission from Canonical to use their trademarked "Ubuntu" name for your releases, domains and the logo too. The presentation of your project certainly lookss quite official (e.g. in terms of security), especially for the uninitiated.

What about the hosting of 3rd party proprietary packages on your (unauthenticated) ubuntusoftware.info site?

Your website's "contact" and "about" pages were nonexisting links so I'm kind of wondering who's actually vouching for the software on offer and its  security.

If it was simply "TheeMahn's Ultimate Linux distro (based on Ubuntu xx)", or even "TheeMahn's Ultimate Automatix-on-steroids Script for Ubuntu xx", it probably wouldn't have piqued my interest. But since it's "Ubuntu" from a "ubuntusoftware.info" site...

And I quote from ubuntu's website:
```
This draft trademark policy is itself published under the CC-BY-SA license, you are welcome to base your own project trademark policies off it, just let others use your changes and give credit to the Ubuntu project as the original source!.
```

This I do thorough out my website.

Logo:
```
Logo Usage Guidelines

Our logos are presented in multiple colours and it is important that their visual integrity be maintained. It is therefore preferable that the logos only be used in their standard form but if you should feel the need to alter them in any way you should keep the following guidelines in mind. It should also be borne in mind that the more you wish to vary our logos from their standard form the smaller is the chance that we will be able to approve your proposed use.

    *

      If presented in multiple colours, the logo should only use the “official” logo colours.
    *

      You may use transparency and gradient/depth tools but should retain the “official” colours.
    *

      A monotone version may be acceptable in certain situations, if the use requires it (e.g. desktop backgrounds).
    *

      Any scaling must retain the original proportions of the logo.

```

In which I do.

As far as licensed software...

I do not accept any license agreement the "end user" you must accept the license agreement.  The script will download it open a terminal for you to accept each piece of software that requires it.

The about page has been started actually there are 3 separate pages (have yet to be merged).  We now have a webmaster on it.  But I can not give you a deadline as to when it will be done.  Actually a great portion of the website is incomplete.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-09
> **ccal said:**
> TheeMahn, keep on your good work, we appreciate all you have done for the community :guitar: 

A LinuxTracker member (xmaus) reported the "check CD for defects" option menu test fails with 2 errors. I checked my iso image, same error message, I don't know if this is critical as md5sum is ok.

The cd check in the menu will only compare the "original disk" to the md5sum it is built upon the md5sum and feistys are different so it will always fail it's own cd check.  The md5sum I have posted is correct if it passes md5sum before burning the disk should be ok, I do however suggest burning at a slower speed to ensure integrity is met.  I am not sure where md5sum is stored on the disk, I'm sure I could somehow replace it with the correct one.  something I will have to look into.  Just so that portion works.

### Post by misGnomer on 2007-07-09
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> And I quote from ubuntu's website:
```
This draft trademark policy is itself published under the CC-BY-SA license, you are welcome to base your own project trademark policies off it, just let others use your changes and give credit to the Ubuntu project as the original source!.
```

This I do thorough out my website.

Well I didn't really see any clear references to Canonical or Ubuntu's origins so the crediting on your site appears somewhat ambiguous. Somehow I couldn't imagine Red Hat or Mandriva allowing third parties to use their respective trademarks quite as directly.

[http://www.ubuntu.com/aboutus/trademarkpolicy](http://www.ubuntu.com/aboutus/trademarkpolicy)

There were quite a few other requirements for using the Ubuntu trademark so surely it wouldn't be too troublesome to write to the email address on that page and get an official blessing for your home-rolled "Ubuntu Ultimate" release.

> As far as licensed software...

I do not accept any license agreement the "end user" you must accept the license agreement.  The script will download it open a terminal for you to accept each piece of software that requires it.

AFAIK some of those proprietary vendors may prohibit distribution without permission for various reasons, such as to be able to monitor the number of downloads (and usage). If your scripts download the original packages from the vendor's site and they've approved of the scripted method it's naturally alright.

> The about page has been started actually there are 3 separate pages (have yet to be merged).  We now have a webmaster on it.  But I can not give you a deadline as to when it will be done.  Actually a great portion of the website is incomplete.

I was merely wondering. Contact and About infos are typically expected from a site that offers something as important as a whole distro or scripts to access relatively unknown or unverified repos. A third party repo could push anything to users' machines, especially if there is implied trust. Perhaps crediting Canonical and "their version of Ubuntu" would be worth a link or two as well.

---

### Post by ascetic on 2007-07-09
i have just downloaded the Ultimate CD Edition 1.4 and burned to cd. everything boot properly until I entered the login page. 
It asked me for Username and Password! randomly entered several names and passes. No Work. 
Any solution? Is this a bug? Or what is exactly the username/pass?

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-09
> **ascetic said:**
> i have just downloaded the Ultimate CD Edition 1.4 and burned to cd. everything boot properly until I entered the login page. 
It asked me for Username and Password! randomly entered several names and passes. No Work. 
Any solution? Is this a bug? Or what is exactly the username/pass?

It should not do that it is a problem with the ubuntu-desktop meta package.  no bug report filed as most people do not build disks.  I will be straight in telling you what I had to do was to whack my current installation (I assume you run feisty) and all went through fine why a live disk scans your current drive / os before being installed especially when some may just want to run it as a live CD.  I have no clue.  I will not file a bug report there are a bunch filed against it one being that of gaims involvement as well as compiz.  The actual package does nothing except its involvement as a live disk as far as I can see, and can be removed once installed without any re-percussion.

I know this is not the answer you were looking for but thought I would post for some of the other users.  I apologize I do not have a fix for your situation other then a workaround...

I found a HowTo on setting up a windows / ubuntu network, this evening, a huge [howto]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=202605"), Ultimate did everything for me (after upgrade and selecting file sharing) to set up a windows network minus setting a password, and the folder I wish to share.  I thought I would post a howto :)

Step 1 select the folder you wish to share on the comp Ubuntu Ultimate is installed.  Right click hit share... SMB and give it a name or you can leave the name the same see shot 1 & 2 & 3, may add what the windows side looks like later.  I assure you this works.

Step 2 open a terminal:
```
sudo smbpasswd -L -a **your_username**
```

replace **your_username** with your user name on the ultimate system.  when it asks for password enter it 3 times.  Once for the sudo part and twice to set it.

Step 3 goto your windows machine and grab what you shared ;)  It will ask you for a password enter the same password you set.

Amazes even me how much Ultimate has made a difference as far as things that used to take me all day are now a minuscule task ;)  This will only get better as time progresses.

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-10
For those with video problems...

Envy is in its earily stages, I love the work he has taken on as well the programmer he is an admin here.  He has worked hard to make what he has made believe that...  Imagine writing software for all computers, at first glance seemingly easy at first glance NVIDIA or ATI... as most computers run,  The real question... Primitive or top of the line ATI, NVidia?  It  is a huge task he has undertaken, there is no guarantee it will work, however an option.  I'm sure [tseliot ]("http://www.albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html")did not believe that his software would be used to help make an os be more forthcoming however has been used twice.  Mint uses it in the same fashion.  I did ask him prior to release of 1.4, and can upload newer versions to my repo and all benefit instantly.  I will let you know does not work on mine SLI (2 X NVidia 7600 GT's), we just opened a new ball of wax there :)  Like I said an option at this point.

Never, slam upon him for his work or me I guess because your video drivers do not work.  I offer 3 methods of installing drivers from my website, and my god theres probably 1,000 others from just this site.  What may work for one may or may not work for you.

I am sorry if I seem like I am being a D*** I just understand what is involved...:guitar:

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-11
> **CrankyFilipino said:**
> for some reason boswars doesn't install right.. also i messed up somethign the first go around.. so i deleted everything.. added 15 GB to the partition and re-installed.. hopefully this time i won't mess up anything this time..

There is an error in the boswars section of the script or with a dependancy problem.  it indeed installs boswars-data but not boswars itself this should have been handled as a dependency to boswars-data, however it did not.  It will be fixed at the next script update.  If you would like it fixed now open a terminal and enter the following:

```
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes boswars
```

Sorry about that...

### Post by DigitalScar on 2007-07-12
Well, you really did it this time... My wife and kids are REALLY upset with me now thanks to Ubuntu Ultimate!

For years, I have been playing with different Linux distros trying to find one that was good enough to finally break the "Window"s and switch for good. I knew that Ubuntu was going to be the one to do it. For the past year now I have had dual boot, Ubuntu and Windows XP then Vista. I of course favored Ubuntu and the wife and kids used Microsuct. I downloaded Ubuntu Ultimate last week and installed it on my Ubuntu partition. I LOVED IT!!! Since then I have wiped my entire hard drive and installed Ubuntu Ultimate and nothing else! My wife and kids are having windoze withdrawals but every time they come up with a program they used in winders I was able to quickly ease their minds with an open source alternative. They are slowly being converted into Ubuntuians... Muhahaaa my evil plan is working...

To be honest, I think that if (when) I get Beryl to work they will all love it!

Thank you for all of your hard work and PLEASE keep it up. 


-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-
DigitalScar

### Post by wolfen69 on 2007-07-13
WOW! i'm using the new live cd right now, and i have to say i'm impressed. i'm so glad that a cd version came out. the dvd was a little too much for me. it's easy to install any extras you may want anyway. it was the easiest no brain install EVER. one great thing i love about it is that if you have enough RAM, you can treat it like a regular install and make system changes and download packages, etc.   you could easily forget you're on a live cd. i had the system up and running 100% like on my regular install within 10 min.  and thats with TV tuner setup, bittorrent, etc. awesome! great job.  i will definitely put ultimate on someones computer that wants to try linux.

i decided to re-seed for a while. i'll give all the upload bandwidth i can spare.

### Post by drewu on 2007-07-14
@TheeMahn2003
The link to beryl svn is timing out, so installing it via svn isn't going to work for your tutorial at [http://ubuntusoftware.info/beryl.html#compile]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/beryl.html#compile")
So I thought I might write my own process down here. It uses repos instead of compiling, so it may be more user friendly too.

Note though: this worked for me, and since I am going off of a couple of beryl's web tutorials there is no guarantee this is going to work for everyone else. But it should it theory, right?

Here is my method:
First make a backup of sources.list (optional)
```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list.backup
```

Open sources.list
```
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

Paste these repos into it
```
deb http://download.tuxfamily.org/3v1deb feisty eyecandy
deb-src http://download.tuxfamily.org/3v1deb feisty eyecandy
```

Save and exit

update
```
sudo apt-get update
```

install the packages
```
sudo apt-get -y --allow-unauthenticated install beryl beryl-manager emerald-themes 
```

if you are using nvidia (note that I don't really know what this does, just got it off the tut. seems to work though.)
```
sudo nvidia-xconfig --add-argb-glx-visuals
```

Make beryl-manager autostart (optional, you can always start it yourself from Applications->System Tools->Beryl Manager)
```
sudo cp /usr/share/applications/beryl-manager.desktop /etc/xdg/autostart/beryl-manager.desktop

```

setup some desktop icons (optional)
```

cp  /usr/share/applications/beryl-manager.desktop ~/Desktop/beryl-manager.desktop
```

Well it worked for me, Again, this is kinda patched together from all over the beryl website, so tell me if it is correct. Especially ATI because I was unable to test it.

---

Off work early day ;) not that it feels that way, and don't have to work until Monday.  ;)

Now onto your problem...  In a terminal...
```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

go down to your screen section and add the following:
```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
```

it should look similar to this do not copy and paste this:

```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
```

Save and exit.  Press and hold ctrl-alt and hit backspace.  Or you can reboot the computer and try it again.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-14
Wasn't going to build Ultimate 1.4 DVD today because I worked enough today, however a nice warm shower and a few brews have encouraged me ;)  Building now.

```
124 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove and 5 not upgraded.
Need to get 135MB of archives.
After unpacking 31.6MB of additional disk space will be used.
```

Sounds miniscule...  I will continue to edit this post until perfection is met.

People often wonder why I have a terabyte of space I will show you the answer before I am done.

The following happens each and every time I build a disk most times it happens 100's of times (DVD's are far worse as far as consumption):

```
Total translation table size: 2048
Total rockridge attributes bytes: 46826
Total directory bytes: 161792
Path table size(bytes): 968
Max brk space used 62000
352665 extents written (688 MB)

Build Time: 0:15.60  CPU: 12%

ISO Located: /media/BigMac/ubuntu-ultimate-1.4dvd-administrative.iso
```

No longer minuscule, please allow me to explain if the kernel was upgraded proceed to step #1, I want to help everyone understand what is involved.  I also encourage you to make your Ubuntu blah blah edition.  Give credit of course to Canonical w/o was not possible.  Do I view Linux Mint as competition?  I probably provided him the how to do I care no.  Open source movement advancing is what I want to happen, I openly provide my source, one way to kick the Evil empire in the A** ;)

Burn & test, for this I recommend a DVD/RW actually at least 2 of them 1 for the working copy and 1 for the testing...

I will continue this post as far as involvement, until I see can't post 32,000 characters or close to that.  But I want to help others and will do so, if I help just 1 person this post is not wasted...

### Post by BlahMan_of.Doom on 2007-07-17
Thanks dude. I just installed this today, only one problem. You should make it so that Envy launches at the end of installation, because Ubuntu will attempt to install the Mesa drivers over the nVidia, while leaving the xorg to nVidia, therefore creating a mass confusion. This happened to me, i just redid the xorg by scratch :(.

But other than that, it's bundled with some pretty cool stuff.

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-17
> **-Vampyre- said:**
> Took the dive. Ultimate runs fine. Just a couple of quick questions. The update script. I ran the gamers, does it have different things on different options between the gamers upgrade adn the others. Also I was trying to install read/write access through Automatix and it failed. Is there any NTFS software you recommend? Other than that the upgrade went smooth and me likey.

Thanks

The Gamers option just installs games, nothing else, ultimate will install commonly used apps, custom allows you to pick and choose what you would like to install, I highly recommend this option.  In this option there is a NTFS read/write check it it will install a program that will be located in applications>>system tools>>NTFS configuration tool.  Start it up and check both boxes and click apply it provides instant write access to your NTFS partitions, both internal and external, enjoy and thanks.

It is now building... Fully loaded and surprisingly smaller then other DVD releases I have put out.  The distro "name" was not changed on any release I have made even though I could have done so, the reason I left it the same is to allow for the end user to just that upgrade, as well as use custom repos.  As far as converting from feisty to Ultimate I have written a script it is posted somewhere within this thread that does just that.  when Gusty goes final, I will re-write the script to check what it is running on and allow the end user to make the decision of weather or not to upgrade to gusty based.  The script will be an answer to prayers as I continue to work on it people can no longer see adjustments I make to the script as I use a new location for testing purposes before committing changes to everyone who is running it, this avoids any chance of messing up anyones distro, everything is well tested from here out to ensure that nothing borks up someones installation of Ultimate.

```
Total translation table size: 2048
Total rockridge attributes bytes: 46826
Total directory bytes: 161792
Path table size(bytes): 968
Max brk space used 62000
775847 extents written (1515 MB)

Build Time: 0:38.37  CPU: 13%

ISO Located: /media/BigMac/ubuntu-ultimate-1.4dvd.iso
Build Complete...
```

Attempt to upgrade to gusty gibson from Ultimate 1.4 CD results so far:
```
1131 upgraded, 78 newly installed, 9 to remove and 1 not upgraded.
Need to get 874MB of archives.
After unpacking 306MB of additional disk space will be used.
```

So yes it will do so, will post results...  This also means it is indeed possible to upgrade from 1.3 to 1.4 with a little work on my end.  I will not post how I did it.  I will not risk the chance of someone copying and pasting what they see, without knowing what they are doing.  If someone else is interested in doing so please PM me, I will explain what you are about to undertake.

Well an hour just downloading updates... Even at 800+ KPS (capability)will take quite a while, when completed claims will only use 306MB additional space that in itself is quite impressive.  I have never seen Gusty so it will all be new to me.  I have nothing to loose, but maybe the hour ;)

Final results?

```
Fetched 874MB in 49m51s (292kB/s)

Sorry for the delay in mailing you back, I'm sure you can understand how busy it is for me around here. http://www.tuxdistro.com/torrents-details.php?id=396 , crazy downloaded 566,000+ times, just in torrent traffic, I have seen well over 100,000 downloads as well does not include mirror activity. This may be the reason the net  shuts down on you; someone may have been attempting to break into your system, although you may view this as a bad thing to loose your internet, it may have took it from you with good reason.  This is not in 1.2, I learned about vulnerability from a advanced book on Linux TCP/IP exposed and tried to protect you, and everyone else for that matter. ### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-27
> **acowboydave said:**
> Like I said thumbs up. I am still having problems with ugrade. If you get a chance will you look at my thread about lib/x11/fonts. Also in the dpkg. log it starts off with the date of 4/15/07 and it seems like the pkgs. are half installed. Ran apt-get update and at the end recieved this message. I know your busy for just my problem, but you know it the best.

replace your source.list as follows in a terminal:
```
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

Paste and replace with the following:
```
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty main
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security main restricted
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security main restricted
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty restricted
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty universe
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security universe
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu feisty-security universe
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty multiverse
deb http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ feisty-updates main restricted
deb http://repoubuntusoftware.info/ feisty all
```

save & exit

sudo apt-get update the errors should be gone you had dupes in your sources.list.

I will get back to you on the other problem in a few minutes after I have looked at it.

Edit:  I can't find your "04/15" post on my screen it shows weeks ago 1 day ago etc.

---

I would try the following in a terminal:
```
sudo apt-get install -f
```

as well as:
```
sudo dpkg --configure -a
```

Sounds like a broken or missing package the above should resolve your situation.  If it does not work please post.

Edit: 2.6.20-15 kernel has been replace with 16 just to let you know.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-07-29
> **diablillo77 said:**

I'm not a seasoned Ubuntu user. I started with Feisty about 2 months ago and the only reason I boot Windows is to share my HSPDA connection with the other to machines in my apartment.  At work I use nothing my Feisty! kinda proud of that :D anyway. I downloaded Ultimate yesterday and installed it. The script should have told you afterasking you if you want music or not "This will most likely take a long time depending on your computer speed, selection and internet connection, even if it appears as nothing is happening please be patient.  This will all be fixed as I update the script.  WARNING!!!  Canceling while the script is downloading or installing may have very negative and perhaps irr-reversible results, please do not close the currently opened terminal window it will be closed at the end of the upgrade process for you."

I'm not sure where it quit you may want to try the following in a terminal in this order:

```
sudo apt-get install -f
```

```
sudo dpkg --configure -a
```

```
sudo apt-get clean
sudo apt-get autoclean
```

Try to continue...

### Post by diablillo77 on 2007-07-30
Hey guys! i'm back... I found that somehow my gamers.sh file when blank that's why the upgrade wouldn't continue. I booted from the liveCD, copied the gamers.sh file and pasted it back in my install. I also found that if the upgrade stops, the next time it'll just go down the list skiping all that is already installed until it gets to where it left off and continues from there. Cool! ### Post by lotuseclat79 on 2007-07-31
Hi,

I am an Ultimate Ubuntu 1.4 Live CD user.  I have a 4-disk system with Red Hat Fedora Core 3 (FC3) permanently installed on one disk that I use to store items that enhance the experience of using UU 1.4.

In the normal course of initializing my use of the Live CD, I make a mount point (as root), then issue a mount command over to my FC3 disk.  I then copy over a shell script that copies into the Live CD environment (in RAM), the items that I may typically use.

Just yesterday, I ran into the situation that the mount command only partially succeeded.  The directory in which I store everything related to UU went missing (at least as far as I could see). A minor panic could have ensued, but I normally don't panic until I am able to verify whether something is actually lost or not.  Lucky I back things up!

Upon rebooting my system to FC3, the missing directory was really there with all of the expected contents.  Since it looked like UU1.4 was not going to be able to successfully mount the directory on disk, I switched from UU 1.4  to Ubuntu 6.0.6 LTS Live CD.  The result was that I was able to mount the directory, and I connected to the Internet using U 6.0.6 LTS.

Today, UU1.4 was able to successfully mount and access the data that went missing yesterday.

What I would like to know is whether the partial mount was probably caused more by the number of times my system was booted via Live CD usage without booting from the FC3 disk in the interim and because I have saved a lot to the disk, or may have some other cause?

Is it likely that I may need to issue a sync command (as root) in the mounted partition after saving data before I exit the Live CD?  I suspect that I may need to do this as a standard precautionary measure to avoid this occurence in the future.  

Your thoughts on this issue please.

Tia,

-- Tom

### Post by acowboydave on 2007-07-31
In Ubuntu all you have to do is in terminal enter (sudo aptitude install kubuntu-desktop)..it will install about 167 pkgs. To remove Kubuntu enter(sudo aptitude remove kubuntu-desktop). ### Post by nynoah on 2007-08-01
Try the program again.  Really all it is, is a graphical interface that lets you reconfigure you Xserver without having to go through the command line part of it.  The reason I liked it was because it gave me the ability to check my system before I saved the changes.   Which is nice for people who might make a mistake.  I could keep them from bigger headaches if they configure the xserver wrong.  

Really I am just a lightweight learning as I go.  So please take a lot that I saw with a grain of salt.  I am not an expert.



Do you happen to know why my system keeps on wanting to remove Kubuntu in the adept updater?  It lists it as installed but always requests to remove it for some reason.  It runs fine.  I am using Kubuntu right now to type this message.  

Thanks
Noah

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-03
> **PharaohSD said:**
> Hey TheeMahn,

downloading the 1.4 dvd right now, great stuff ;)

saw the youtube video and was wondering how you brought all the open windows from the different desktops in one place (if that was actually what you did, video time: 2 min 18 sec) what's the shortcut for that?

And what program was running at the bottom left corner at the beginning of the video?

ps. when will the 64bit version be out?

drag your mouse to the very top right and it will "tile" your windows (with compiz fusion running).  The program at the bottom left is MGM (Moaning Goat Meter), available under applications>>system tools>>MGM System Monitor.

I currently have no intention of writing a 64 bit edition, this does not mean it is not going to happen; just no intentions as of now.  On to Gamers edition next probably over 4 GB would not surprise me.

### Post by NumericalMe on 2007-08-05

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-05
> **NumericalMe said:**
> Is there a problem with the upgrade script?  On two separate machines, I ended up with a mostly blank file gamers.sh after it said that a newer version was available on the server, etc...

I was messing around with some other things, but I just wanted to check...

Thanks.

Well, I will touch bases with you  The script is exactly the same and has not changed since you could have even downloaded 1.4 CD or DVD for that matter, I have seen it change but no other person could have seen it, maybe ccal don't know how fast he can download & install.  Otherwise not a chance.  The only way it could have been zeroed is no net connection.  When I released it the script indeed was changed, and grabs it from the server, and upgrades it to the newest.  1.4 downloads 1.32 was the latest prior, I wrote the script with this in mind.

I will take the time to explain...  I did shoddily release 1.4 because I knew I could fix it script wise on the CD edition, the DVD, however is a new ball of wax and has made if no-one else happy me.  I do have my hands full in case you have not seen so, do you want to volunteer to work for me?  All is now fixable...  I do feel I explained that well with last post.  Do you want to crucify me because XMMS is not there?

From here forward after the release of gamers I can adjust every "Ultimate" to add new features, upgrade, etc...   All will be script based, including mime based as well as adding a news engine.   Weather you choose that option or not is totally your choice.  I am here to help If nothing else get you started.  I will also continue to help "time permitting".

Otherwise, you can as initial post says download the newest version [here]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh").

Please excuse me if I seem like I am being a prick, I do have my hands full.  Imagine for a minute take care of 100,000 ppl, nothing personal.

Hardware:

Dell 6400, 2 GB RAM, T7400 processors, ATI Mobility Radeon X1400 display adapter, Broadcom 802.11n network adapter... need more?

Thanks a billion if you can get me started.

Nick

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-05
> **snomann said:**
> I have checked the byte size of the DVD image and it is the same as the size shown at your web site.

Further to the first message, if I persist with the install/run I get the following:

isolinux: Disk error 40, AX=0201, drive 00

open a terminal goto where the iso is located and enter the following command:
```
md5sum ubuntu-ultimate-1.4-dvd.iso
```

If the number does not exactly match: **a4b8f312737cb2bab78cd22190e1bd49** delete it and re-download

Burned a few different times, but still cant get it to boot up consistantly. Foxconn 6100 socket 939 MB/AMD64 3100/1GB DDR2100/Linksys WMP54GR (RT61)/

Sounds like you have a hardware problem, these are difficult to know exactly what is causing it, I would start by booting from the DVD and selecting memory test let it go all the way through, if you see so much as one error most likely your ram is bad or you have it overclocked too high.  This is just where I would start.  If it does I would go into the bios and "load defaults" and try again.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-07
I have modified this post to make it into a Video drivers mini-howto

ffmpeg & transcode are integrated onto the DVD, personally I compile my own driver for my nvidia card ... Gives me the newest release and seems to be quite stable.  I am going to now write a short how to for you and others this is the exact method in which I install to get everything current and working...  I suggest you print this post as there will be a time when you will be unable to read this post.

**Pay close attention to the order in which video drivers go in**

1. Install UUE DVD
2. initial bootscript I install acro read and flash no video drivers yet.
3. Run upgrade script, let it complete this upgrades the kernel - still no video drivers installed.
4. reboot
5. Install Video drivers

There are 3 methods (I can think of currently) of installing video drivers, I suggest the envy method (Applications>>System Tools>>Envy) for those running 1.4+, if it fails run envy -t in the terminal and remove them, you can also try the restricted manager (System>>Restricted Drivers Manager), if all else has failed and irregardless even if it was successful be sure to review the xorg section of this mini-howto:  

**_Video Drivers: Nvidia_**

Note: Some may find it easier to download the driver in the GUI then to have to type wget blah blah you can obtain the newest nvidia driver [here]("http://us.download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/100.14.11/NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run").  After you download it set execution rights on it (see [screenshot]("http://ubuntuforums.org/attachment.php?attachmentid=40091&d=1186514414")).  Right click properties execution rights checked.  Those that use the GUI method **will not** have to enter 2 of the following commands as seen below:
```
wget -c http://us.download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/100.14.11/NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run
sudo chmod 777 NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run
```

_Getting started_

1. Press ctrl-alt-F2 & login
2. terminal:```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop
wget -c http://us.download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/100.14.11/NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run
sudo chmod 777 NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run
sudo ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86-100.14.11-pkg1.run

```
3. accept the license agreement, yes you wish to compile, no you do not wish to download your kernel, yes update your xorg, in my case I copy an xorg overtop /etc/X11/xorg.conf from another hard disk, this keeps all my tweaks intact.
4. Bring up the GUI:```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start
```

_XORG_
Edit your xorg to allow for beryl / compiz.

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

Go down to the **screen** section and paste the following:
```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
```

Should look similar to:
```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
```

Save and exit, press ctrl-alt-backspace to restart x-server with changes.
Enjoy your [cutting edge video driver]("http://ubuntuforums.org/attachment.php?attachmentid=40092&d=1186515243") & Beryl / Compiz ;)

### Post by Lucretius on 2007-08-08
> **jfg69 said:**
> WOW, booted up no problems. Actually recognized my wireless. After a few attempts, I was able to get a signal showing, but no connection. It kept losing the WEP key settings and all the wireless info had to keep being re-entered.

I'm about to see what happens if I install it, not sure if that will fix those issues, but its def a giant step in the right direction!

Only other thing I noticed was that the Firefox page pops up with "Welcome to 7.04 Fiesty Fawn" as opposed to 7.10 Gutsy Gibbon... how do I confirm the actual build?

Glad to see it worked for you, I would imagine bugs are still being worked out but from what I gather Gutsy is a big step in the right direction.

after installing all the restricted drivers I would recommend doing this.

Open Terminal:

type:

"sudo apt-get update"
enter your password when it asks

after it updates you can then type

"sudo apt-get upgrade"

this will upgrade your software

you can then type

"sudo apt-get dist-upgrade"

this will make sure you are running the latest gutsy build

I do this on a daily basis, but it's up to you if you are happy with it then you can leave it :)

It will boot up to user/pw. The the screen goes black for a while and the wallpaper shows up. Thats it, goes no further. After rebooting to the restored(?) option, it gave me an error relating to Compiz.

How do I get back in to disable the NV drivers?

NVidia is easy, envy -t in a terminal
remove all drivers and [reinstall]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472"), restricted which I assume involved your wireless should have been done before rebooting.  My main post warns about this.

And I quote: "Burn & test All upgrades from feisty 116 in all are integrated on the cd, only upgrade I did not preform is the kernel, the script will handle this for you, however you will have to install any restricted driver prior to reboot, envy will take care of video but some may have to add wireless drivers. You have been forewarned."  <<- this is wireless for example.  This is the same with DVD and most likely gamers as well.

Every problem you see is due to a kernel upgrade, it is the same in feisty etc.  I did it for you in the "christmas edition" and realized I can not do so w/o being sued by nvidia etc., as well as anything not released as GPL or involves a license agreement, java, flash etc.  I apologizes I can not "just integrate" everything onto the DVD.

I also have a wireless card and offered to give the card to ccal, doesn't work anymore the neighbors either went wired or didn't pay their cable bill ;)  Also linksys (RA2500 chipset), worked perfect with any ubuntu I ever installed.  His however is PCMCIA, so all went to the crapper on that idea.  In order for it to work however I did have to get the net working from the live DVD, then install.  The upgrade icon dropped my net (after reboot the wireless anyways) which I didn't care cuz I do have wired.  The upgrade script will upgrade your kernel no matter what direction you choose, when it says finalizing distro that is exactly what it is doing.  This is the point in which you want to remove restricted drivers and re-install them.

I am sorry to inform you but as it stands you will have to do so each and every time your kernel is upgraded, be it running ultimate, feisty etc.

I am currently working on having dual cable (will be 1.8MB a sec. yes, megabytes not megabits per sec), I have had dual cable before but not that fast, kinda makes me feel like I'm on a college line ;)  For what I download building Gamers currently the repo will feel the feeling ;)

Example of current:

```
Get:253 http://repoubuntusoftware.info feisty/all ufoai-data 2.1-0~getdeb1 [257MB]

```

O.K. an explanation is in order 253'rd program downloaded 257 MB download, how many total?
```
0 upgraded, 296 newly installed, 0 to remove and 7 not upgraded.
Need to get 2136MB of archives.
After unpacking 3383MB of additional disk space will be used.
```

the 7 will turn most likely into a 3 when done, all kernel related.

This is stage 1, had to step back to original 1.4 DVD (even though what I run is actually better).  I will not accept anything short of perfect, if I have to start over so be it.  Sorry for posting to you for something which in all rights not related minus the kernel thing :)

Lets turn this into 1.8MB a sec (I think my webservers are capped to 600KPS per user):

```
Get:296 http://repoubuntusoftware.info feisty/all toycars 0.3.3-1~getdeb1 [87.8kB]
Fetched 2136MB in 58m3s (613kB/s)

### Post by lotuseclat79 on 2007-08-10
With regard to post #372,376,380:  I posted a thread in the General Help support forum: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3165827#post3165827](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3165827#post3165827) entitled:
"mounting ext3 shows/hides files/dirs intermittently"
and have since settled on using the sync option in the mount command as follows:
# mount -v -t ext3 -o sync /dev/sdb2 /tmp/disks-conf-sdb2

Since my FC3 RH Linux disk is not booted up and running and its filesystem is journaled while the UU1.4 Live CD is not journaled (running in RAM), it was probably likely that the state of the FC3 filesystem was out-of-phase when the sync option is not used.

I still don't understand why Ubuntu 6.0.6 LTS (Dapper Drake) Lice CD worked and UU1.4 Live CD did not - perhaps filesystem differences I suppose.  That is the great unknown!

-- Tom

---

### Post by jfg69 on 2007-08-10
Update: After modifying  /etc/network/interfaces file, i was able to finally connect, but the connection was painfully slow.

Turns out I was *still* connected to the "other"  network. I went back to network settings and figured out that I was in ASCII mode as opposed to hex for my WEP key. A flip of the switch and it found my router and I have been online since, through a few reboots as well.

Apt-get updated and upgraded, ENVY'd the NV drivers, all seems good. Had 1 problem with screen where fonts got all weird, reboot fixed that.

Trying to get Beryl up and running, all went well till it couldnt find the:  
   svn://svn.beryl-project.org/beryl/trunk/ beryl   It just times out repeatedly.
Maybe a look at Compiz-Fusion is a better bet?

---

### Post by jrickard on 2007-08-10
I just downloaded Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 and installed it on my system.  By way of criticism, the point at which you do the upgrade script, download flash/java/adobe, and install the video driver was a very confusing thing for me and I've been running Ubuntu for about six months.  I had a failure to load Xorg several times before I got it all worked out.

That said, this is the BEST distro I have ever seen, and your Ultimate is essentially a NEW distro.  Virtually EVERYTHING on my system works for the very first time - webcams, wireless, network, USB, Wacom tablet, wireless keyboard, printers EVERYTHING.  This has never happened in two Fedora releases and two Ubuntu releases.  I always seem to be staggering along with SOME things working, SOME NOT, and most frustrating, as I get the non-working items worked off one by one, some of the things that DO work, quit with an upgrade.

I love Ubuntu but it has been a busybox for me until this Ultimate version.  It has put me out of Windows, but it is a battle with the hardware all the time.  Other than the lack of a basic README for the procedure above, (which I later did find in this forum) this was the most painless installation I've ever done.  When I turned COMPIZ on, I nearly dumped a loaf!  I now have a fully functional, and BEAUTIFUL Ubuntu (sorry, I dumped the blackmagic/voodoo theme - too gothic for me).

In any event, you are THE MAN.  Your written instructions on your website and files are extremely clear, concise and well written.  I was a technical writer for 25 years and actually published an Internet magazine titled Boardwatch about 150 years ago.  I think you could unseat Microsoft all by yourself. 

Who are you and what do you need to continue this?

Jack Rickard
[email]mjrickard@gmail.com[/email]

---

> With regard to post #372,376,380:  I posted a thread in the General Help support forum: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3165827#post3165827](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3165827#post3165827) entitled:
"mounting ext3 shows/hides files/dirs intermittently"
and have since settled on using the sync option in the mount command as follows:
# mount -v -t ext3 -o sync /dev/sdb2 /tmp/disks-conf-sdb2

Since my FC3 RH Linux disk is not booted up and running and its filesystem is journaled while the UU1.4 Live CD is not journaled (running in RAM), it was probably likely that the state of the FC3 filesystem was out-of-phase when the sync option is not used.

I still don't understand why Ubuntu 6.0.6 LTS (Dapper Drake) Lice CD worked and UU1.4 Live CD did not - perhaps filesystem differences I suppose.  That is the great unknown!

My, my I know quite a bit about linux but you take the cake when it comes to filesystems, been with the *nix community for some time I assume.  I just recently learned XFS filesystems get fragmented ( I have 2 320GB drives XFS)  .  My favorite filesystem is XFS @ least until I found that out.  EXT2 and EXT3 waste a ton of space when partitioned out to utilized the full 320GB, designed for smaller partitions, Reiser while a great filesystem is slow to mount when booting takes 22 secs per drive, XFS almost instant and have never lost a drive.

I don't mount my hard disks from the live DVD / CD, so didn't realize that was a problem, I am unsure if it is a change between dapper & feisty, or edgy for that matter, I am fairly certain it is not a "Ultimate" issue.

Beryl I do believe has shut down SVN build to the public however, [Treviño’s Ubuntu Repository]("http://3v1n0.tuxfamily.org/"), hosts a daily build of beryl.  In case for some reason you don't want to use compiz fusion, which I also believe he hosts daily builds of.  Best of luck.

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-10
> **jrickard said:**
> I just downloaded Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 and installed it on my system.  By way of criticism, the point at which you do the upgrade script, download flash/java/adobe, and install the video driver was a very confusing thing for me and I've been running Ubuntu for about six months.  I had a failure to load Xorg several times before I got it all worked out.

That said, this is the BEST distro I have ever seen, and your Ultimate is essentially a NEW distro.  Virtually EVERYTHING on my system works for the very first time - webcams, wireless, network, USB, Wacom tablet, wireless keyboard, printers EVERYTHING.  This has never happened in two Fedora releases and two Ubuntu releases.  I always seem to be staggering along with SOME things working, SOME NOT, and most frustrating, as I get the non-working items worked off one by one, some of the things that DO work, quit with an upgrade.

I love Ubuntu but it has been a busybox for me until this Ultimate version.  It has put me out of Windows, but it is a battle with the hardware all the time.  Other than the lack of a basic README for the procedure above, (which I later did find in this forum) this was the most painless installation I've ever done.  When I turned COMPIZ on, I nearly dumped a loaf!  I now have a fully functional, and BEAUTIFUL Ubuntu (sorry, I dumped the blackmagic/voodoo theme - too gothic for me).

In any event, you are THE MAN.  Your written instructions on your website and files are extremely clear, concise and well written.  I was a technical writer for 25 years and actually published an Internet magazine titled Boardwatch about 150 years ago.  I think you could unseat Microsoft all by yourself. 

Who are you and what do you need to continue this?

Jack Rickard
[email]mjrickard@gmail.com[/email]

I have had alot of people thank me, and appreciate each and everyone of them taking the time to do so.  This is probably one of the best thanks I have seen, and once again appreciate it.  Envy is just a tool to assist with video driver installation, many have problems, I being one; others having success.  To be honest I wish I could just integrate the drivers on to the DVD, so the end user would not have to deal with it, but I'm fairly sure NVidia, ATI etc. would be beating on my door ;)

I prefer TheeMahn, however my real name can be found by:whois ubuntusoftware.info in a terminal, I am going to continue the project and do not want the end user contributing anything, but maybe some of their time and that is optional.

Thanks again,

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-10
> **jfg69 said:**
> Well, I tried to get Compiz working, got the manager up etc... cant figure out for the life of me how to get the cube on the desktop. Also has my window settings all screwy, no max/min/close graphics in the upper right corners on any apps anymore.

Can anyone recommend a good tutorial on the setup and use of Compiz, or if need be - Beryl?

I'm gonna try and get that repo into the apt-get list and see if I cant get Beryl up and running.

Thanks for listening!

I assume you run an NVidia video card...

edit your xorg:

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

goto the section screen and add the following:
```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
```

should look similar to this:
```
Section "Screen"
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
```

save exit ctrl-alt-backspace and problems with title bar min / max should be gone.

to spin the cube ctrl-alt left / right arrow or hold ctrl alt left click and rotate.

Let me know if you need further assistance.

Sorry for attaching shots to your post ;)

---

### Post by lotuseclat79 on 2007-08-10
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> My, my I know quite a bit about linux but you take the cake when it comes to filesystems, been with the *nix community for some time I assume.  I just recently learned XFS filesystems get fragmented ( I have 2 320GB drives XFS)  .  My favorite filesystem is XFS @ least until I found that out.  EXT2 and EXT3 waste a ton of space when partitioned out to utilized the full 320GB, designed for smaller partitions, Reiser while a great filesystem is slow to mount when booting takes 22 secs per drive, XFS almost instant and have never lost a drive.

I don't mount my hard disks from the live DVD / CD, so didn't realize that was a problem, I am unsure if it is a change between dapper & feisty, or edgy for that matter, I am fairly certain it is not a "Ultimate" issue.
Hi,

Right you are - I started using Unix on a PDP-11 in the early 80s. I've been working as a system software engineer for a number of years now.  I didn't think it was a problem with UU1.4, just that I may have been a little too trusting in how I did the mount command until the problem started to happen.

When I get a faster connection than dialup I'll remember this problem and take a look at the source code in the filesystem to see what the differences are from as you say dapper-edgy-fiesty.

Thanks for your comment,

-- Tom

Most likely is screwed it failed when I uploaded it @ 1:17 this afternoon I had it resume only a few 100K bytes to go evidently they CRC check when uploading via FTP, the hosting company that is and wont allow download via http until meets CRC until fixed.  I will upload it 1000 times if I have to until this happens.  It is hosted on a linux server so size is not a problem I know FAT32 has limitations, I see can see you downloaded it from the [screenshot]("http://ubuntuforums.org/attachment.php?attachmentid=40453&d=1186878197") in windows, perhaps I should ask what file system you use in windows?  I'll download it to see if this is the issue.

And based upon that I may rebuild it to keep it under 4GB (by byte size), I don't want windows users coming in here and complaining a bad download because there O/S is limited...

Let me look up the limitation on fat32 is 4,000,000,000 bytes or actually 4GB.  If the limitation is 4,000,000,000 bytes you can download it all day even though it is good will fail on a windows machine using FAT32, NTFS has this limitation somewhat removed, every filesystem I know of in *nix minus UFS(which is CD based) can support files larger.  This will be a huge issue I don't want to deal with.  I will be back with the answer.

Funny a search for [file system limitations]("http://www.google.com/search?q=file+system+limitations&sourceid=navclient-ff&ie=UTF-8&rlz=1B3GGGL_enUS235&aq=t") M$ comes up #1 ;)  Even though the actual size is 3.9GB goes over 4,000,000,000 bytes.  Many companies call that 4 GB (especially hard disk manufacturers)

Damn even [NTFS]("http://www.discinterchange.com/TechTalk_large_files_.html") has low limitation on file size.

Quoted from that page:"Files up to 4 GB are accessible in all modes with normal I/O calls to the OS; files over 4 GB may be accessible via normal I/O calls; files to about 90 GB are accessible via DLL calls; larger files require raw disk I/O.  See discussion under "Maximum OS File Size", above."

Start over no more then 4,000,000,000 bytes.

Amazes me the plague of this issue in [windows]("http://support.microsoft.com/kb/237740").

When my download completes, remember I am uploading it again, so will take some time.  I will CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) it with MD5SUM and if it passes then all linux users can download it however, I will not post it for any windows user and will rebuild gamer again (a ubuntforums exclusive) not linked anywhere within my site and will be clear about it).  I will not have as said above, have people coming down on me for something I have no control over.

### Post by bryan4134 on 2007-08-12
OK - after many attempts to get Ultimate 1.4 running on an iMAC under Parallels, I have finally succeeded. It appears that there are three things that make the difference. Under Parallels I set the virtual machine memory to 512MB and Video to 32MB.  When the Live Disk boots up there is a menu of options: before hitting Start Ubuntu (or whatever the first option is) press F4 and set the screen resolution - I used 1024x768 (16) - then sit back and wait for it to load.  The actual installation to the hard disk was a bit tricky - I had to try a few times before it actually installed.  The main thing is to keep your hand off the escape button when it looks as though the machine has hung - I went away and left the machine for an hour or so and when I came back it had installed.

Well worth the wait!!

Mine is installed on iMAC 24inch Intel with 2 MB RAM.

Bryan

---

Welcome, I'm only a few steps ahead of you in joining the ranks of *nix users. Try opening a terminal window and typing
```
compiz --replace
```
then hold down ctrl+alt and hit the down, left or right arrow keys.

 .. and following the instructions above I get

jeremy@laptop:~$ compiz --replace

I am going to buck the trend and completely reinstall XP (no not even dual boot) as - windowblinds works - printers install easily - root / file permissions are point and click - workgroups and computers are visible and browsable across my LAN without too much effort  - I can set universal permissions across work groups and I don't have to code individual conf. files to get stuff going and the list goes on.

Unlike LINUX there isn't a flavour of XP for each day of the week, even though there are some not too legal tweeked versions to suite gamers; but all in all it is the same basic structure.

This Ubuntu Linux OS has great potential but lacks refinement and 10 out of 10 to TheMann for this excellent Ultimate distro which goes a fare way to addressing some Windoz crossover wannabees like me - but as a whole its just too frustrating to get the basics running when I have one laptop installed and running and one Celeron/ASUS based PC that wont even run basic Ubuntu but doesn't complain about Sabayon.

I will be back but not until someone comes up with WINUX - LOL (which is not a virus or a bootloader)

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-14
I am sorry I have not been in here to answer questions / help you all, I have been busy writing a howto: Super Tweaking Ultimate copied and pasted from the page :)  2 days in the writing, this is no small document and is a great read, alot of blood sweat and my tears has went into the writing of it.  It is written specifically with Ubuntu Ultimate in mind, however I see no reason it can't be used for feisty as well.  I am not going to currently post a link to it, as it is being reviewed by Tom, boy isn't he lucky ;)

The current subjects within table of contents:
```
Tweaking Ultimate:

    * Disclaimer
    * Services
    * Start-up applications
    * File systems
    * Tweaks you can really feel.


```
Depending upon the use of the document number of hits = demand, I may further expand the document to include further tweaks I have learned.  I hope people don't just use the tweaks you can feel, otherwise I have wasted a bunch of my time, I really hope you like the page as much as I did making it.

Once it has been reviewed I will post it on the main [thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3165721#post3165721"), as well as my howtos [page]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/ubuntu_howtos.html").

Enjoy!!!

TheeMahn

Your problem with compiz was most likely a simple fix; simple to me anyways, people do tend to get a bit scared when the terminal comes open to preform a task when they live in a 100% GUI world of windows, I do want to inform you windows was not always that way, it was built on dos (virtually same thing as a terminal), back in the day.  They do make a knock off of linux that "acts" just like windows it is called Lindows made by Linspire and runs both linux and windows software.

Do I care if you prefer Sabayon or Lindows over Ultimate?  Absolutely not... All I care is to see is you prefer a *nix over windows ;)  Eventually as a sum this will happen.  Here is a piece of thought for you, almost all webpages you view on the entire Internet are hosted from a *nix server, should tell you a little about security as well as performance.

The bottom line is, at least you tried and I think that is great not long ago a few years I personally did exactly what you did, and said piss on this, after advancements now spend 99% of my day in *nix, the learning curve will go away.  I personally intend to help that movement by killing the terminal, all in due time.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-14
> **ExXxTaZi said:**
> Hello,i want to install ubuntu ultimate gamers but its more than 1000 mb...how i can burn him?:P
i need a 3 gb disk?!:guitar:

All is not lost for you, if you do not have a DVD burner, a [CD version]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_CD/") is available and will download games for you, however your net connection will get quite wrapped up in "downloading" the games, gigs and gigs to download.  The Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 Gamers DVD was canceled due to the size of being over 4GB, it is being re-built (to make the size under 4GB, some windows file systems don't like files over 4GB) and is the best option if you have a DVD burner, however this will be some time before released.

If you have a DVD burner and cant wait there is a Gamers edition not as advanced and with less games also available for [download]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/ubuntu_ultimate_gamers/").

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-14
> **ExXxTaZi said:**
> Ok i will buy dvd pack today,but i have some few questions to ask:
1-Which version ubuntu ultimate gamers use?,6 or ubuntu 7?
2-The installing is like the orginal ubuntu install?
3-I saw  screen shoots for "the coding studio" games like cube...There is better games?
I mean enemy territory and Americans army included?
Thats all for now..how you will ans me:popcorn:

The Gamers edition that is currently available for [download]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/ubuntu_ultimate_gamers/") is based on Edgy Eft (6.1), I have made one based on Feisty Fawn (7.04), after uploading decided not to release it due to size restriction in windows (some file systems in windows can not hold a file over 4GB; the release was 4.1, so the only way you could have been able to download it and burn it was in linux, I don't want restrictions on the end user), I have now went back to "the drawing board" and started building a smaller version.

Neither of which of  the two you mentioned will be on the DVD Americas Army is over 800MB by itself, however is installable via the upgrade icon on the desktop, if you can't wait for me to release it; it will do the same from the Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 CD, however it will download the 800MB file from the net, not the disk, as well as all the rest of the games.  It will not have many that I included and now have to rethink that was going to be in the Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 Gamers Edition.

Yes installing is the same.

Yes there is action cube, the list of what was on the last one is that was canceled is [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3134528&postcount=423").

I hope this has not been too confusing for you, if you still have a question please feel free to post.
