### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-14
> **snomann said:**
> Orright -- after three reinstalls and near bleeding fingers and working out what SUDO actually means add to this using the best part of my homes DL limit for the month I need to ask a small handful of questions.
This is based on my Intel Pent 4 3Ghz x 512 Meg ram with ATI 9600 vid card.
1. After the initial install and reboot the install flash/adobe ...  and then the install ATI/Nvidia driver; this runs every time on login.
How do I stop this?
2. After following the instructions for Beryl - and I feel - due to the root being locked - I cannot get the final step to work (cd beryl ... etc) because nothing seems to be written to the root or the home so the console commands just do nothing.
How do I unlock the ROOT so I can make some new files or write files to HDD?
3. This question is beaten to death everywhere on these forums but I never see the right answer. I have seen the videos on YOUTUBE and it can be done, I have SAMBA running to share files off of this machine but I cannot access the files on my WINDOZ machines across my MSHOME network. I get to find the network (WINDOWS) then the workgroup (MSHOME) then zip - no computers no files nothing and the joy of an error stating that there&#8217;s nothing to be found.
How do I get LINUX to play I'll show you mine if I can see yours with windoz? and how do I share the root files or shared directory without needing a damn pass and user to access this computer?
4. WindoZ allows me to login with my default USER/PASS without so much as a how do you do I have have been into the user/group panel and basically allowed all options to my login account.
How do I remove the necessity to use a pass to do nearly anything on this computer or correctly associate the root and personal accounts together and get rid of password?
5. I have 3 networked printers with their own IPs the driver for the Brother went straight in and worked (top job soooo easy) I found a LINUX RPM installer for the driver for the FUJI XEROX but once again I cannot modify any folders or write files below desktop and need to extract some files to the USR level.
??????arrgghh???? LOL
6. IS there a repository for networked scanners? the brother is a Multi Function Unit -fax scan print- but I can&#8217;t seem to find anything except SANE or XSANE
Is this what I need?

Thz -SNO- .. and did you want the web space?

I skipped you right from the beginning ;)  I knew it would take an hour and a day to type everything you ask lol.

1. I wrote a brief [how to]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") to address this issue, I did not however cover ATI driver installation, this is covered on my [website]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/beryl.html#ati").  Remember Envy is just a tool what may work for one may not work for another.  If my website does not help you there are many others within this [forum]("http://ubuntuforums.org/search.php?searchid=25386184").  Please excuse me for not taking more time to address this issue.

2.  Beryl is no longer available to SVN build and when I get time will re-write the how to, cutting edge beryl is available from [Treviño&#8217;s repository]("http://3v1n0.tuxfamily.org/dists/feisty/3v1n0/").

3. I personally have written a [how to]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2994349&postcount=230") in this thread and do not blame you for not finding it this thread is huge.

4. If you want to get rid of it asking you for a password all together (highly unrecommended for security reasons) you can learn about that [here]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu:Feisty#How_to_use_.22sudo.22_without_prompt_for_password_.28not_secure.29").
There is alot of good reading there, a mass volume of information at your fingertips.

5. Have you consdered converting the RPM to a DEB with alien?  Alien is built into Ultimate, so no extra software to install ;)The command to do so is:
```
sudo alien -d  [name of rpm.rpm]
```

6. sane and xsane is devised to handle all scanning devices your millage may vary, what is the exact make and model?

### Post by michaelramm on 2007-08-14
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> I'm sorry I wasn't here to help, before failure, I am glad you didn't give up all hope ;)

Nah, I have learned through many attempts at using linux that these things happens.

I hopped onto the #compiz-fusion IRC channel last night and talked to some folks there while I was reinstalling. It seemed that none of them have any good things to say about Envy installing video drivers. Of course, they tell me this RIGHT AFTER Envy installs my ATI drivers. So another reinstall so that Envy would not install my drivers. The guy said that Envy does not completely install the 3D acceleration for the drivers.

So I started another install at about 11:30pm. I let the Restricted Drivers Manager install the video drivers. I left it at 12:45am running the Upgrade script after installing all of the updates.

So that is where I am now. A fresh new system (again) and I will work on getting Compiz Fusion running tonight. I guess I need to setup the XGL stuff so that I can boot into an XGL Session.

TheeMan: Is there a way to login to a different session from the login screen? I remember that all of the other installs that I have played with, you could choose the session that you booted into (i.e. a different windows manager, or in this case, an XGL session to test settings. If I could have done that, I could have saved myself a reinstall since I would not have had to make logging into XGL the default login method.

Thanks so much for UUE! I really am excited about using it, once I can get CF and AWN setup and running. Then I am off to try to get World of Warcraft running in Wine.

Michael

### Post by michaelramm on 2007-08-14
That, to, is my dilemma. I don't think that it works out of the box.

From what I have seen, you have to initiate an XGL Session to run in. I am not sure what that is exactly, but it has to do with your video card and 3D acceleration.

There is no way (that I have found) for you to change sessions on login (like other distros that I have used). It is something that I have been discussing since page 56 I think, if you want to start there and read.

Hope that this helps,
Michael

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-15
> **frickea86 said:**
> i'm confused, why do you even need XGL, what does it do?  an why are there so many guides that don't even ask you to install it like the one link stated above.

i'm going to try an reinstall XGL and see if i can try configureing it to work correctly.  will post findings soon, thanks for all the help guys...:)

XGL is unnecessary with the introduction of Xorg 7.2.0, maybe it was 7.1.0 can't remember.  I assume you have an ATI card while I am certainly no expert in installing ati drivers (which I believe is the problem you are having) you need to install xorg-driver-fglrx, this can be done from the terminal, I will not try and help you other then to point you in the right direction, if you were to mess it up I am of little use to you no computer in this house uses ATI they are all nvidia (5) computers.  There are many wonderful how tos on the net this is perhaps my [favorite]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu:Feisty").  If it is Beryl or compiz fusion you are looking to try to get to work [here]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu:Feisty#How_to_install_Beryl_.28ATI.29").

If you take the time to learn everything in that guide you will be a pro ;)

### Post by lotuseclat79 on 2007-08-15
> **lotuseclat79 said:**
> Hi,

I am an Ultimate Ubuntu 1.4 Live CD user.  I have a 4-disk system with Red Hat Fedora Core 3 (FC3) permanently installed on one disk that I use to store items that enhance the experience of using UU 1.4.

In the normal course of initializing my use of the Live CD, I make a mount point (as root), then issue a mount command over to my FC3 disk.  I then copy over a shell script that copies into the Live CD environment (in RAM), the items that I may typically use.

Just yesterday, I ran into the situation that the mount command only partially succeeded.  The directory in which I store everything related to UU went missing (at least as far as I could see). A minor panic could have ensued, but I normally don't panic until I am able to verify whether something is actually lost or not.  Lucky I back things up!

Upon rebooting my system to FC3, the missing directory was really there with all of the expected contents.  Since it looked like UU1.4 was not going to be able to successfully mount the directory on disk, I switched from UU 1.4  to Ubuntu 6.0.6 LTS Live CD.  The result was that I was able to mount the directory, and I connected to the Internet using U 6.0.6 LTS.

Today, UU1.4 was able to successfully mount and access the data that went missing yesterday.

What I would like to know is whether the partial mount was probably caused more by the number of times my system was booted via Live CD usage without booting from the FC3 disk in the interim and because I have saved a lot to the disk, or may have some other cause?

Is it likely that I may need to issue a sync command (as root) in the mounted partition after saving data before I exit the Live CD?  I suspect that I may need to do this as a standard precautionary measure to avoid this occurence in the future.  

Your thoughts on this issue please.

Tia,

-- Tom
Hi,

Well, this AM the issue happened again.  With a little more digging and experimentation, I've concluded that since today's episode occured probably because I had mounted the hard drive with the FC3 ext3 filesystem some 46 times without an fsck being run against it - i.e. the superblock was probably not clean, I simply rebooted the FC3 Red Hat Linux and it performed an fsck during the boot sequence.  I usually do something else during booting, and had not observed this - but, should have known it anyway.

Then and probably only then was I able to boot up with UU1.4 and mount the hard drive to access my initialization scripts.

I suppose I could cut a CD with my scripts, but nah - why bother unless the disk is on its last legs.  Ah, maybe I'll do it anyway.

I probably only need to do the sync option on the mount command when I am done my seesion and want to save my browser environment and any files I have downloaded - however, I do not think that will avoid the problem in the future.

I specifically did not use the Ubuntu 6.0.6 LTS Dapper Drake immediately after the issue reoccured.  I will be sure to do this the next time the issue occurs to see if I can again access the disk without the issue occuring.  I am not exactly sure what that would mean, simply because I am now fairly certain that what causes the condition is not Ubuntu related, but a state of the filesystem on the FC3 disk without being fsck'd over so many mounts.

If the problem really occurs only due to an fsck not being run after so many mounts and the Journalling being out-of-phase, then Dapper should not be able to access the directory/files where I keep my scripts.  We shall see the next time this issue occurs.

Normally, I don't think this issue occurs too often as systems that are mounted are being run on a Lan which I don't have, and then they would have a booted up OS running.  My situation is a little different in terms of running just a Live CD environment where the other disks in my system are merely spinning and not running a booted OS when power is applied to my dssktop.

-- Tom

Quote:
Originally Posted by Detox06
Hey. First off, just let me say I lvoe your work. I have just recently (Like a year ago maybe) discovered it, and have been using the Gamers Ed. since then. I just got the 1.4 DVD and am going to install it after I get off work tonight.

I just had a couple questions. I know Linux is GPL and I can do whatever I want with it, and give it out and all that other fun stuff. However, I am starting a custom computer company, and would like your blessing to be able to install UUE as an option on the computers I sell.

Also, how often do you try to upload new versions? (Not including gamers eds./partials.)

Finally. Is it possible that you would be able to get a 1.4CD install that contains the UUE DVD wallpaper, as well as one that installs KDE by default? I am willing to pay you for your work and for use of your OS once my systems start selling, and would greatly appreciate your help.

Thanks.
-Gary
I made my first release Dec. 18th of 2006 called "Ubuntu Christmas Edition", bought the domain and hosting to help others for this reason, less then a year ago. I was wet behind the ears and included everything one could possibly ask for adobe this adobe that, nvidia, ati etc. A real Christmas present

You are not the first that has asked me to make a "custom version" and offer to pay me. Yes you can put any version I make & sell on a computer, I do however want to forewarn you just installing the drivers for the end user complicates things w/o the end user accepting the license agreement, same using any *nix and is the reason I deliberately write scripts to make you do so as the end user, I wish I could do absolutely everything for you, as well as every other *nix user for that matter. While I am sure you could get away with doing such for them, I do want you to understand it is wrong and can be penalized for doing such (sued etc.).

KDE will not be introduced until 1.5 (Feisty Fawn based), just to let you know I have introduced a new version nothing over 30 days as of recent and as I type this I am uploading, I know you said not gamers, but that is what I am uploading. I am here for everyone not just one person. I apologize, I can not be bought, don't take that hard. I will help you any way I can, minus custom builds; this takes time away from helping others.

I am really glad you like my works, and wish you the best of luck in selling computers, I hope I have not offended you in anyway.

Best regards,

TheeMahn

I am posting this for all others to see, I hope it does not bother you, it is not the first time I have been asked, and would like it to stop, please don't take it to heart.

EDIT: If it bothers you I will remove the entire post...  I should have consulted with you first.

### Post by kbuzzard on 2007-08-16
I apologize 1st as I haven't figured this forum out but I "MUST" congratulate and thank you for all your efforts,time and persistance in making the UU series. I have e-mailed you about ATI AIW support and frankly have spent DAYS with XP trying to keep it working correctly with that. Thank you for your response on that issue and for those who want TV on their UU OS I suggest you find a "Leadtek WinFast TV2000 XP RM (or EQ) card which works with 98/98SE/ME/W2K/XP's/Vista/ and MOST importantly Linux !!. Some googling will need to be done but by using TVtime it is damn near out-of -the-box. NOTE - Be sure it has the bt878 chipset ****. All that aside, your UU1.4 DVD is on 3 of my PC's and has spurred me to learn Linux by using A/V sources and I will now stop using XP on a day to day basis. By google I have my UU in auto login (Home user) and with the exception of TV Capture and my Driving simulator games I can HONESTLY say I am a very happy UU user, Please keep up the good work, I wish I had your gift with Linux. You are in fact "THE MAN", your timing is so good, I can't imagine what I would do if I had no other option but to use  "VISTA - You got to be kidding ME...
THANK YOU,THANK YOU,THANK YOU.      kbuzzard - Buford,Georgia.   btw, I have tried almost every Linux distro out there since jan 2007 and with exeption of GeexBox for A/V use only, UU 1.4 DVD ROCKS !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I just updated the [howto]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") to include editing of xorg, this does not help you as you use ati, sorry I assumed you were running nvidia, I suggest a look [here]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu:Feisty#Install_Beryl_using_proprietary_FGLRX_drivers_from_ATI") as far as your driver is concerned, Envy is just a tool some people have success with but not all.. manager "Synaptic" or run "sudo apt-get install -f" in a terminal to fix this issue at first.

problem.  The built in disk checker will fail on any Ultimate as it compares values to the original feisty and not ultimate, when I said MD5SUM check that is the .iso you downloaded, this can be done from a terminal using the following command:
```
md5sum ubuntu-ultimate-1.3-gamers.iso
```

It should return a value exactly as below:
```
f80f6adb2d97432a7e2353ab2880433e
```

Let me know if you have any further problems.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-16
> **ExXxTaZi said:**
> Lol i am stuck in the 
"gksudo wget [http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg](http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg) -O- | sudo apt-key add -"
i need to add it to the "third party app"in software soruces buthe dont  let me press ok><
the second command is giving me "file not found",as you can see I am 99% noob to linux even if i am working with him allmost month!
I know just the basic commands like sudo apt-get ...its ok or I need to just leave linux?:|
When you used linux in your first month,you knew more than this?
I am 15 years old by the way,its to young for linux?](*,)](*,)

never too young to learn ;) replace the first gksudo with sudo as follows:
```
sudo wget http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg -O- | sudo apt-key add -
```

Should look as follows:
```
shadymahn@SledgeHammer:~$ sudo wget http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg -O- | sudo apt-key add -
--21:41:28--  http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg
           => `-'
Resolving ubuntu.beryl-project.org... 195.114.19.35, 208.113.193.9, 80.77.247.17, ...
Connecting to ubuntu.beryl-project.org|195.114.19.35|:80... connected.
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK
Length: 2,415 (2.4K) [application/octet-stream]

100%[====================================>] 2,415         --.--K/s             

21:41:28 (2.74 MB/s) - `-' saved [2415/2415]

OK
shadymahn@SledgeHammer:~$ 

```

The difference between sudo and gksudo is gksudo is the graphical front end to sudo (terminal based), the graphical portion is not coming up since it was executed from a terminal.

### Post by michaelramm on 2007-08-17
Well, I have found out that it is probably my motherboard that is the problem. I have an nForce2 [Asus A7N8X-E Deluxe]("http://www.xbitlabs.com/articles/mainboards/display/asus-a7n8xe-deluxe.html") and I saw on the [ATI Linux Wiki]("http://pages.citebite.com/m2v0l6l0w2awq") that there are possible issue with the nForce3 mobo that were in production.

So, could the same issue be raised with the nForce2 mobo, like the one that I have. Sure, I would think so.

So I am chalking up the incompatibility of my Radeon x1600 Pro with XGL to the motherboard.

Now, do I find another mobo that supports my RAM and Processor, or buy another video card?

It will probably be easier to get another video card, but will I have the same kind of issue with a nVidia card? I would hope that an nVidia-based motherboard and an nVidia video card would work together, but you never know.

Michael

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-17
> **michaelramm said:**
> Well, I have found out that it is probably my motherboard that is the problem. I have an nForce2 Asus A7N8X-E Deluxe and I saw on the ATI Linux Wiki that there are possible issue with the nForce3 mobo that were in production.

So, could the same issue be raised with the nForce2 mobo, like the one that I have. Sure, I would think so.

So I am chalking up the incompatibility of my Radeon x1600 Pro with XGL to the motherboard.

Now, do I find another mobo that supports my RAM and Processor, or buy another video card?

It will probably be easier to get another video card, but will I have the same kind of issue with a nVidia card? I would hope that an nVidia-based motherboard and an nVidia video card would work together, but you never know.

Michael

I see no problem with your mainboard and actually has high reviews with *nix however, your [Video card]("http://www.leenooks.com/page/show/ATI%20Radeon%20x1600%20PRO%20PCIE%20(RV%20530)") a different ball of wax.  Note the topic:  Hardware That Doesn't Work with Linux.  Probably why I have 5 computers 0 with ATI.  When I build a computer for someone NVidia is going it even if they buy a low end computer, sorry to tell you that.  I have in all my time only seen 1 NVidia video card that has problems with *nix and that is a 5200 AGP but is fixable by adding option AGP "true" to the xorg.  No I don't work for NVidia, sure sounds like I endorse their products ;)

> I see no problem with your mainboard and actually has high reviews with *nix however, your [Video card]("http://www.leenooks.com/page/show/ATI%20Radeon%20x1600%20PRO%20PCIE%20(RV%20530)") a different ball of wax.  Note the topic:  Hardware That Doesn't Work with Linux.  Probably why I have 5 computers 0 with ATI.  When I build a computer for someone NVidia is going it even if they buy a low end computer, sorry to tell you that.  I have in all my time only seen 1 NVidia video card that has problems with *nix and that is a 5200 AGP but is fixable by adding option AGP "true" to the xorg.  No I don't work for NVidia, sure sounds like I endorse their products ;)

I forgot to copy the links in my reposting of my findings.

[QUOTE=ATI Linux Wiki]For nForce3 based motherboards: if the install seems to go well, but fglrx still fails and $ fglrxinfo tells you you're still running Mesa, there may be a problem with the nVidia AGP-PCI Bridge module's compatibility with your BIOS (or vice versa, I haven't yet investigated).[/QUOTE]

I have an nForce2 board and I think that there is a strong possibility that is the problem. That exact thing was happening to me. Everything installed fine, then it all said Mesa 3D for the OpenGL stuff.

I also forgot to mention that I put the x1600 into another system and installed a vanilla system on it and it was able to login to XGL sessions and run Compiz Fusion and Avant (albeit slowly cause it is a Celeron 1.2GHz with 512MB RAM). I am going to reformat that system with UUE and see if it runs. If it does not, then I will try my main system with the x1600 and see if it runs.

I need to make sure that it is not UUE that is the problem, since I was able to make it work with a vanilla install.

Michael

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-17
> **Detox06 said:**
> Just a random hope... I've asked before, and I'm just hoping maybe the anser will change at some point, but as far as anyone knows, there is absolutely no way to get DVD playback without the internet, correct? (No packages with the Libdvd codecs?)

Does this cover it, if so it is integraded in the Gamers (I should specify 1.4 Gamers):
```
library for reading DVDs
libdvdread provides the functionality that is required to access many DVDs. It
parses IFO files, reads NAV-blocks, and performs CSS authentication and
descrambling.

libdvdread currently uses libdl to dynamically probe for libdvdcss at runtime.
If found, libdvdcss will be used to decrypt sections of the DVD as necessary.
```

However not included:

```
Library for accessing DVDs like block device usind deCSS if needed
libdvdcss is a simple library designed for accessing DVDs like a block device
without having to bother about the decryption. The important features are:
 * Portability. Currently supported platforms are GNU/Linux, FreeBSD, NetBSD,
   OpenBSD, BSD/OS, BeOS, Windows 95/98, Windows NT/2000, MacOS X, Solaris,
   and HP-UX.
 * Simplicity. There are currently 7 functions in the API, and we intend to
   keep this number low.
 * Freedom. libdvdcss is released under the General Public License, ensuring
   it will stay free, and used only for free software products.
 * Just better. Unlike most similar projects, libdvdcss doesn't require the
   region of your drive to be set.

This package contains the libdvdcss2 runtime library.

Homepage: http://www.videolan.org/libdvdcss

This is in Medibuntu as it violates patents.
```

Read the last line that is why it is not included...

Quite the list one starts to realize a huge offering...  Had to modify it many times, due to constraints but gives a general idea.  I am attaching the results of dpkg -l >software.txt from 1.4 gamers in case you want to see the end results.

This should tell you something...

uug1.4gamers.txt:
Your file of 268.6 KB bytes exceeds the forum's limit of 19.5 KB for this filetype.  ;)

I suppose this is truly the way to look at it its 4GB 1.4 is 1.9GB I'm sure a great portion is games, but that was not the only thing I added.  It will eat 8+ gig of your drive when installed.  in my case 10 see shot.

Damn now that I look @ that looks like time to buy another drive, they make 1 TB drives now, I burn up space quick building it, not to mention the folders I have for different repos.

Well the cowboy made the wallpaper I personally like it and none of the shots I've shown recently has  got the TheeMahn on it :)  I could spend 1 week with you and I do feel this to be correct You would know programming in short order.  I had a "buddy" that re-wrote code I wrote in windows world, heavy NTL utilizing the API and replaced my name with his, I suppose did upset me I would not have brought it up.

I could have you writing Advanced Applications as said in short order, I have taught many but one pre-requisite is smart, I don't like to repeat myself.  I don't mind here I can point to what I have posted, funny isn't it.   Ultimately I am here for the new guy.  I am not here to teach people to program, if you want to learn I will have you speaking bash within less then a day... Where do go from here?  The API in windows world is the same as C.  I think all programming should take a step back to ASM, I did used to poke, peek values memory was limited then... sys 49152 is hex for C000, how do I know that I lived that world.

Forgive me for not addressing the question asked:  Aero (checkered), is the theme or mine Ultimate II if it has grey, at least for now, have much work ahead of me.  Allow me to bust it all down so you start to feel it 0 off 1 on, ASM is as close as you can touch that...  A computer is a bunch of switches, woot step to the world of electronics, PNP NPN transistor, did I mention I know electronics?  integrate it on a chip, live the life.  Supercomputers is just around the corner, not that it is not there now.  I suppose I can talk about this now, I have been out of the military greater then 10 years, they had when I was in there called JTIDS (joint tactical Identification system) I won't take the time to break that down to you.  Funny now you see it on TV.  I really wonder what they have now...

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-18
> **redmonster13 said:**
> I just installed ultimate and I love it.

I would however like to know how to get the splash screen back to the one that it was using when it first started loading. I am talking about the all grey one.

Available splash screens can be found by:  System>>Preferences>>Splash Screen

If it is not there what version do you run?  I will upload it and provide a link.  Unless you are talking USplash (the animated one while booting).  I have many available [here]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/sum.html").  Look at the bottom of the page other resources.  Then use SUM System>>Administration>>StartUp-Manager goto appearance tab, down at the bottom it says USplash themes click manage themes, add+ select the file you downloaded, click close. in the same place (usplash themes) select the theme and click close.  Next reboot it will be there.  As a side note you can increase the resolution and color as well as displaying text while it boots see screenshots.  Usplash from 1.4 Gamers has not yet been posted, however is attached to this post.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-20
> **michaelramm said:**
> I did the Full Ubuntu Upgrades that were available from Update Manager.

Is there something that I am missing, or is that what you were talking about?

Michael

O.K. when you installed the driver, after which and before reboot you let the upgrades complete be it from the upgrade script or be it from the update manager.  You should before even installing the driver let the upgrades complete which one of which is a kernel upgrade, reboot then install the driver.  When you do it the way in which you did, try this hit the esc. key on boot-up and select the older kernel and see if your drivers work then.  If they do you will have to purge the drivers from the kernel modify your xorg and go though a ton of problems to get them into the new kernel.  I have written a short [how to on how I install UU]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472"), ignore the Nvidia & xorg section.

Ill, just paste it here...
1. Install UUE DVD
2. initial bootscript I install acro read and flash no video drivers yet.
3. Run upgrade script, let it complete this upgrades the kernel - still no video drivers installed.
4. reboot
5. Install video drivers

### Post by michaelramm on 2007-08-21
> **bigbrovar said:**
> i have had my Good times but My linux adventure is rapidly becoming a nightmare...i hve formated my system 3 times bcus of issue that i could not resolve
and whenever i post them in the community nobody rely me...first it was funny annoying startups...i posted the issue..didn't get any help....i had to format my system and install feisty again...then my system started freezing and hanging after installing compiz fusion...again posted..again no help was giving i had no other option than to reformat (after hours of setting up the system and downloading updates)..i decided to  install Ubuntu again but this time UU. -everything within the space of 24 hours-..now i cant get compiz fusion to work cus when i lunch it i get windows without border lines and Firefox start freezing...again i have come to the community and again  (this is my forth post here) no body has offered any help..i have done soo much given soo much just to have ubuntu on my system working as smoothly as the vista that came with it but which i hardly use anymore..its 2 am in my country i have been up all night checkin to see if my post have been replied..am still waiting....:(

I hate to say this, but...

It is time that you gave up on ubuntu.

You are taking this way too seriously. I have had to reinstall my system 5 times in the last week troubleshooting CF/UUE errors (it would not run on mine either). I was about to buy a new video card so that I could run Compiz Fusion. Then I installed regular Feisty Fawn and i was able to get compiz fusion to work. So, **suggestion #1, install regular ubuntu feisty fawn and see if you can get it to work. **

It really is not up to TheeMahn to support Compiz Fusion in UUE. There are DOZENS of other threads that deal with CF that you should be reading. Have you looked at the [Compiz Fusion forums]("http://forum.compiz-fusion.org/")? They have CF experts roaming around in there, and they may be able to help you faster. Have you looked at the Compiz Fusion IRC Channel [#compiz-fusion on irc.freenode.net]? I got tons of help when I was in there troubleshooting my problems. And it is live chat, so it is instantaneous. Maybe that Intel card will not support CF. ** Suggestion #2, look for more avenues of support and official avenues of support.**

TheeMahn is just a guy who added a bunch of useful software to Feisty Fawn. He does not work for ubuntu, or Compiz Fusion. He tries to help when he can, but he really has other worries at the moment, like not having to pay many $$$ for bandwidth.

I know that you are frustrated. I was too when I first started learning linux. It is a bear to get a handle on. But ubuntu really has brought linux to the mainstream, but there is still a LOT of knowledge required. Everything is not plug and play like Windows. It takes tweaking and PATIENCE to learn it. I have given up on Linux at least 6 times in the past 8 years, but with Ubuntu, I am here to stay and there is no Windows on the machine that I am typing on right now.

During my troubleshooting, I would just understand that a reinstall might be necessary. If it is, no big deal, it is another 2 hrs of my life.

You are not alone on these forums, but you have to explain EXACTLY what is happening on your system. Linux on EVERY SINGLE MACHINE is different. We need to know what you did, and what happened and why you think that it is not working correctly. Break up your posts so that they are not one big paragraph (people like me will automatically skip over that). 

I hope that helps you so that you can help us help you. (Man, that was confusing!)

Michael

---

### Post by Detox06 on 2007-08-21
BigBrovar: 

I know when I started using Linux I couldn't get anything to work. And it was made all the worse because I was using Fedora 4 with no wireless support and I couldn't stretch an ethernet cable to my machine. I had absolutely nothing working, and no one to help me. 

That kinda killed my interest in Linux for a while, and then a couple months ago I started hearing about this 'Ubuntu' thing. Sure, it still wasn't great, but there was enough of a support base that I could get help with what I needed. Now, I can most minor things on my own. You just need to persevere. Try Ubuntu 7.04, like Micheal said. Or 7.10 when it comes out in October. See if that fixes the problem. 

Gary


On an unrelated note, here's a story for you all. Not looking for sympathy, just showcasing how even someone as perfect as me can make dumb mistakes. lol. 

So I have been using Ubuntu as my only OS for about 6 months now. UU 1.3 Gamers. A friend of mine needed a favor from me last night, to look at some stuff in Vista. So, I used Gparted, made an NTFS space, and tried to install Vista. The disc I have isn't bootable. =( So, Install XP. Now XP and Ubuntu are working perfectly. Put in the Vista disc. Clean install. Good. Boots into Vista. Now go to set the Ubuntu partition to bootable. Reboot. No OS found. =( Erase the NTFS partition. Still no NTFS. *grrr* Completely unformat the whole drive with livecd. Make new NTFS partition. Install XP. Install Vista. Install UUE 1.4. Boot. works. No sound in Vista. Mount my Music and Movies drives in UUE. After 6 hours, I'm finally back in business with a dual boot system. 

Moral of the story? Be patient. Learn from your mistakes. My night was crap. But I learned my lesson from it. And its a good thing I had my main HDD all backed up. I only erased my main drive like 3 times. Heh. 

Linux is great. Especially the UUE. But it takes patience. And a willingness to fail a couple times. Buy an external drive and back up all your data. Then you can break your system all you want. 

=)

i have bben to many forums and i have posts pending there too..i just felt the since theeman built UU is might be able to shed on light on how i can go about it..

I had the same problem, but I am running nVidia card. I forgot to uninstalll Compiz, and went to Add/Remove and installed Beryl Manager and Beryl setting Manager.  Compiz started working with windows with borders. I click on the Beryl Manager>select window manager and it gives me a selection of, Beryl, Compiz, and Metacity. This is not by the books at all but I haven't had any Problems or crashes doing it this way.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-22
> **TormentedTeddy said:**
> First started with 1.1 & .3 versions later, U.U. still runs smoothly! Thanks for bringing UU into the mix, TheeMahn2003! Greatly appreciating your continued time & hard work! Hoping to see a donation link one day though. ;)

Even had wireless working while couldn't get working in Windows or vanilla *ubuntu.

Happily converted from KDE to Gnome (:

Thanks, I am really glad to hear that.  Even though I am a bit short on cash and had to shut down 2 of the 3 servers temporarily, I will not accept a donation.  I do not feel it is your job to pay.  The hosting fees are typically paid by ads on the website.  Unfortunately Google does not send these checks out until the 25th of the month, arriving close to the first.  I had to prematurely shut down the download server because it has a 2 Terabyte limit on bandwidth and had used over 5 TB at a cost of $1.49 per gig over 2TB would have cost me literally $1000's same situation with the repo.

To look at it from the bright side, the mirror is still up and 42 people are downloading it as I type this with a 100 user limit.  He has sent out as of just 1.4 DVD I am assuming 4020.82GiB (4TB). He unlike me has no limits in bandwidth.  He has also offered to provide me space to setup the repo on.  I have to wait until he gets back from Italy.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-22
> **bigbrovar said:**
> i have had my Good times but My linux adventure is rapidly becoming a nightmare...i hve formated my system 3 times bcus of issue that i could not resolve
and whenever i post them in the community nobody rely me...first it was funny annoying startups...i posted the issue..didn't get any help....i had to format my system and install feisty again...then my system started freezing and hanging after installing compiz fusion...again posted..again no help was giving i had no other option than to reformat (after hours of setting up the system and downloading updates)..i decided to  install Ubuntu again but this time UU. -everything within the space of 24 hours-..now i cant get compiz fusion to work cus when i lunch it i get windows without border lines and Firefox start freezing...again i have come to the community and again  (this is my forth post here) no body has offered any help..i have done soo much given soo much just to have ubuntu on my system working as smoothly as the vista that came with it but which i hardly use anymore..its 2 am in my country i have been up all night checkin to see if my post have been replied..am still waiting....:(

I am sorry I can not hang out on the forums waiting for a post all day / night long.  Every post up and until your last 2 to the best of my knowledge I have replied to.  I do spend a lot of time on these forums to do just that.  Please remember I am just 1 Mahn.

Try this and post results:
```
glxinfo | grep direct
glxgears # (leave for 10 or 15 secs)

```

Should be similar to this:
```
shadymahn@SledgeHammer:~$ glxinfo | grep direct
direct rendering: Yes
shadymahn@SledgeHammer:~$ glxgears # (leave for 10 or 15 secs)
16206 frames in 5.0 seconds = 3241.115 FPS
16474 frames in 5.0 seconds = 3294.715 FPS
19388 frames in 5.0 seconds = 3877.460 FPS
X connection to :0.0 broken (explicit kill or server shutdown).
shadymahn@SledgeHammer:~$
```

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-22
> **Detox06 said:**
> BigBrovar: 

I know when I started using Linux I couldn't get anything to work. And it was made all the worse because I was using Fedora 4 with no wireless support and I couldn't stretch an ethernet cable to my machine. I had absolutely nothing working, and no one to help me. 

That kinda killed my interest in Linux for a while, and then a couple months ago I started hearing about this 'Ubuntu' thing. Sure, it still wasn't great, but there was enough of a support base that I could get help with what I needed. Now, I can most minor things on my own. You just need to persevere. Try Ubuntu 7.04, like Micheal said. Or 7.10 when it comes out in October. See if that fixes the problem. 

Gary


On an unrelated note, here's a story for you all. Not looking for sympathy, just showcasing how even someone as perfect as me can make dumb mistakes. lol. 

So I have been using Ubuntu as my only OS for about 6 months now. UU 1.3 Gamers. A friend of mine needed a favor from me last night, to look at some stuff in Vista. So, I used Gparted, made an NTFS space, and tried to install Vista. The disc I have isn't bootable. =( So, Install XP. Now XP and Ubuntu are working perfectly. Put in the Vista disc. Clean install. Good. Boots into Vista. Now go to set the Ubuntu partition to bootable. Reboot. No OS found. =( Erase the NTFS partition. Still no NTFS. *grrr* Completely unformat the whole drive with livecd. Make new NTFS partition. Install XP. Install Vista. Install UUE 1.4. Boot. works. No sound in Vista. Mount my Music and Movies drives in UUE. After 6 hours, I'm finally back in business with a dual boot system. 

Moral of the story? Be patient. Learn from your mistakes. My night was crap. But I learned my lesson from it. And its a good thing I had my main HDD all backed up. I only erased my main drive like 3 times. Heh. 

Linux is great. Especially the UUE. But it takes patience. And a willingness to fail a couple times. Buy an external drive and back up all your data. Then you can break your system all you want. 

=)

I agree with you totally, I can not count how many times I have installed UUE perhaps 1000's as I build / test it.  I can not count how many times I have forgot to do even such as a simple task as to back up my firefox favorites to another drive, or like a huge fool left the ISO I just made in the home folder and lost it.  I certainly have learned from my mistakes one at a time...

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-22
> **nynoah said:**
> so should I just wait then?

yep I am working on a temporary fix for this 9 hours in uploading.

The new repo is added using the following:
```
sudo echo deb http://ubuntusoftware.info feisty all | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list
```

The upgrade script will be updated when I get the chance to reflect these changes, I will re-write it so if it does go down it will look on all 3 servers surely one will be up ;)

### Post by kbuzzard on 2007-08-22
I feel for you TheMan having all these problems, BUT ,the human body is close to the perfect machine and is screws up fairly regularly unless ut most care is observed, especially after mass doses of Brews !!. You will now show me as using UU1.4DVD and in converting to UU from XP,I thought to suggest some things to help fellow XP converters to UU, If they want to convert to Vista, no comment - have fun. I will add some links to use as tutors for new Linux/Ubuntu users like myself to ease transition pains. I use 40gig SATA hdds as I have learned NOT to store data on my OS hdds, I use external usb2 hdd kits so I can clone and swap,replace as XP has taught me SO WELL.            "Killdisk"  [http://www.killdisk.com/downloadfree.htm](http://www.killdisk.com/downloadfree.htm)   cleans hdds to NEW RAW state, great for fresh installs of any OS, formatting does NOT clean that well, you can get floppy or CD boot versions and is EASY to use. If you have XP running on you PC and want to install UU's,  [http://www.howtoforge.com/dual_boot_windows_xp_vista_ubuntu_feisty](http://www.howtoforge.com/dual_boot_windows_xp_vista_ubuntu_feisty)    An excellent HowTo site for any of us. I have follwed this tutorial 4 times without issues with XP !!. If before you do that , you want to CLONE your working XP to another hdd first    [http://www.fssdev.com/products/casper/trial/](http://www.fssdev.com/products/casper/trial/)     CasperXP is EASY/FAST/RELIABLE hdd cloner for XP (windows) does not support Linux. If you do this and adding Linux to that hdd fails you have a perfect XP hdd back-up !!!. TheMan sets up UU so well that adding software is almost un-necessary but "howtoforge.com" has lots of very useful info !!. After you install UU as dual-boot with XP, you turn on NTFS internal drive config, you can pull info from your XP partition. I also found that if I install    [http://www.fs-driver.org/](http://www.fs-driver.org/)     EXT2 read/write !! to XP, I can see UU from XP and with my XP brain can see and look at UU from XP and helps me figure UU out !!. I also pulled upper desktop panel to bottm, bottom to top (taskbars) as well as set my UU to auto login (hate signing in over and over again)
System/Administration/Login Window/"password"/ "Login Window Preferences"-Top Tab - Security - Enable Auto Login read and follow the plan.  I hope this is OK by 'TheMan" I hope this helps, if it does, I will add a bit more, If not I will still ENJOY !! My UU 1.4DVD install. XP is for NFS Games at my house. My UU also won't capture TV with TVtime BUT it plays, again, re-boot to XP and use Leadtek software to Capture. I have been linux'ing since Jan 2007 and if Linux continues to mature as fast as it has since Jan, Microsoft will have little to offer except BUY this, Buy that, I wish I had TheMans knowledge, UU is MY favorite, I have tried damn near every other Linux that is downloadable ......  Keep up the GREAT work, Many Brews to YA !!!!!!!!! :)  Oh,Previous to TheMans UU1.4DVD, This was the best I found, still has great noobie UU user info to read    [http://www.howtoforge.com/the_perfect_desktop_ubuntu7.04](http://www.howtoforge.com/the_perfect_desktop_ubuntu7.04)

### Post by jrickard on 2007-08-23
I'm building a new machine and it is somewhat of a monster.  It uses Intel's 6850 Quad Extreme 2 processor and DDR3 memory.

The Ultimate Ubuntu 1.4 I have installed on a little HP mini is very nice.  But it appears to use a generic (i686) version of the kernel.  It's actually a dual processor 64 bit machine.  

The one I'm building is obviously a quad 64-bit.  Is there a way to get a 64-bit version of UU or UU gamers?

Second question.  Raid rears its ugly head.  Two Hitachi 1 TB drives.  I want to RAID 0 them.  That pretty much takes me to the Alternate install disk to get mdadm so I can setup a software RAID.  Is there any way to get mdadm into UU or UU gamers?

Jack Rickard

It is fixed the problem is some of the debs came from a windows server imagine that...  naming convention of blahblah%3a_1.2 etc., renamed and re-indexed.

You will need to run update:
```
sudo apt-get update
```

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-23
> **kbuzzard said:**
> I feel for you TheMan having all these problems, BUT ,the human body is close to the perfect machine and is screws up fairly regularly unless ut most care is observed, especially after mass doses of Brews !!. You will now show me as using UU1.4DVD and in converting to UU from XP,I thought to suggest some things to help fellow XP converters to UU, If they want to convert to Vista, no comment - have fun. I will add some links to use as tutors for new Linux/Ubuntu users like myself to ease transition pains. I use 40gig SATA hdds as I have learned NOT to store data on my OS hdds, I use external usb2 hdd kits so I can clone and swap,replace as XP has taught me SO WELL.            "Killdisk"  [http://www.killdisk.com/downloadfree.htm](http://www.killdisk.com/downloadfree.htm)   cleans hdds to NEW RAW state, great for fresh installs of any OS, formatting does NOT clean that well, you can get floppy or CD boot versions and is EASY to use. If you have XP running on you PC and want to install UU's,  [http://www.howtoforge.com/dual_boot_windows_xp_vista_ubuntu_feisty](http://www.howtoforge.com/dual_boot_windows_xp_vista_ubuntu_feisty)    An excellent HowTo site for any of us. I have follwed this tutorial 4 times without issues with XP !!. If before you do that , you want to CLONE your working XP to another hdd first    [http://www.fssdev.com/products/casper/trial/](http://www.fssdev.com/products/casper/trial/)     CasperXP is EASY/FAST/RELIABLE hdd cloner for XP (windows) does not support Linux. If you do this and adding Linux to that hdd fails you have a perfect XP hdd back-up !!!. TheMan sets up UU so well that adding software is almost un-necessary but "howtoforge.com" has lots of very useful info !!. After you install UU as dual-boot with XP, you turn on NTFS internal drive config, you can pull info from your XP partition. I also found that if I install    [http://www.fs-driver.org/](http://www.fs-driver.org/)     EXT2 read/write !! to XP, I can see UU from XP and with my XP brain can see and look at UU from XP and helps me figure UU out !!. I also pulled upper desktop panel to bottm, bottom to top (taskbars) as well as set my UU to auto login (hate signing in over and over again)
System/Administration/Login Window/"password"/ "Login Window Preferences"-Top Tab - Security - Enable Auto Login read and follow the plan.  I hope this is OK by 'TheMan" I hope this helps, if it does, I will add a bit more, If not I will still ENJOY !! My UU 1.4DVD install. XP is for NFS Games at my house. My UU also won't capture TV with TVtime BUT it plays, again, re-boot to XP and use Leadtek software to Capture. I have been linux'ing since Jan 2007 and if Linux continues to mature as fast as it has since Jan, Microsoft will have little to offer except BUY this, Buy that, I wish I had TheMans knowledge, UU is MY favorite, I have tried damn near every other Linux that is downloadable ......  Keep up the GREAT work, Many Brews to YA !!!!!!!!! :)  Oh,Previous to TheMans UU1.4DVD, This was the best I found, still has great noobie UU user info to read    [http://www.howtoforge.com/the_perfect_desktop_ubuntu7.04](http://www.howtoforge.com/the_perfect_desktop_ubuntu7.04)

Thanks for the info, someone will come through here and most likely use your advice.  This thread is Phat ;)

I have been checking out some of the games [Gridwars]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/gridwars_0.0.0+20060309-1~getdeb1_i386.deb") is the shiznit, addictive.  Windows may have hit the curb 100%.  Have gotten 1.2 Million on hard, better then the author has done from what I read on his page. ;)

I thought I'd toss down a screencast of [UUE 1.4 Gamers]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/screencasts/appsngames.ogg").  Watch the list of games scroll Enjoy!!!  Be aware the screencast is real high resolution 1680 X 1050 widescreen.

Thanks again for your input and assistance.

The one I'm building is obviously a quad 64-bit.  Is there a way to get a 64-bit version of UU or UU gamers?

Second question.  Raid rears its ugly head.  Two Hitachi 1 TB drives.  I want to RAID 0 them.  That pretty much takes me to the Alternate install disk to get mdadm so I can setup a software RAID.  Is there any way to get mdadm into UU or UU gamers?

Jack Rickard

Jack,

Wow!!! Quad Core, must be nice.  In a terminal do the following:
```
uname -r
```

Mine shows 2.6.20-16-generic  <<- don't confuse the word generic is it for amds 64 or 32 bit single or dual, intel etc.  Starting I believe in edgy they have made the kernel load the appropriate module based on your CPU as a module instead of compiling it in.  I could be wrong perhaps before then.

To see if both err all 4 of your processors are being used by the o/s drop the following in a terminal:
```
cat /proc/cpuinfo
```

Should display similar:
```
processor       : 0
vendor_id       : AuthenticAMD
cpu family      : 15
model           : 35
model name      : AMD Athlon(tm) 64 X2 Dual Core Processor 4600+
stepping        : 2
cpu MHz         : 2407.333
cache size      : 512 KB
physical id     : 0
siblings        : 2
core id         : 0
cpu cores       : 2
fdiv_bug        : no
hlt_bug         : no
f00f_bug        : no
coma_bug        : no
fpu             : yes
fpu_exception   : yes
cpuid level     : 1
wp              : yes
flags           : fpu vme de pse tsc msr pae mce cx8 apic sep mtrr pge mca cmov pat pse36 clflush mmx fxsr sse sse2 ht syscall nx mmxext fxsr_opt lm 3dnowext 3dnow pni lahf_lm cmp_legacy ts fid vid ttp
bogomips        : 4831.28
clflush size    : 64

processor       : 1
vendor_id       : AuthenticAMD
cpu family      : 15
model           : 35
model name      : AMD Athlon(tm) 64 X2 Dual Core Processor 4600+
stepping        : 2
cpu MHz         : 2407.333
cache size      : 512 KB
physical id     : 0
siblings        : 2
core id         : 1
cpu cores       : 2
fdiv_bug        : no
hlt_bug         : no
f00f_bug        : no
coma_bug        : no
fpu             : yes
fpu_exception   : yes
cpuid level     : 1
wp              : yes
flags           : fpu vme de pse tsc msr pae mce cx8 apic sep mtrr pge mca cmov pat pse36 clflush mmx fxsr sse sse2 ht syscall nx mmxext fxsr_opt lm 3dnowext 3dnow pni lahf_lm cmp_legacy ts fid vid ttp
bogomips        : 4814.92
clflush size    : 64
```

Your list will be twice that long ;)

Raid is a real bear to get set up I have tried to raid my 320's and failed miserably.  I used the live DVD connected to the net and installed mdadm, tried note the word tried to set it up to no avail.  I'm sure I could raid them as storage but not as a boot device, which I would imagine would be lightning fast.  I suggest you use the same technique from the live DVD.

64 bit edition?  I have tried to build it quite a while back, edgy days and it crashed each time I tried, software was in it's infancy then, not saying it is rock solid, which I'm sure it is not.  Perhaps time permitting I may take another stab at it.

This is an error in firefox's CSS (cascade Style Sheet), the upgrade script will fix it.  This is a [ known problem]("http://ubuntuforums.org/archive/index.php/t-509241.html") when using a darker theme and every time firefox gets upgraded it overwrites the one I used to fix it.  One option is to use one of the other 100 themes I included ;)

Sorry bout that was breaking it down as fast as I seen it :)  1.5 will hold that if it is released as 1.3 had KDE, Gnome, enlightenment & windowmaker.   I have had a great delay.  I have built probably 100's if not thousands of PC's through-out my life, personally 99% of them AMD, no I do not charge them as if they were buying Intel... It is the biggest bang for the buck.  Typically talk them into AMD Sorry to involve you :)  Sorry Jack but the (quad core you are going to build) will be dropped by an AMD quad core.

This is the thing that grabs most users... Where does one draw the line?  Well I could wait for the next latest and greatest... No matter when you "buy" you lose what is cutting edge is junk tomorrow, the honest truth broke down.

I will give you a suggestion buy an NVidia Video card any one of them but a MX not saying MX's don't work. 99.9% does and no 8800, I thought I'd take the time to break that down.  I could have bought an 8800 (top of the line) vid card for less then I have in my rig to buy 2 of them.  Even though I have 2 vid cards which smokes most that pay high $, that was not the sole reason I did so.  Most main boards do not support 2 vid cards, lets get that out in the open.

Even though the 8800 has support for DX 10 woot to me even if it did mean anything to me other then frame rate is squat, does my card err cards not support DX 10 (and faster then a 8800)?  Yes some of the pixel shaders maybe not, I run all my games max resolution the game can handle and does not choke.  This is what you need to think about when buying a card, read the reviews, place weight on best you can get for you budget.

Even if you are on a limited budget, review the card... Funny more limited then me :)  This goes back a long way when I say is it better to be "sound blaster" compatible or to have a sound blaster?  Same as NVidia Compatible.  Just my 2 cents.

### Post by Applegeek on 2007-08-24
I downloaded the 1.4 DVD yesterday and loaded it up, on a fresh hard drive.  This is an AMd64 system with a nvidia 7100 card, and it previously had been running Fiesty-AMD64 with Beryl for a few months, very stable.

UU looked pretty good and Envy got the new nvidia drivers running.  Then I made the mistake of hitting the "Upgrades Available" icon and let Synaptic upgrade everything.

After all the downloads in place I -think- there was a kernel rebuild to -16, then the system would no longer boot - it hung telling on me X server couldn't start.  I replaced the xorg.conf files with 2 separate backups, still no boot - one log is helpfully telling me that "X" server can't start, another telling me version do not match.

I'm not an "X"-pert, so after a while I just reloaded Feisty-amd 64, and installed the nvidia drivers manually, and everything is just fine again.  It looks like still some issues with UU.

The little that I saw of UU was pretty neat, although the dark theme makes some text really hard to read, but the other themes are just a click away.

I'll look forward to see the attempt at UU Gamer's!!

Will this be made available in a 64-bit build?

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-24
> **nynoah said:**
> I have done the change in the repositories.  But still for some reason I can not get the "Upgrade" icon to work.  It launches it and then it closes instantly.  Do you know what might be going.  I am starting from a fresh install.  There is no computer problem I think, because I am running 64 bit Kubuntu on my other hard drive.

The repo is fixed (running just for the scripts purpose on a temp free server), however since you clicked the icon while it was down the file has been replaced with a 0 byte file.  Below will fix this situation, in a terminal:

```
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh

```

Sorry about that...

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-24
> **Applegeek said:**
> I downloaded the 1.4 DVD yesterday and loaded it up, on a fresh hard drive.  This is an AMd64 system with a nvidia 7100 card, and it previously had been running Fiesty-AMD64 with Beryl for a few months, very stable.

UU looked pretty good and Envy got the new nvidia drivers running.  Then I made the mistake of hitting the "Upgrades Available" icon and let Synaptic upgrade everything.

After all the downloads in place I -think- there was a kernel rebuild to -16, then the system would no longer boot - it hung telling on me X server couldn't start.  I replaced the xorg.conf files with 2 separate backups, still no boot - one log is helpfully telling me that "X" server can't start, another telling me version do not match.

I'm not an "X"-pert, so after a while I just reloaded Feisty-amd 64, and installed the nvidia drivers manually, and everything is just fine again.  It looks like still some issues with UU.

The little that I saw of UU was pretty neat, although the dark theme makes some text really hard to read, but the other themes are just a click away.

I'll look forward to see the attempt at UU Gamer's!!

Will this be made available in a 64-bit build?

I have written a short [howto]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") that will be of great interest to you in particular and other NVidia users.

At current there is no 64 bit build of UUE at all, crashed each time (very unstable then) I tried to build one that was back in edgy days.  I have been asked this question many a time.  I am now going to try again...  Downloading 64 bit edition to attempt a 64 bit Gamers build.  Will post results.  I believe this to be a futile waste of my time I'm sure many of the games do not provide a 64 bit edition, but will attempt none the less.  Thought I'd toss down a [screen cast]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/screencasts/upgrading_ultimate_gamers.ogg") of me downloading it and running upgrade script, note the upgrade script does not download squat other then Ubuntu 64 downloading in background ;)  that is until it hits Americas Army which is not on the disk BTW. ### Post by Applegeek on 2007-08-24
So far the Amd64 Fiesty has been very stable for me - the only issue being a few plug ins for Firefox - but the workarounds seemed to be fine.  I haven't yet run into a situation where I couldn't do something because I had the 64 build.  I did see stability problems with Edgy 64, though.

Thanks for the HowTo for the NVidia crowd, and I always enjoy testing this stuff.

Here's what my test boxes are used for:  I give them to my teenage daughters and let thme surf the 'Net for a week, load photos & music, edit movies, print stuff, use wireless access, etc.  So theses boxes get a workout.  And believe me the girls _will_ complain if something seems wrong.  So far Ubuntu Feisty-64  has behaved itself fairly well.  Much better than Vista...

---

### Post by Applegeek on 2007-08-24
Ahhh...My mistake was to install the video driver before all the upgrading.

It looks like the secret is to do all the upgrading FIRST, --then-- install the NVidia driver.

In any case scenario any Ubuntu / *nix is better then tolerating that.  Pretty soon XP will no longer be supported, where are the users with limited hardware coming?  To visit me or another Nix distro, either way I am happy. Just my 2 cents.  With out a doubt I will run a 64 Bit O/S in the future, however I feel that is not today (other then to test it).

### Post by Shadowrunner340 on 2007-08-25
Hi, you're doing great things here, and I just have one question. 

Do you have all the necessary DX libraries for non-OpenGL games for Wine? And if I install this, can I just install a normally Windows game and go?

---

### Post by Scruffynerf on 2007-08-25
> **Shadowrunner340 said:**
> Hi, you're doing great things here, and I just have one question. 

Do you have all the necessary DX libraries for non-OpenGL games for Wine? And if I install this, can I just install a normally Windows game and go?

I really, really recommend that you start asking some questions in the gaming forums here.

This distro, like all the others does NOT have DX libraries. None of them do. This is linux... not windows. Some groups, like those with the WINE system are emulating most of the windows API's

That said, even with WINE, NO windows game will just loadup and install under linux. A game is essentially an application that relied on the operating system. Most applications out there are designed with the window's operating system in mind. The linux operating system is totally different to windows.

---

Try this take a tour through google yahoo and msn and search for "ubuntu ultimate", naturally #1 of 2,270,000, now take the time a search in which I did not design for even though the name is there "ubuntu software", I am once again #1, sorry getdeb, they deserve that to me but the results?  54,400,000 websites I am #1, amazing is it.  I can make yours the same way.  I highly suggest you get your ducks in a row.. I will push you traffic.  Perfect what you have going on and I will send you 1000's of people, each day I see rarely less then 10,000 people a day, not hits close to 16 Mil on that, most people underestimate me on that, and fold their server.  I thought I would close out for you ladies and gents for the eve.

I didn't expect this. Changed my PM status. Well thanks for the support, the site still needs a lot of work. I need to arrange or use a different format, but the work there is "Free" to anyone who wants to use it. My support is to you, this OS, and to the Linux community.

### Post by Ritchstorm on 2007-08-26
Hey TheeMahn, I've just installed the DVD version of Ubuntu Ultimate and I very pleased with it so far. I want to ask you just a few things. Firstly, I use a wireless card that requires windows drivers. I normally use ndiswrapper to install it but I thought I might try out the little Windows Wireless thing I noticed in the Administrator tools. I haven't tried it yet but as shown in this topic ([http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=225206&highlight=wusb54gs](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=225206&highlight=wusb54gs)) you have to copy two more files into the same folder as the driver for my specific wireless card for some reason. I was just wondering can I do this or would it be better to use ndiswrapper? Secondly, the theme is so nice but it sometimes makes  some text hard to see. Is this for everyone or just my dire gfx card (ATI RADEON x300). One other thing that I noticed is when I booted from the DVD first time it had that cool loading screen but every other time since then it's been standard ubuntu. IS this a bug or something?

Many thanks in making such a good distro! 
Ritch Powell

### Post by kbuzzard on 2007-08-26
Conclusion, VM server easy, XP installs and runs fine - be SURE to use custom setting to get sound,usb,etc support. For Games/TV cards, No support for usb game controllers or TV card type hardware. If you mess up, uninstall all VMS and re-do, I did 3x to get right. I just used Wubi to install Ubuntu inside XP, superslick for XP users to try ubuntu but dl's u 7.04 from net, maybe if internet off it would work from CD/DVD but doubt it,  Did considerable research on Cedega and there are lots of un happy folks concerning that, needs $$ to support. SO, xp for ms games amd uu for everything else, now I have to learn Xawtv and do a final systems build. Thanks The Mahn for this space, I hope I help the xp to UU folks out some, keep on keep'n on.

---

### Post by fuzzmo on 2007-08-26
TheMahn - you are aptly named.  A brilliant effort by you which has made me come back to Linux (had enough of vista - XP is actually ok but I would rather stay with Linux).

The Ubuntu Ultimate theme is superb - trying to get conky going - I can but everytime a window goes over it it seems to 'rub' it out - any suggestions from anyone would be greatly appreciated.

However I thought I would ask - with the Ultimate theme I find I cannot see any text when accessing my gmail or facebook (within firefox - Opera seems ok).  I note the theme doesn't allow for changing of colours and I have played around with changing system fonts and colours but to no avail.  Am sure there is a simple solution to this but I can't see it.  Any suggestions?

Many thanks!

Fuzzmo.

### Post by Jeff_From_VA on 2007-08-26
Is it possible to get ubuntu ultimate in 64 bit, and also with the ubuntu alternate installer?  

I ask because the alternate installer allows me to use software raid to install on my dual raptors.  I just built a new system about 5 days ago, and have been playing with installs and such, with normal ubuntu, I can not seem to get fakeraid to work under linux with my mobo.  (GIGABYTE GA-P35-DQ6) -- However with the alternate install cd from ubuntu, I can use software raid and have both / and /home on a raid 0, while I keep /boot on a single drive.  

I would love, and I mean LOVE in a filthy, dirty, immoral, and perverted kind of way, to try this out. It looks like exactly, 100% everything I am looking for. Frankly, from the looks of it, you have ubuntu the way it would be in a perfect world.

Is this the alternate installer option available? If not, will this be at some future point in time so I can keep checking for it?  If not, is there a way to upgrade my feisty install to ultimate?

I will let you know how a 64 bit build of intel based goes.

If you decide to install it I suggest following the [top instructions]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") skipping the nvidia and xorg sections.  After you have installed in the order provided I suggest a trip here for your [ATI drivers]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=414194").  This unlike the normal ATI howto I typically recommend is written with the new guy in mind this will just "get it working" and not with the latest and greatest driver.  Envy is just a tool, it is not a fix all of fix all, your millage may vary.

Jack,

Great to see you hanging out in here ;)  I will let you know the AMD 64 Gamers edition is uploaded and am uploading the X86 version 18 hours just to upload it almost 4GB!!!, the AMD 64 edition there is no such thing as Flash in a 64 bit format...  There is a work around that allows the 32 bit counterpart to run in a 64 bit environment.  I will have to update the script to test arch and offer the end user the same work around.  In this case I can only help you with Java & acroread right now (I am running the 64 Bit version).  I am going to re-install my gamers X86 and come back and tell you the solution for flash.

This is just my humble opinion The AMD 64 Edition has made leaps and bounds over the edgy days, however the lack of availability of many packages (have not been converted to 64 bit) has pushed me back to the X86 edition.  I'm sure in time I will go back to the 64 Edition, however that time is not now, will wait for the growing pains to go away.

Your fix:
```
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes j*-mozilla-plugin 
```

Well my my just found out no acroread here either (64Bit world), Im sure there are alternatives, ill be back with fixes for both sorry.  I have to install the X86 edition so I can post screenshots, and a screencast for the webpage anyway.

O.K. make sure you are using the new repo the main repo is down until my check arrives from Google, they just sent it today, was in progress since the 24th, or you can wait till I get it will be put back online not a "free account", I know this has been a thorn in everyones side since it went down.  I don't even make $5,000 a month to pay hosting, which would have been out of my pocket, and no I won't accept donations.

first open your sources list:
```
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

New repo address temporarly: deb [http://ubuntusoftware.info/](http://ubuntusoftware.info/) **YOURDISTRO** all

replace **YOURDISTRO** with edgy or feisty depending on the version running 1.3 or less is edgy 1.4+ is feisty.  If you wait until the repo goes back up you won't have to adjust squat.  Introduced in 1.3+ the repo is integrated, accordingly.  Works the same way with the unreleased and also integrated AMD 64 Edition, however not just my repo but ccals are integrated, so if mine goes down again you as well as anyone else running it will be unaffected, I think a big thanks to ccal goes out for this reason as well.  I will have to discuss what is involved with a repo to get him to put it online, one thing at a time I certainly have my hands full ;)

With repos in place the following will obtain your flash and acroread:
```
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes flashplugin-nonfree acroread-plugins
```

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-29
> **erechm said:**
> I have already installed Ubuntu 7.04 AMD64.  Is there a way to turn it into Ultimate Gamers without having to reinstall?

By the way, great work!  I tried the Live DVD and love it.  I'm waiting on the Gamers Edition though!

Thanks!

Well now a guinea pig lol, you would be the first to try since the 64 bit edition has not been released yet.  I will now write a script just for you or anyone else running AMD 64 Feisty Fawn, and are willing to accept the possible consequences.  Do not download it and run it unless you are willing to do so.

What this script will do is download scripts from main server and related files, create an upgrade icon on you desktop, and add the AMD64 repo.  Will attach the script to this post as well as code after I write it in a few minutes.

```
#!/bin/bash
# AMD 64 Feisty Fawn to Ubuntu Ultimate 64 Gamers
# -----------------------------------------------
# Script Via TheeMahn
# By running this script you take all responsibilies for possibiltys of data loss etc.
# Create local copy of scripts from main server
sudo mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/axelfmix.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/playlist.m3u
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh

# Repo into sources.list
sudo echo deb http://ubuntusoftware.info feisty all | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update sources list
sudo apt-get update

# Grab required packages
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy

# Create Shortcut on desktop
echo "Creating Gamers shortcut..."

# Create desktop shortcut for Gamers
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

Nothing to better to do as I upload for the next 16 1/2 hours ;)  I am in UUE 1.4 Gamers (not 64 bit) I have no way to test this script, let me know if it works for you.

---

### Post by mnallen on 2007-08-29
Hi.  I'm kind of new to Ubuntu, but even though this is my first post, I do have a little bit of an idea what I'm doing.  I've just always been able to find answers to everything I need without posting.

I had Ubuntu 7.04 for a while, but I couldn't get CF to work, so I tried out the Ultimate 1.4 Live DVD.  I've got it working pretty well, even though when I updated the kernel to 1.6, xorg.conf somehow got messed up, so I'm currently just using the 1.5 kernel.

I'd like to say first, great work on Ultimate! I'm really enjoying it, and I'm sure I never could have gotten Feisty looking this good on my own.

The main problem I'm having right now, though,  is similar to what I just read in the last few posts.  I can't get flash to work in firefox.  I'm on a duel core computer, so I assumed I needed to use the nspluginwrapper work around, after the flash plugin in the repos didn't work.  But it kept coming back saying it didn't match my system's architecture: it says I have i386.  But if neither it or the plugin in the repos works, I don't know what I have.  I just did a standard install from the Live DVD, just like I did with Feisty before.

So I tried updating my repos and then using "sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes flashplugin-nonfree acroread-plugins".  

The first time I tried, I entered the name of the repo:  deb [http://ubuntusoftware.info/feisty](http://ubuntusoftware.info/feisty) all.

The plugin command came back with "Malformed line."  So I added a space before feisty.

Then it came back with:

Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Couldn't find package acroread-plugins

Am I missing something?  Or is this just not the answer to my problem anyway?  Thanks for any help!

### Post by erechm on 2007-08-29
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> Well now a guinea pig lol, you would be the first to try since the 64 bit edition has not been released yet.  I will now write a script just for you or anyone else running AMD 64 Feisty Fawn, and are willing to accept the possible consequences.  Do not download it and run it unless you are willing to do so.

What this script will do is download scripts from main server and related files, create an upgrade icon on you desktop, and add the AMD64 repo.  Will attach the script to this post as well as code after I write it in a few minutes.

```
#!/bin/bash
# AMD 64 Feisty Fawn to Ubuntu Ultimate 64 Gamers
# -----------------------------------------------
# Script Via TheeMahn
# By running this script you take all responsibilies for possibiltys of data loss etc.
# Create local copy of scripts from main server
sudo mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/axelfmix.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/playlist.m3u
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh

# Repo into sources.list
sudo echo deb http://ubuntusoftware.info feisty all | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update sources list
sudo apt-get update

# Grab required packages
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy

# Create Shortcut on desktop
echo "Creating Gamers shortcut..."

# Create desktop shortcut for Gamers
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

Nothing to better to do as I upload for the next 16 1/2 hours ;)  I am in UUE 1.4 Gamers (not 64 bit) I have no way to test this script, let me know if it works for you.

Thanks! I'll try it out tonight (ssshhh, I'm at work right now :) ) on my newest computer which Ubuntu is not running stable anyway.  I can't figure out why Ubuntu keeps shutting off the computer.  I think maybe it thinks the CPU is overheating because once it shuts off one time, it won't get past the boot.  Windows and PCLinuxOS run fine on the same machine.  It has a new AMD 64 6000+ processor which is known to run a little hot, so maybe that is it.  If you have any ideas let me know.  

Thanks again for the script!  If it works on the unstable Ubuntu, maybe I'll try it on the other two stable computers.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-29
> **mnallen said:**
> Hi.  I'm kind of new to Ubuntu, but even though this is my first post, I do have a little bit of an idea what I'm doing.  I've just always been able to find answers to everything I need without posting.

I had Ubuntu 7.04 for a while, but I couldn't get CF to work, so I tried out the Ultimate 1.4 Live DVD.  I've got it working pretty well, even though when I updated the kernel to 1.6, xorg.conf somehow got messed up, so I'm currently just using the 1.5 kernel.

I'd like to say first, great work on Ultimate! I'm really enjoying it, and I'm sure I never could have gotten Feisty looking this good on my own.

The main problem I'm having right now, though,  is similar to what I just read in the last few posts.  I can't get flash to work in firefox.  I'm on a duel core computer, so I assumed I needed to use the nspluginwrapper work around, after the flash plugin in the repos didn't work.  But it kept coming back saying it didn't match my system's architecture: it says I have i386.  But if neither it or the plugin in the repos works, I don't know what I have.  I just did a standard install from the Live DVD, just like I did with Feisty before.

So I tried updating my repos and then using "sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes flashplugin-nonfree acroread-plugins".  

The first time I tried, I entered the name of the repo:  deb [http://ubuntusoftware.info/feisty](http://ubuntusoftware.info/feisty) all.

The plugin command came back with "Malformed line."  So I added a space before feisty.

Then it came back with:

Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Couldn't find package acroread-plugins

Am I missing something?  Or is this just not the answer to my problem anyway?  Thanks for any help!

If you are running Feisty Fawn 64 yes, you are correct on both occasions.  If you are running any ultimate  (64 bit has not been released) you may need to update your list before it will allow you to install it.

```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes flashplugin-nonfree acroread-plugins
```
