### Post by jrickard on 2007-08-29
Your fix:
```
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes j*-mozilla-plugin 
```


first open your sources list:
[ime I certainly have my hands full ;)

With repos in place the following will obtain your flash and acroread:
```
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes flashplugin-nonfree acroread-plugins
```[/QUOTE]

Thanks a bunch.  That did it.  I just did not know how to do the Java and apparently the change in suppositories had me a bit hosed up.

I've used a thumb drive to drag off the few files and things I needed from the AMD64.

I'm going to use that machine to test your 64 bit version on.  If it works, I'll upgrade the big new machine.

Speaking of which, I was unable to get it working with software RAID with Gutsy Tribe 5 as well.  So I installed Ultimate Ubuntu 1.4 on it on one drive and Gutsy Tribe 5 64 bit on the other.  ODDLY, each install mounts the other's drive as a drive I can look at and read and write too.  So this gives me a dual boot system and each system can see the other and share files.  Although the mission was to have a 2.0 TB striped RAID 0 drive, I can see some advantage to this.  I can have a trusted desktop system on one drive, and a bleeding edge trial version on the other.

If Gamers 64-bit works on the little AMD64, I'll probably try it on the Gutsy side of the system.  Gutsy so far hasn't show much promise.  I can't get the Nvidia driver loaded on it etc.

Does gamers have enhancements UU 1.4 doesn't have, beyond games I mean.  I'm not terribly interested in games but am very taken with the installation you have created.  At one point, after hosing up the software raid with MDADM and unable to delete the Raid0, UU was the ONLY version I had that would install.  Once installed, I used it to clean off the other drive of partition tables where I think the MD0 stuff was hidden, and then I could install Gutsey Tribe 5.

Jack Rickard

Jack

I will let you know there is quite a bit missing from the 64 Bit edition, many packages I could not find, rock stable I do give it that, had to scour the net just to get something as simple as flash to work.  It is things such as that that made me happy to return to UUE 1.4 Gamers, 64 bit has many growing pains still ahead.

On the flip side weather you play games or not there are many enhancements in 1.4 Gamers, I probably only play 2 or 3 of them and there is quite the list.  What I suggest is the Use of 1.4 Gamers (non 64 bit), if you feel the need you can try the 64 Bit but I think you will be rushing to put the UUE 1.4 Gamers in once you have tried the other out for a while.  I did see speed improvements when doing heavy tasks in the AMD version (it did fully utilize both cores), but the loss of many apps etc. sent me back here in the end.

I recently uploaded a [screencast]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eCGmkPncPOs") of it please note this screencast is being recorded right from the Live DVD it is me showing you the apps and games available and installing it.  If it is hard for you to see I have it hosted in [high res]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/screencasts/Live_Disk.avi") on my server.  It is very choppy recorded at 10 frames per sec, it is hard on a computer to access the DVD and record, but gets the point across.  I will be making another screen cast of it installed and demonstrating a few apps and some of the games after all it is the Gamers edition ;)

### Post by jrickard on 2007-08-29
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> 

  I did see speed improvements when doing heavy tasks in the AMD version (it did fully utilize both cores), but the loss of many apps etc. sent me back here in the end.



UU has always used multiple cores just fine.  I think this is a kernel function now.  A year ago, we had to have the SMP version of the kernel (at least in Fedora) but that seems to have gone away.  

The 64 bit is only important in video and graphics work really, which is mostly what I'm interested in.  But yes, there doesn't seem to be a Flash or Adobe Reader etc done in 64-bit to my knowledge.  Most people are running 32-bit Firefox in their 64 bit systems as a workaround.

Jack Rickard

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-29
> **jrickard said:**
> UU has always used multiple cores just fine.  I think this is a kernel function now.  A year ago, we had to have the SMP version of the kernel (at least in Fedora) but that seems to have gone away.  

The 64 bit is only important in video and graphics work really, which is mostly what I'm interested in.  But yes, there doesn't seem to be a Flash or Adobe Reader etc done in 64-bit to my knowledge.  Most people are running 32-bit Firefox in their 64 bit systems as a workaround.

Jack Rickard

When building a O/S which is the heaviest task I suppose one can do, at least the heaviest I have seen as far as CPU usage I have seen when building in UUE 32 bit it use one core 100% and leave the other open to do other tasks.  This was not the case in the 64bit edition it took 100% of both and finished much faster then I have ever seen.  Just my 2 cents I have never seen a game I can not run at max / res detail so graphic sub system means nothing to me, but I have 2 video cards.  I don't run windows anymore or at least in the past month or better, so I supposed it would be different if I used CAD, people use their computer for different reasons, all my problems as far as things I do are resolved in this distro.  I built it not only with me in mind but that of others; naturally I am open to suggestions for new software.

What you refer to as "a work around" is something I don't feel the end user should have to deal with or tolerate, this I call growing pains ;)  If you are willing to accept inability to view webpages or use a work around, you may enjoy the AMD 64 Edition, I want to be honest with you and others you can go back to my [original thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=309704") #1 viewed BTW in that section ;)  I originally did not want to build a 64 Bit edition for this reason, the reason I build distros is for the ease of use for the end user.  Sure it is pretty, but not the reason I made it.

If I offended you in any way I apologize, I have had a few celebratory brews ;)

EDIT: [#2 viewed]("http://ubuntuforums.org/forumdisplay.php?f=179&daysprune=-1&order=desc&sort=views"), note closed this one won't close so I will be #1 here before I am done ;)

### Post by erechm on 2007-08-30
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> Well now a guinea pig lol, you would be the first to try since the 64 bit edition has not been released yet.  I will now write a script just for you or anyone else running AMD 64 Feisty Fawn, and are willing to accept the possible consequences.  Do not download it and run it unless you are willing to do so.

What this script will do is download scripts from main server and related files, create an upgrade icon on you desktop, and add the AMD64 repo.  Will attach the script to this post as well as code after I write it in a few minutes.

```
#!/bin/bash
# AMD 64 Feisty Fawn to Ubuntu Ultimate 64 Gamers
# -----------------------------------------------
# Script Via TheeMahn
# By running this script you take all responsibilies for possibiltys of data loss etc.

# Create local copy of scripts from main server
sudo mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/axelfmix.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/playlist.m3u
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh

# Repo into sources.list
sudo echo deb http://ubuntusoftware.info feisty all | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update sources list
sudo apt-get update

# Grab required packages
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy

# Create Shortcut on desktop
echo "Creating Gamers shortcut..."

# Create desktop shortcut for Gamers
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
```

Nothing to better to do as I upload for the next 16 1/2 hours ;)  I am in UUE 1.4 Gamers (not 64 bit) I have no way to test this script, let me know if it works for you.

I ran the script, but I don't see the Update Icon on my desktop.  It does look like it installed Envy.  Here is what I did.  I opened Terminal and did a Sudo command so I could enter password and become root.  I then copied and pasted the script.  Part of the way through the  cript it asked me for the Ubuntu 7.04 AMD64 Installation CD so I complied and pressed enter.  It finished up a few more things and that was it.  I  did notice that after it finished, I now have 31 updates available that I have not installed yet.
_________________________________

I went ahead and did the updates.  Then I ran the script again.  It put a shortcut on my desktop, but it doesn't do anything.  The screen just blinks quickly and then nothing happens.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-08-31
> **Detox06 said:**
> Hey. Was really looking forward to this. Downloaded from Europe Mirror last night, burned to DVD this morning, went to install, and got the error:

pidof [3793] can't read sid from /proc/1/stat
pidof [3793] can't read sid from /proc/10/stat
pidof [3793] can't read sid from /proc/11/stat

and UUG wouldn'ty boot. Any idea why this might be, or how to solve it? I would've downloaded via torrent but there are no one torrenting.

2 different torrents...  Get in a hurry did we as I updated the page?  All you should concern yourself with is does it match MD5SUM...

You were not supposed to see that before it's time.  It was not linked anywhere within my site...  If it passes MD5SUM  Did you burn it at 18X?  I have a 18X burner and burnt it at 8X, this ensures a good copy, always burn slower then"you can", as with any distro I always cut my speed at least in half. This is a smart thing to do irregardless.

To check MD5sum X86 (goto the folder you downloaded it to):
```
md5sum ubuntu-ultimate-1.4-gamers.iso
```

Should return:```

6d2e13eb38b2cd56bf1804b711b66fb5  ubuntu-ultimate-1.4-gamers.iso
```

Same as above with the AMD64 Edition:
```
20e7626608ee06c4aef801580d48ecc4  ubuntu-ultimate-1.4-gamers-amd64.iso
```

### Post by jrickard on 2007-09-01
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> When building a O/S which is the heaviest task I suppose one can do, at least the heaviest I have seen as far as CPU usage I have seen when building in UUE 32 bit it use one core 100% and leave the other open to do other tasks.  This was not the case in the 64bit edition it 


Actually, video processing is probably the heaviest task I suppose one could do.  It will use up whatever you've got.  I just had all FOUR processors up over 90% and  one was pretty well maxed out at 100%.  That's using your Ultimate Edition 1.4 and NOT the 64-bit version.

The increases you see on the 64-bit version have to be from the 64-bit.  They are not from symetrical multiprocessor (SMP).  I really think that's in the kernel now. 

That said, most video processing software will use 64-bit if it can get it.  And so my interest in your 64-bit version.  It should be quite a bit faster, but not because it is using SMP.  You already are.  

For what its worth.  I also have a little Athalon dual and it doesn't SMP all that well.  If you watch it for awhile, it's trying.  But it's not very good at it.  But I was seeing very good balance across 4 CPUS in this Intel 6850 - with your Ultimate 1.4.

Compiling software is actually not very CPU intensive.  It is somewhat heroically disk intensive.  H.264 is both but it gives the CPUs a much better workout.  It basically eats great gobs of video files into memory, and then tries to guess what all the pixels are going to do in the future...

Jack

---

I suggest you download [reconstructor]("http://reconstructor.aperantis.com/index.php?option=com_remository&Itemid=33&func=select&id=5") and build a disk.  It uses 100% of both CPU's solid cant be anything more cpu intensive then 100%, graphics can be equal but not more.  I grant you I don't have 4, but I would imagine it would use 100% of each as well.  Try it and let me know.  I plan on upgrading in the near future to a 6000+ am2 socket, not that this one is not fast I'd just assume to sell it and get a new one.  As much as I use a computer, I feel it is a necessary upgrade.  I will never buy an Intel quad core or not, I have always been an AMD man.  To each their own ;)

### Post by Fubie on 2007-09-02
so i followed this

Ccals repo is online for those that would like to add it:

     Code:
     sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list 
     Code:
     deb [http://uu.enarel.eu/repo/](http://uu.enarel.eu/repo/) feisty all

and my kde theme changed drastically.  i can't even change my theme very easily now.  when i added the repo i had 113 upgrades that went thru.

i want to get back to what menu's and themes that i had.

i'm using UUE 1.4 DVD.

It almost worked but stalled out loading gaim and when I tried TBird mail it rebooted and continued that until I disabled wireless in panel. Now when disabled, it will do random re-boot especially when DLing a file, :(, sounds like windblows crap again, NO NOT in UU PLEASE !!!!!!. Thanks in advance. All have GREAT Labor Day weekend. I see there is a Wireless Folder with Broadcom, it has .exe's, this has to be pretty easy, just don't wan't to screw up my install.

Have you tried Ndis wrapper?  Using a windows driver?  

System>>Administration>>Windows Wireless Drivers

A new wine and winedoors has just been added to the repo.  This does not apply to your problem personally, but...

```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade
sudo apt-get install -y --force-yes wine-doors
```

[Winedoors]("http://www.wine-doors.org/") is a new application that eases the setup of may common apps in windows and downloads / installs them for you.

### Post by the bioengineer on 2007-09-05
I have a setup question that I cannot find real info on (that does not contradict another source) so I thought I'd ask you here so all could see (talking to you TheeMan).

Problem is partitioning.  I don't have a problem actually making partitions, the GUI provided on your distro is simple enough.  Problem is in aspect of sizes.

Not talking about swap, plenty out there on that.  My question is on all the other bits.

This page - [www.overclock.net/linux-unix-mac/11208-linux-partitioning-guide.html](www.overclock.net/linux-unix-mac/11208-linux-partitioning-guide.html)
was where I started.  Never one to trust one source alone I looked at Ubuntu's official website at [https://help.ubuntu.com/7.04/installation-guide/i386/partition-sizing.html](https://help.ubuntu.com/7.04/installation-guide/i386/partition-sizing.html)
and I decided it would be best to follow this guide.

I made these partitions to my 3 disk system

IDE Master - 2 Gb / (root) primary
                    3 Gb /var primary
                    15 Gb /usr primary
IDE Slave -   2 Gb NTFS windows VM primary
                    2 Gb swap primary
                    8 Gb /tmp primary
                    8 Gb /home primary
SATA -       30 Gb NTFS Win XP primary
                 100 Gb NTFS win programs primary
                 370 Gb Data extended

Loaded UUE 1.4 just fine (after installing XP.  No problems with grub, no other boot problems.  Was pretty happy with myself - until I updated via system -> admin -> updater.  Everything was fine until I rebooted, no install errors.
That's when I get a major "blue screen of death" Linux style.  The boot just hangs, something about video or some other.

Did I partition wrong, leave something out, ignorantly forget some key aspect I should have seen?  Or is it just a device driver problem?  When I booted the Envy handled my 512Mb Nvidia AGP card just fine, even after reboot.  

please help:confused:

---

> **the bioengineer said:**

4. Bring up the GUI

How do I do this?  What is the command.  So many posts with such little relevance come up when I use the search link, I feel bad to bug you about such mundane matters.

Dumb question, didn't quite read far enough ahead.  

sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start

That is what I am here for, and the only dumb question is a question that goes un-asked.  I did suggest you print the page for that reason, however you can press and hold ctrl-alt and hit delete and it will reboot (after it shuts down properly), not quite the same as the command to re-start GDM but surely the easiest, if you still have problems of your GUI not coming back up please post.

the command as you asked is:
```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start
```


I would just press three keys and have it over with ;)

of your UUE (current 1.4?) on a Live DVD,
due to the huge size and resources needed to download it in
a bit torrent or other form.

on your webpage is disabled...

Can you specifically reply to this message, as well
as post a reply publically on your Ubuntu forum thread,
on these two questions/statements:

1) Due to Ubuntu and other public domain Linux
versions containg open source codes and public
domain avaliability, how is your UUE (Ubuntu Ultimate
Editions) assured and guarenteed free of virus/spyware/
keylogging, etc compaired to the verified releases of
Ubuntu itself?

2) Where can the consumer/user obtain your UUE versions
and/or latest releases on a live DVD without having to have the
access/resources and bandwidth to download 1.7 or more gb of
data via the internet alone?

THANKS![/QUOTE]


#1: Almost all software contained in the DVD is obtained from Canocals Repos or getdeb.net, I suppose one of the 2 could have put in a keylogger or similar, however highly unlikely.  To see all software that is installed in the distro one could:

```
sudo dpkg -l
```

Furthermore to see apps/suspicious software one could in a teyminal:

```
w
ps aux
netstat -ntp
```

This will show any connection to your computer and display all running apps / services, I'm fairly certain you will not see any activity, as well as the possibility of someone being logged into your computer.

#2: I have probably [posted]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3324551&postcount=954") this 5  times or better in my thread.  Today being one of them.

The main reason I do not have the contact working it is hard enough to just maintain answering of posts and PM's and is the #1 reason I do not use Pidgin, I am swamped each and every time I open it.

I did not sell it, I am in for the long hull ;)

For those that don't know alexa measures traffic unbiasedly, seeing the overall big picture, higher is  better on top stat being the individual owns that portion of the internet, I.E. percentage of the people in the world that comes to your site in a given day, website form each day making this harder and harder to achive.  The second is of all websites in the entire world (billions & billions) what position do you get for the day.  Top 100,000 or they don't even mess with you, I believe yahoo is #1 no chance of me being [there]("http://www.alexa.com/data/details/traffic_details?url=yahoo.com"), and I don't care if I have 100 servers.  The last is page views per user it is good to see this go up, means the user typically does not come to your website and view one page and leave, so as far as I am concerned everything you see is a huge plus ;)

You know instead of educating people in *nix, I should be helping people with SEO, whoa wait a minute I already have my hands full :)

Please excuse the many brews I have had lol

```
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh
```

### Post by JBAlaska on 2007-09-09
[COLOR="Blue"]I think it's high time for me to post a BIG Thank You to TheeMahn2003. It's been almost 2 months since a chain of events changed my computer life forever.[/COLOR]

It all started when I came home after a night of partying about 2 months ago intending to burn a dvd for a new female "friend", I started a transcode in Nero and it decided to crash Vista...no problem...reboot...oops the crash somehow corrupted my grub bootloader in which resides a certain BIOS "Fix" that was allowing me to erm.."evaluate" Vista Ultimate...no problem, I'll re-install the "fix"...oops forgot to disable my internet on reboot (did I mention I was drunk)..Vista locks me OUT...oops..

Two days later and there's no way (Short of buying a $300.00 key) I'm booting vista..Finally I say to hell with it and format my OS only boot partition and install W2k3.

So my system is back up, all my data is secure and most of the programs I use are installed...But somethings missing??..oh yea, the eye candy. Even though 2003 server is MUCH faster on my AMD64 than Vista, I really miss the eye candy..what can I say, I make my living sitting in front of my puter for 10-16 hours a day and I need something that looks good to keep me from getting bored to tears.

Shortly thereafter I was surfing and ran across a youtube video of Compiz Fusion..Damn, now that's what I'm talking about!..hmm, "runs on Linux"..Ok no problem, I learned computers on DOS (Yup I'm old) and have dabbled with *nix over the years..so time to find a distro..

My first thought was knoppix as I already had a DVD and remembered it was good at hardware detection, but it was a older version and I decided to do a google search.

My google search turned up **Ubuntu Ultimate Edition**, I followed the link and was impressed by the package of apps included, I installed UUE and was further impressed by the ease of the install and the sheer speed and stability of Ubuntu on my system.

So that was over 2 months ago and although I setup a duel boot with w2k3, I haven't even been in my winblows partition for over 2 weeks. I do cheat somewhat since I have a XP encoding box that I VNC into to use Gordian Knot and VirtualDubMod. Other than that, I have no intention of ever using windoze again. 

**If it hadn't been for UUE catching my eye I probably would not have found out about Ubuntu. For that alone (not to mention all your hard work and bandwidth) I say again,**

[SIZE="4"][COLOR="Red"]Thank You TheeMahn2003, You helped me get the microshite monkey off my back![/COLOR][/SIZE]

---

### Post by the bioengineer on 2007-09-09
Been playing a lot with my UUE 1.4 DVD distro.  But when I tried the Upgrade icon on the desktop to go to gamers, many installs failed.

Figuring this was due to the high traffic, I thought downloading the DVD of the gamers edition would be more appropiate, as I could copy the disc to give to the ppl who I talk to about UUE.:)

Two days later my .iso file is finished (whopping 50Kb/s, sic.)  from the Europe mirror.  "Awesome!" I say to myself, burning my first disc.\\:D/

And then......  the disc does not work.  The files are there, but the Live bootable quality is not.  I get a disc boot error every time.  "Sh!t" I say, and burn at the lowest speed possible.  30 min. later, the second disc does not work either.  "Must be a bad download" I say, and try another route. :-k

I try the torrent, but the seeders drop off at an amazing rate (there were only six to begin with) while the demand holds at 20+.  Before a half-hour is up there are no seeders at all.:cry:

I am now trying to get the .iso file from the other mirror, as the main server link does not work at all.  So my question to TheeMan and my UUE brothers/sisters is this - Have any of you experienced this?  Is there some way around it to make the DVD bootable again?  Two days is a long time to wait for a bad file, I'd really like to know if that's just the way it's going to be or if there is something I can do on my end to rectify the problem.:confused:

### Post by JBAlaska on 2007-09-13
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> I have 0 control over the release of 1.5 my server only allows up 2 GB Apache problem (hosting service running older but more reliable *nix) this release is 2.2GB, I have contacted ccal it is up to him if we see a 1.5 minus via script which all running 1.4+ will still see.  The ball is out of my hands...

2GB storage or 2GB upload limit to the server, If it's the latter you might consider rar'ing the ISO into 50mb chunks and including a sfv file for error checking. imho this should be the way the ISO is released on bittorrent as it's much more efficient than a single 2.2GB file.

Also error checking with a sfv file allows for a GUI rather than having to match a MD5 string. Releasing in rar format also makes sense for HTTP and FTP distribution as well.

In my erm..hobby we distribute avi and dvd iso's this way through BT and FTP and it works a treat.

I will be editing this post to keep people informed:

**09/14/07** I have discussed it with ccal and he does not care (he has unlimited bandwidth and pays the same no matter how many people "hit" him") there will be a 1.5 release (uploading it now), however I will be unable to host my own release, a lot of weight will be on his shoulders.  Ccal deserves a huge thanks, he is about to be at max capacity for a long time to come, I will adjust the script to remove his repo from your list 200 people at a time is more then anyone should ask for.  After traffic calms down I will update the script and put it back in, the script update will be no time soon, obviously I have "bigger fish to fry", I wish people could see what I do.

**09/12/07** I have completed and am currently running UUE 1.5 (Gnome, KDE, Enlightenment, Window Maker & XFCE included) each environment 9 in all are selectable from the login window.  I have made a short [screencast]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/screencasts/kde.avi") of the KDE environment & posted a few [screenshots]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3354935&postcount=994") of Gnome environment.  With the current amount of [traffic]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3355692&postcount=996") it is unknown when / if it will even be released the servers are at maximum capacity, if traffic slows down perhaps we will have a release; those of you that run 1.4+ will want to wait for the script update, this will allow you to upgrade to 1.5 w/o having to download the 2.2 Gig 1.5 release.  Will post more info as it becomes available.

**09/07/07** Updated [Super Tweaking Ultimate]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Howto_tweak_ubuntu_ultimate.html") page, now have UUE 1.4 Gamers booting into gnome in 15 secs ;)  Bonus tip added for those with dual core CPU's.  If and only if you like the end results, please provide me a few [kudos.]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Super_Tweaking_Ultimate_Feisty").  Probably by the time you read it fully a thing of the past, a large webpage ;)

**09/04/07** Paid for the repo & download server to be put back online, repo problems should now be a thing of the past.  The download server however will take at least 24 hours to refill (unless ccal helps me out).

**08/30/07** Due to the great work of Ccal as always.  Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 Gamers is now available for [download]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4_Gamers/"), [screencast]("http://youtube.com/watch?v=duhAtZT11vw").  Enjoy!!!  His repo is now online as well downloading from it now ;)  If you have time I'd appreciate a few [diggs]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1_4_Gamers_Edition_i386_DVD_Now_Available_at_LinuxTracker").  Lets see if ccal can handle the digg effect ;)

**08/29/07** Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4  Gamers edition (64 Bit) is now uploaded, 18 hours to upload 1.4 Gamers X86 (16 1/2 left).

**08/28/07** Repo is now mirrored via ccal, 2 standard repo mirrors and one on the way ;)  Will be set in motion in a few days.  UUE 1.4 Gamers Edition (AMD64 or EM64T architecture (e.g., Athlon64, Opteron, EM64T Xeon) has been built and is uploading as I type this for those of you with 64 bit CPU's.  There is also a standard X86 release (already done and ready for upload) for those with intel or don't want to run a 64 bit O/S, a huge download most games are integrated on the DVD.   You can read more about it [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3266670&postcount=862").  If you would like to see the list of games included you can read more about that from my [website]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4_Gamers/") please note this page is under construction as of time of this post, don't expect the download links to work yet, screenshots and screencast are from 1.4 DVD not Gamers yet.

**08/21/07** Repo and download server are down here is [why]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3225468&postcount=725"), please don't click on the upgrade Icon until they are back up, will take days in just re-uploading, thanks for your patience and sorry for the inconvenience.

**08/18/07** Even though a re-built Ubuntu Ultimate Gamers 1.4 (smaller & windows users happy), I will not [release]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3212131&postcount=699") anything that cant be downloaded, no matter the OS you run; release has been uploaded to main server, there will be no "official" release until ccal comes back from Italy (1 week and & 5 days), or someone PM's me with unlimited bandwidth and ability to serve a hungry crowd.

Example:
```
Bandwidth:  	 4579333.41 MB of 2000000.00 MB (228.97%)
```

This release will exceed that manifold...  Not just in size (4GB) but demand.  If you have 4GB of space and unlimited bandwidth (what you see above is Terabytes not Gigabytes of bandwidth, thought I would shut down the PM's before started), please message me.

**08/14/07** I have written a [How to Super Tweak Ultimate]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Howto_tweak_ubuntu_ultimate.html") page, even though it is written specifically with Ubuntu Ultimate in mind I see no reason why a Feisty Fawn user can't use it as well.  Enjoy!!!

**08/12/07**  Sorry to inform you that we may not have a release exempt a ubuntuforum exclusive.  You most likely will not be able to download Gamers unless you run linux.  Due to the limitation in [Windows]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3175230&postcount=543")
 this is the best case situation, I may have to re-build it all.

**08/10/07 Gamers Edition is done!!!** You can read more about it that [here]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3168828&postcount=511").  Will probably be 2 days before you can download it between uploading & mirroring a release of this size.

**08/07/07** I wrote a brief [howto]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472") covering installation of UUE DVD & Nvidia Driver installation.  Testing a build of Gamers Edition a bit over 4GB!!!  Download server has exceed it's bandwidth limits after almost 400,000 downloads of UUE 1.4 DVD and almost 200,000 of 1.4 CD, simply crazy and done in short order.

**08/04/07** I thought I would shttp://hare with you all the making of UUE 1.4 Gamers, most wont be able to view it unless you run UUE 1.4 or have the codecs installed, first [beginning]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/test/starting"), and [midstride]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/test/midstride") along.  Please note no extension *nix doesn't need them ;)  Going quite well thus far.  245,517 downloads of 1.4 DVD from main download server only.  Not bad for 2 days ;)  Massive [traffic]("http://ubuntuforums.org/attachment.php?attachmentid=39812&d=1186257394").  Progress on [UUE 1.4 Gamers Edition]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3134528&postcount=423").  We really could use some mirrors, post if you can help.

**08/03/07** Mirror & torrent now setup.  Please feel free to give me a few "[diggs]("http://digg.com/linux_unix/Ubuntu_Ultimate_Edition_1_4_DVD_Gnarly_Gnome_Released")", I dugg my own website I want to see if it remains up, the propose of having 3 to begin with.  Thanks.

**08/02/07**  [Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 DVD]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4/") is available for download enjoy!!!.  [Screencast]("http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vLUXjXo7MuU").  Mirrors & torrents will be set up tomorrow.

**07/31/07** [Ubuntu Ultimate Edition 1.4 DVD]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_1.4/") is now uploading ~2GB will take nothing short of 9 hours to upload, mirrors please stand by.  Many [wireless issues]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3063468") have been addressed and integrated onto the DVD ([screenshots]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3111424&postcount=379")).

### Post by lotuseclat79 on 2007-09-14
Hi,

I was wondering how to modify the black theme border of Ultimate Ubuntu 1.4 in just the toolbar area without changing the theme to use a lighter color so that the text fields of one of my exentions to FF 2.0.0.6 shows through.

The extension that I have uses a text that is also black and does not show the values.  Consequently, since the text of the extension looks somewhat difficult to change, and I have not been able to change the text color of the extension via about:config a couple of questions are raised:

1) Is there an about:config item that controls the text color of extensions?  Note: by messing around with the few color items in about:config and then reverting about:config back to default - the color of the extension text fields do show up in green on black - but, I have to do that repeatedly since I only use UU1.4 Live CD.

2) I am not sure whether a userChrome.css file could do the job and I am not conversant in .css.

3) Short of changing the theme which I know works is there a way to get a lighter colored toolbar section of the FF browser?  Or is the theme change the only way to go?

Thanks for any input in advance,

-- Tom

P.S.  I have contacted the extension authors and they did not seem to have a clue.

---

### Post by CaptainInsaneO on 2007-09-14
I'm wondering this as well... Azureus doesn't display correctly in the UU theme.

I was able to fix the Firefox problem though, try Edit>Preferences>Colors tab.

Then, find something about using system colors, make sure Firefox is NOT using system colors. That should work a treat for ya.

---

### Post by kbuzzard on 2007-09-14
I was able to fix the Firefox problem though, try Edit>Preferences>Colors tab.

YEA !!!!!!!, Nice tip, Thank You !!!

---

### Post by CaptainInsaneO on 2007-09-14
Always glad to help!

Just to clarify, I was slightly off in my last post.

To fix this problem, go to Edit>Preferences and then click the Content tab up top, then click the box that says "Colors" and go from there.

My last post was a little misleading...

Hello I am TheeMahn, and I am from the U.S.A. ;)

The reason that was happening to you is you installed the nvidia driver and then upgraded your kernel, this is how I install [UUE]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472")..

If I had a dollar for everytime that question has been asked I would be a rich man ;)  I have wrote a script that does this I have been told it does work, but have not tested it personally.  You will have to add the [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/#1.4") manually.

Please give me a few to dig through a 1000 or so posts, will get back with you on script location.  I went through 812 of them and cannot find it, I am sure I posted it, been using the time to make a [FAQ]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3370885&postcount=1041") as I went along, and still no link for you yet, If I have to I will re-write the script and create a new post for it.  I really do not care for the search features here I can't seem to get a handle on getting it to "just" search my thread.  Love the find command knew I had written and posted it:

```
theemahn@SledgeHammer:~$ sudo find / -name feisty*Password:/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-session-splashes.list
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-gdm-themes.list
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-wallpapers.md5sums
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-wallpapers.postrm
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-wallpapers.list
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-session-splashes.md5sums
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-gdm-themes.md5sums
/var/lib/dpkg/info/feisty-wallpapers.postinst
/var/log/bootchart/feisty-20070915-1.png
/var/log/bootchart/feisty-20070915-2.png
/media/BigMac/Website/scripts/feisty2ultimate.sh
/media/BigMac/Website/software/distros/feisty
/media/BigMac/Ccals_Repo/dists/feisty
/usr/share/app-install/channels/feisty-commercial.list
/usr/share/app-install/channels/feisty-commercial.eula
/usr/share/doc/feisty-gdm-themes
/usr/share/doc/feisty-session-splashes
/usr/share/doc/feisty-wallpapers
/usr/lib/python2.5/site-packages/feisty_gdm_themes-0.21.egg-info
/usr/lib/python2.5/site-packages/feisty_wallpapers-0.11.egg-info
/usr/lib/python2.5/site-packages/feisty_session_splashes-0.12.egg-info
/usr/etc/automatix2/ax_data/script_data/feisty.xml
/usr/etc/automatix2/ax_data/feisty.autoscript
/usr/etc/automatix2/ax_data/feisty_uninstall.autoscript
theemahn@SledgeHammer:~$ cat /media/BigMac/Website/scripts/feisty2ultimate.sh
#!/bin/sh
#
# Feisty to Ultimate Script 
#
# Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
rm -R *
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
chmod +x gamers.sh
cd ~/Desktop/
apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy
#create Shortcut
echo "Creating Upgrade shortcut..."
# create desktop shortcut for Upgrade
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop
theemahn@SledgeHammer:~$
```

I have now written a specific [post]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3371567&postcount=1042") pertaining to it. Only [choice]("http://www.osdisc.com/cgi-bin/view.cgi/products/dvd/ubuntuultimate"), as I see it currently and will remove every trace of that site, it has already been ordered and if it is was not exactly what he ordered (they are sending him the newer 1.4 now) will strip that site from each and every post I have made. _**F.A.Q.**_

[LIST]
[*][What is Ubuntu Ultimate]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/faq.html#ultimate")?
[*][How do I install UUE / Video Drivers & get Compiz fusion / Beryl working]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3149537&postcount=472")?
[*][Can I upgrade to UUE from Feisty]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3371567&postcount=1042")?

[*][I don't have a DVD burner do you make UUE on CD]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Ubuntu_Ultimate_CD/")?

[*][Now that I have it installed.  How can I speed this thing up]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/Howto_tweak_ubuntu_ultimate.html")?
[*][Wow this is great, how can I make my own linux distro]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/distro.html"), or [write a module to install my custom software]("http://ubuntusoftware.info/rmods.html")?
[*][Can I install the newest kernel in UUE]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3386575&postcount=1078")?
[*][I would like to install cutting edge software, is it possible]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3386878&postcount=1080")?  (not recommended)
[*][Who do we have to thank]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=3371931&postcount=1044")?
[*][Tons of further info previous thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=309704").  Also heavily [viewed]("http://ubuntuforums.org/forumdisplay.php?f=179&daysprune=-1&order=desc&sort=views") #2, before closed.
[/LIST]

I would like to answer another question that has been asked many times in the past as well.  Do you accept donations?  No, all costs incurred in hosting fees etc. are paid through advertisements on the website, I do not feel you or anyone else should pay for any linux, it is all about "Free as in beer" ;)  I will however accept mirrors to take the tremendous loads off the servers, if you have unlimited bandwidth on a 45+ Mbit (T3 or greater) line please PM me, I will be forthcoming in volunteering the sort of traffic the main download server sees (gigabit shared connection), I believe it to be 9 maybe 10 days worth of traffic:

One of the mirrors we have thanks to ccal (he provides 2 mirrors of the same capacity currently)has been solid 100 of 100 slots full since the release of 1.4, 1.5 is coming.
```
4963994.45 MB of 250000.00 MB (1985.60%)
```

About 5 Terabytes, this does not include mirrors or torrent traffic, ccal smokes my stats by the way, pretty sad I made it and he is the one getting hammered.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-15
This post is being made just for those that don't want to take the "Nestea plunge" ;)  This is a question that has plagued me in the past (tons of PM's) and I am now taking the time to address this issue for the second time, could not find my previous post ;)

**Can I upgrade from Feisty to UUE?**

Technically no, but you can get close to it will not change your theme background and probably a chunk of software is missed but a great start.  I have taken the time to write a script just for you fellas that addresses this issue it will get you onboard, before doing any of the below you will need to add my [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/#1.4").  This has only been tested (and not by me) using Feisty Fawn, nothing for Edgy based and there won't be..  As always I publish my code openly:
```

#!/bin/sh
#
# Feisty to Ultimate Script 
#
# Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
sudo mkdir /usr/share/ultimate
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm -R *
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/blackcry.mod
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/halloween_theme.s3m
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/tunez/NIN_HEAD.S3M
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/bootscript.sh
sudo wget http://ubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/ubuntu_ico.png
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh
cd ~/Desktop/
sudo apt-get install -y --allow-unauthenticated envy
#create Shortcut
sudo echo "Creating Upgrade shortcut..."
# create desktop shortcut for Upgrade
echo "[Desktop Entry]
Version=1.0
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Upgrade
Type=Application
Exec=gksudo sh /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh
Icon=/usr/share/ultimate/ubuntu_ico.png
Terminal=True
Categories=GNOME;GTK;Application;System;" > ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

# chmod shortcut
sudo chmod a+x ~/Desktop/Upgrade.desktop

```

Attached below is the script, for those that don't like the copy & paste method.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-17
> **mikeescott said:**
> HI all. I got my gamers 1.4 dvd from OSDiscs and got it all installed and updated. I did an error check on the dvd before installing and there was an error in 1 file. Is this normal or should I ask OSDiscs to replace it? I got an error message when loading from dvd about gnome deamon something. I have a screenshot at home and I am at work now, so I will post them later. I also got an error when Envy was loading the ATI drivers. I tried to play a few of the games and they were very slow and choppy. 1 of the cpu's was maxed out, but the other 3 had little activity. Am I doing something wrong or is that just the was it is? Would I be better off with the 64 bit version? I could not find it at OSDiscs and I have been trying the download for over a week. I have also not been able to get the desktop cube or wabbly windows to work. Also, when I try to open a usb drive from the desktop I cannot access the documents. Help???? let me know if there is anymore info you need to help me. Thanks, Mike

In the first screenshot you are running the 64Bit edition, look at the driver x86_64.run (I really did not want to make a 64 bit edition, for reasons such as this, let me see if ATI even makes a 64Bit driver); Envy doesn't appear to support ATI on a 64bit platform or at least that is what envy was attempting to fetch (did you try the restricted manager), and yes I did notice what it initially said .  Second shot yes this is normal if you have previously partitioned your drive and goes away after installation.  This is a bug in ubiquity and has been filed.  What filesystem NTFS, etc. are you trying to access on third screenshot, and second of all is it encrypted?  I notice it is called "My Documents" from a windows install I assume this is encrypted and they do so to keep you from seeing the contents of another user, there are ways around this but is considered "hacking".   I'll get back with you a few with your answer on ATI, brb.

Actually before I waste my time, envy reported 32 bit and tried to grab 64 bit reason beyond me.

Please drop this in a terminal and give me the output should be either 32 or 64
```
getconf WORD_BIT
```

I downloaded a few programs in Automatrix and when I was done, all of my frives were mounted and accessible (XP, XP64, Ubuntu 7.04, UUE Gamers[each on a seperate drive], a drive for backups, and 2 external drives). My problem now is getting the cube and wabbly windows to work and the games to not be so slow. It looks like Envy is trying to install 64 bit drivers on my 32 bit OS.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-18
> **atlfalcons866 said:**
> could i put 2.6.22 on this?

Here is how I would do it:

1. Backup sources list:
```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.bak
```
2. Change sources list to gustys:
```
sudo sed -e 's/\sfeisty/ gutsy/g' -i /etc/apt/sources.list
```
3. Update sources:
```
sudo apt-get update
```
You will probably get a few errors as my repo does not have gusty based packages currently, you can ignore these errors.

4. upgrade just the kernel & restricted:
```
sudo apt-get install linux-headers-2.6.22-11 linux-headers-2.6.22-11-generic linux-image-2.6.22-11-generic linux-restricted-modules-2.6.22-11-generic linux-ubuntu-modules-2.6.22-11-generic
```
5. Restore your list:
```
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.bak /etc/apt/sources.list
```
6. Update:
```
sudo apt-get update
```

You will probably have to remove and re-install your video drivers after rebooting, using the new kernel.  I have not tested the method above, so you use it at your own risk ;)  I should not have posted that without first trying it my self now I have and it does work, if you don't know how to remove and reinstall video drivers / Wireless if you have it I would steer clear of this post.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-18
Not that I have to I would like to share how to get the most cutting edge software **Be aware this could bork your system** This is not recommended for just anyone, this indeed could cause you to have to reinstall, you have been warned...

The reason I am posting this as well as the above post, I have noticed that people would like to get the most out of their computer, I wanted to post this before I have had even 1 beer ;)  First we are going to update our sources list using Trevino's sources list please note this will make your sources list huge probably 70 repos or so.

1. install the sources list:
```
sudo apt-get install 3v1n0-sources-list
```

2. Once it is installed we will have to make 1 adjustment to the list:

```
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

search through the list and "find" **randumb** remove that entire section, I also suggest you re-add my [repo]("http://repoubuntusoftware.info/") to the end, for the upgrade script purposes.  save and close. 

3. Grab the keys for each repo:
```
sudo apt-get update 2> /tmp/keymissing; for key in $(grep "NO_PUBKEY" /tmp/keymissing |sed "s/.*NO_PUBKEY //"); do echo -e "\nProcessing key: $key"; gpg --keyserver subkeys.pgp.net --recv $key && gpg --export --armor $key | sudo apt-key add -; done
```

4. Update:
```
sudo apt-get update
```

5. Upgrade:
```
sudo apt-get upgrade
```

Enjoy your cutting edge beryl, amarok, compiz-fusion etc.

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-21
> **enixidfrag said:**
> i completly agree man i got a new PC here right Core Duo 2 GB ram nvidia gforce 8800 sli and vista still took FOREVER to boot the heck up :(

so first thing i did was remove vista and replace with xp and i wanted to tri-boot xp/linux/mac osx :D and fell in LOVE with ubuntu :D

heres my ubuntu on the other hand notice the sytem resource monitor in the taskbar can you see ANY activity ROFL

8800 SLI... I bet your video totally rocks...  to use SLI AA in ubuntu add the following to your XORG **device** section sudo gediit /etc/X11/xorg.conf

```
    Option         "MultiGPU" "1"
    Option         "SLI" "AA"
```


Should look similar to:

```
Section "Device"
    Identifier     "nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7600 GT]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option         "MultiGPU" "1"
    Option         "SLI" "AA"
    Option         "Coolbits" "1"
    Option         "TripleBuffer" "1"
    Option         "CursorShadowAlpha" "64"
    Option         "CursorShadowXOffse" "4"
    Option         "CursorShadowYOffset" "2"
    Option         "CursorShadow" "TRUE"
EndSection
```

I suggest the coolbits too will allow you to overclock it not that you need too ;)  Just thought I would try and help and point out a few things that are not so obvious to the common user.  When you view your card you will see that it shows SLI mode is enabled such as shot below.  Overclocking options also shown.  If you already knew this sorry for wasting space to post, just like to help out when I can :)

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-22
> **IceReaver75 said:**
> This is the 2nd time I down loaded the DVD iso.  The first time I had the same error when I burned it and now the same thing with the 2nd download.  I didn't check the download.  I burned the the disk at 1x so I did burn it as slow as I possibly could cause I do know the slower the better.

If you downloaded the 1.5 yes there may be a chance it was corrupted, however that was only available to beta testers & mirrors you should not have even seen it.  md5sums are accurate, in most cases tested personally.  I hope you are not referring to the "onboard" test on the boot menu, if so probably your first one was fine.

The iso is supposed to be tested prior to even wasting a disk, welcome aboard md5sum.  You are correct in slower the better as far as burning.  I'm gonna step out on a limb here and say not only your second is good but first as well.  I suppose an explanation is in order for all there is a file called md5sum.txt in the root of the cd which reconstructor evidently does not do it job on to modify to make it match what the md5sum is going to be for the newly created ISO & is the reason you see that.

---

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-09-22
> **enixidfrag said:**
> yeah i FINALLY got some beans >.<

btw it will take me 2 days about for the upload to be finished and the rs links **WONT** die cause they are on my prem account ;)

also if u need any help with the forum i could help with that ive become EXCEDINGLY good at that now ;)

and btw should i be able to see sli some where in the nvidia settings once i added to the xconf cause i dont ROFL and whats the thing you were talkijn bout for overclocking?

I placed a post, not that he asked I just figured you have it why not use it (on the SLI post), he certainly did not need to overclock it is always nice to have the option though right?  I will label it out for you, I do suppose I did not take the time then will do so now (my xorg):

```
Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "Coolbits" "1"
    Option         "TripleBuffer" "1"
    Option         "CursorShadowAlpha" "64"
    Option         "CursorShadowXOffse" "4"
    Option         "CursorShadowYOffset" "2"
    Option         "CursorShadow" "TRUE"
    Option         "SLI" "AA"
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
```
My screen section, I will not go any further then what I told him and please **DO NOT** think you can just paste it into yours.  The:
```
Option         "Coolbits" "1"
```
as you see above will allow over clocking of your video card (nvidia only).

I appreciate the offer as far as forum, many in mind before you; please don't take that wrong.  Don't you think ccal should be boss, if he were to accept the option?  I greatly appreciate that you will help take the load off the servers.  A forum at best is a ways away I want people I can "100%" trust, and not have to worry about it (won't have time building your 1.6 etc.).  etc. is a huge word to me by the way...

### Post by drama1981 on 2007-09-23
> **TheeMahn2003 said:**
> Well I am running 1.5 and tried what you refered to as a problem see screenshot.  When I get done with my overwhelming load of things to do I will install 1.4 and see if I can re-create the problem you are having.  Unless someone else would like to try ;)

just installed uue 1.5 (hdd died and couldnt find my 1.4 disc). i figured out the problem though. it ended up being a hardware issue. i have 2 soundcards. a crappy onboard and a pci card one. it turns out my pci soundcard and wine  (among other progs) just dont agree with each other. removed and viola wine now works flawlessly (well as flawlessly as can be expected). however i went through the system logs before pulling the card (this is what first tipped me to the fact it was the sound). i see the following entry:

ice1724: no matching model found for id 0xc3140517
ice1724: invalid eeprom version 1

does anybody know exactly what this means or how to fix it? i dont remember ever seeing the same error when i used the standard version of ubuntu or ubuntu studio. thats not to say it wasnt there i just never had a reason to check the logs. starnge thing is the card works fine except i cant get 5.1 to work only my front channels have sound no rear center or LFE . 

not a huge deal to me but i thought id give you a heads up as im sure everyone else may very well have the same prob. ran into a problem trying to run the initial updates. there seems to be a problem with authentication. i dont know if its incompatable packages or if it lies elsewhere.

hal
hal-info
kaffeine
kdebase-bin
kdebase-data
kdebase-kio-plugins
kdesktop
kicker
koffice-data
koffice-libs
krita
krita-data

them are the ones that wont update. strabgely krita and krita-data have already been updated once but for some reason it says there is another update available. ive tried everything i can think of but still no luck. ive version locked all them programs for the time being though (had to do it once before with gimp i think). have attached 2 screenshots also. any help from anybody would be greatly appreciated. thankx in advance :)

drama

p.s. excellent job on the new release. it seems more polished and runs much smoother im my opinion. kudos for adding kde too. its a much welcomed addition in my opinion. :)

you can use the 1.4 cd and do an install and then upgrade your 1.4 to the 1.5 dvd. It will take a lot of downloads though. [1.4 cd iso = 685 mb, 1.5 dvd iso = 2.18 gb] it will work though.

that is the link for the 1.4 cd.

them are the ones that wont update. strabgely krita and krita-data have already been updated once but for some reason it says there is another update available. ive tried everything i can think of but still no luck. ive version locked all them programs for the time being though (had to do it once before with gimp i think). have attached 2 screenshots also. any help from anybody would be greatly appreciated. thankx in advance :)

drama

p.s. excellent job on the new release. it seems more polished and runs much smoother im my opinion. kudos for adding kde too. its a much welcomed addition in my opinion. :)




I was having a similar problem found in your screenshots on my 1.4 gamers install, but when I reformatted and installed 1.5 my problem is exactly like yours with the same partial upgrade list.

---

### Post by drama1981 on 2007-09-25
> **mikeescott said:**
> I was having a similar problem found in your screenshots on my 1.4 gamers install, but when I reformatted and installed 1.5 my problem is exactly like yours with the same partial upgrade list.

i just found a solution to this. if you open a terminal and do sudo apt-get dist-upgrade it solves the problem. it will update all of them but it also removes a fair number of packages kaffeine-xine and kubuntu-desktop are just a few that i can think of right off hand that it removes. so far letting it remove the package sit wanted to hasnt caused any problems so far as they get replaced by other packages aside from maybe kaffeine-xine. you will also lose the kubuntu splash screen that shows up when you log into a kde session but the desktop still loads fine. there will still be two packages that are held back but it doesnt seem to cause problems while running updates (no more partial update warnings).

---

### Post by drama1981 on 2007-09-26
> **IceReaver75 said:**
> Well I don't know what else to try here.  I first downloaded 1.4 DVD version and burned it to disk and I got an

tty; job control turned off error 

so I downloaded the 1.5 DVD version.  md5sum checked it and everything checked fine.  Burned it to disk at a speed of 2x.  Restarted the computer and the same problem with the 1.5 version

tty; job control turned off error

i had a similar problem. when the disc boots hit f6 and remove "quiet splash- (may be splash quiet but either way remove both) and hit enter. look for any errors or anything that seems out of place. do you have a floppy drive or card reader? that ended up being my problem. it kept trying to access the floppy and card reader. so i unplugged both and it then booted fine. after install i plugged both back in and havent had a problem since. it may not work in your case but it is something to try.

### Post by drama1981 on 2007-09-28
> **PharaohSD said:**
> Hey Themann

Thanks for the wonderful distro,  probably the only thing that still keeps me on linux :) (and a special thanks goes to ccal, for keeping  the torrent  alive :) )

I do have a couple of problems; (running the new 1.5)

1) The Upgrade script doesn't work for me (worked right after i installed ultimate, but after that, every time i run it it pops up, asking for pass, but then flashes and closes.)

2) the  Ubuntu update manager doesn't work, it keeps giving these errors:[look at the 3 scr shots attatched] how do i fix that?

3) I am trying to install the latest version of conky 1.4.7? (or something like that) but when i go to synaptic packet manager the highest version it has there (yes, after refresh update) is 1.4.5....why? maybe the repo's that are installed haven't been updated?

thanks in advanced theeman/whoever answers..

i cant help with your first or third problem. i dont see your screenshots. but i have a feeling that your second problem (update manager) is the same as i was having. check the screenshots in my previous post found here

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3415814#post3415814](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=3415814#post3415814)

mainly look at the first one as the second on is the same process just ran from the terminal as opposed to the gui. anyway if you look at my first screenshot it obviously wont let me update. i didnt take a snap of the screen before that but it basically says "some updates cant be installed." i then click partial update. it then goes to the error in the first screenshot. if this is the problem you have simply open a terminal and do sudo apt-get dist-upgrade. this will tell you its removing some packages but dont be alarmed they get replaced with other packages. this will remove the splash screen that shows when you log into a kde session though. however the desktop still loads fine. the only package that gets removed and isnt replaced that i can think of that may cause problems down the road is "kaffeine-xine" but i cant verify if it will actually cause probs though because i dont use kaffine.

the reason why you have to do this is because there are certain dependencies that you need but for some reason the update manager wont or cant grab them. hope this helps.

drama

p.s. sorry if this is difficult to read or make sense of my sentence structure isnt the best.

p.p.s you may also want to open software sources and ensure all boxes are checked on the first tab except maybe cd as well as the first two boxes ONLY on the second tab.before trying this. idk if it would make a difference but thats how i have mine set.

---

```

sudo rm gamers.sh

sudo chmod +x gamers.sh
```

### Post by drama1981 on 2007-09-29
> **PharaohSD said:**
> Thanks!
worked like a charm, (don't know why the scr shots didn't go, managed to get 1 up, the other two won't go...think it's the size :s)
question about this, it upgraded the linux kernel, (from -15 to -16) now every time i boot in the grub menu i get like 6 linux options (kernel 15, 15 something, 16, 16 something, memory test, something else and 2 of my vista install (main & recovery)

will the kernel's keep piling up in my grub menu every time there is a kernel update?

thanks again!



your welcome. glad to hear it helped you too. as far as the grub menu goes. im sure you know why there is 2 for 15 and 2 for 16 (your standard kernel as well as one for recovery i suppose incase you need to fix something). your vista entry is slightly different. vista is to actually boot into vista while with the vista recovery line would launch your recovery partition (hp recovery) and do a complete reimage back to the way it was shipped from the factory (again im sure you already knew that). but as far as your actual question yes each time your kernel updates it will add 2 new line in the grub menu. you can tweak this though. some people prefer to only have the grub entries shown that they actually use. i on the other hand prefer to keep at least 2 entries just incase for some reason one of them quits booting. if you want to edit this open a terminal window. do 

sudo gedit /boot/grub/menu.lst  

for clarification thats not a typo its supposed to be .lst not .list (been asked that before so i thought id add it)

this will open your grub menu in a text editor.

if you scroll down (almost to the bottom) you will see something similar to this (yours will obviously have a different kernel listed as i just pasted these entries from another page as a reference but the idea is the same)

title Ubuntu, kernel 2.6.10-5-386
root (hd1,2)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.10-5-386 root=/dev/hdb3 ro quiet splash
initrd /boot/initrd.img-2.6.10-5-386
savedefault
boot

title Ubuntu, kernel 2.6.10-5-386 (recovery mode)
root (hd1,2)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.10-5-386 root=/dev/hdb3 ro single
initrd /boot/initrd.img-2.6.10-5-386
savedefault
boot

add # at the beginning of the title line you want to hide. it would then look like this

#title Ubuntu, kernel 2.6.10-5-386 (recovery mode)
root (hd1,2)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.10-5-386 root=/dev/hdb3 ro single
initrd /boot/initrd.img-2.6.10-5-386
savedefault
boot

then click save and your done. please take care when editing this file though as you can easily make your system unbootable if you screw up. i always double check the file after editing before i save just to be sure i have done it correctly. hope this clears it up. cheers :)

---

### Post by itm on 2007-09-29
I've just installed Ubuntu Ultimate 1.4 on a Windows XP2 SP2 machine, running VMWare Player2.0.1.55017. I followed the Install Guide, as I'm a Linux newbie. Everything went OK apart from getting access to drives on the host windows machine - when I double-click on Workgroup I see no machine listed, and when I try directly entering the smb://hostname address I can't see it either. Internet access is fine from the Ubuntu VM. 

IPConfig on the host machine shows the following:
Ethernet adapter VMware Network Adapter VMnet8:

        Connection-specific DNS Suffix  . :
        IP Address. . . . . . . . . . . . : 192.168.184.1
        Subnet Mask . . . . . . . . . . . : 255.255.255.0
        Default Gateway . . . . . . . . . :

Ethernet adapter VMware Network Adapter VMnet1:

        Connection-specific DNS Suffix  . :
        IP Address. . . . . . . . . . . . : 192.168.222.1
        Subnet Mask . . . . . . . . . . . : 255.255.255.0
        Default Gateway . . . . . . . . . :

Ethernet adapter Local Area Connection 2:

        Media State . . . . . . . . . . . : Media disconnected

Ethernet adapter Local Area Connection:

        Connection-specific DNS Suffix  . :
        IP Address. . . . . . . . . . . . : 192.168.1.8
        Subnet Mask . . . . . . . . . . . : 255.255.255.0
        Default Gateway . . . . . . . . . : 192.168.1.1

The Eth1 adapter of the VM shows that it uses DHCP to allocate an address.

Does anyone have any idea what I've done wrong?

### Post by PharaohSD on 2007-09-30
> **mwob said:**
> Hi,

I hope this isn't the wrong place to post this...

I recently installed UU1.4 from the DVD. When it finished, I did a dist-upgrade to update everything, and then  tried to run the "update" icon on the desktop... But the icons doesn;t do anything!

It seems to want a file called /usr/share/ultimate/gamers.sh, but this file is empty!!

Can anyone offer any help?

this worked for me

in terminal paste:

```
cd /usr/share/ultimate/
sudo rm gamers.sh
sudo wget http://repoubuntusoftware.info/upgrades/gamers.sh
sudo chmod +x gamers.sh
```

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-10-02
> **PmDematagoda said:**
> Excuse me TheeMahn.

When I run the upgrade script, it asks me whether "I wish to grab the default packages for the above distro.", what does this mean and do I need to do that when I already have Gnome, KDE, Xfce and Enlightenment?

The script was originally developed with 1.4 in mind, more specific 1.4 CD.  The next update you as well as all the rest will be able to update to Gusty, or maintain feisty based (1.5 option becomes available for those that run 1.4).  It will be a heavy undertaking believe that, when I upgraded from 1.5 to gusty it downloaded 1,100 updates, it indeed may be less of a burden on the end user to grab the 1.6 DVD and have it over with.  I probably won't release the script until I have a 1.6 built (I use this to test the script).

If you select Gamers it will download & install a bunch of games (originally from 1.4 Gamers).  I suggest custom so you get exactly what you want, unless you have a ton of space, won't slow it down like windows does.  There is no such thing as bloatware here ;)

The upgrade script will handle it all for you, Transition by your choice to Gusty or you can remain with Feisty based.  I have already upgraded to Gusty from 1.5, all from a script I wrote and will not publish it openly until it is "Final" & well tested.  If you would like to be a beta tester  before it gets centrally served please PM me, I will send you the "code", it will modify your sources list , and download a ton.  But my repo does not get removed so yes still UUE ;)

Until I get the bugs worked out, be prepared to re-insall, if this is not something you can handle (or if your system is a production system) maybe best left alone.  While testing I have installed as many as 5 times in 1 day.  Find a bug fix it, re-install & test  ;)

### Post by TheeMahn2003 on 2007-10-04
Alot of talk about themes here allow me to share with you a new USplash I made... This will be used in 1.6, I spent all day re-writing the code for it until I archived perfection ;)  It works perfectly at 640 X 480+ the 400 is fall back to ensure compatibility with all cards.  Loads into startup manager current resolutions supported:

640 X 400 --> Untested
640 X 480
800 X 600
1024 X 768
1280 X 1024

Both graphical and text mode are supported (text mode is what made it take so long).

As always I present my source openly:

```
/* Ubuntu Ultimate Usplash Maker
 *
# Copyright (c) 2007  Ubuntusoftware Team <http://ubuntusoftware.info>
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
 */

#include <usplash-theme.h>
/* Needed for the custom drawing functions */
#include <usplash_backend.h>
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_usplash_640_400, pixmap_usplash_640_480;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_usplash_800_600, pixmap_usplash_1024_768, pixmap_usplash_1280_1024;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_back_640_400;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_back_640_480;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_back_800_600;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_back_1024_768;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_back_1280_1024;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_fore_640_400;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_fore_640_480;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_fore_800_600;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_fore_1024_768;
extern struct usplash_pixmap pixmap_throbber_fore_1280_1024;
extern struct usplash_font font_helvB10;

void t_init(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_clear_progressbar_640_400(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_clear_progressbar_640_480(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_clear_progressbar_800_600(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_clear_progressbar_1024_768(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_clear_progressbar_1280_1024(struct usplash_theme* theme);
void t_draw_progressbar_640_400(struct usplash_theme* theme, int percentage);
void t_draw_progressbar_640_480(struct usplash_theme* theme, int percentage);
void t_draw_progressbar_800_600(struct usplash_theme* theme, int percentage);
void t_draw_progressbar_1024_768(struct usplash_theme* theme, int percentage);
void t_draw_progressbar_1280_1024(struct usplash_theme* theme, int percentage);
void t_animate_step_640_400(struct usplash_theme* theme, int pulsating);
void t_animate_step_640_480(struct usplash_theme* theme, int pulsating);
void t_animate_step_800_600(struct usplash_theme* theme, int pulsating);
void t_animate_step_1024_768(struct usplash_theme* theme, int pulsating);
void t_animate_step_1280_1024(struct usplash_theme* theme, int pulsating);


struct usplash_theme usplash_theme_640_480;
struct usplash_theme usplash_theme_800_600;
struct usplash_theme usplash_theme_1024_768;
struct usplash_theme usplash_theme_1280_1024;

struct usplash_theme usplash_theme = {
	.version = THEME_VERSION, /* ALWAYS set this to THEME_VERSION, 
                                 it's a compatibility check */
    .next = &usplash_theme_640_480,
    .ratio = USPLASH_4_3,

	/* Background and font */
	.pixmap = &pixmap_usplash_640_400,
	.font   = &font_helvB10,

	/* Palette indexes */
	.background             = 0x0,
  	.progressbar_background = 0x7,
  	.progressbar_foreground = 0x156,
	.text_background        = 0x15,
	.text_foreground        = 0x31,
	.text_success           = 0x171,
	.text_failure           = 0x156,

	/* Progress bar position and size in pixels */
  	.progressbar_x      = 212, /* 640/2-216/2 */
  	.progressbar_y      = 321,
  	.progressbar_width  = 216,
  	.progressbar_height = 8,

	/* Text box position and size in pixels */
  	.text_x      = 120,
  	.text_y      = 307,
  	.text_width  = 360,
  	.text_height = 100,

	/* Text details */
  	.line_height  = 15,
  	.line_length  = 32,
  	.status_width = 35,

    /* Functions */
    .init = t_init,
    .clear_progressbar = t_clear_progressbar_640_400,
    .draw_progressbar = t_draw_progressbar_640_400,
    .animate_step = t_animate_step_640_400,
};

	.version = THEME_VERSION, /* ALWAYS set this to THEME_VERSION, 
                                 it's a compatibility check */
    .next = &usplash_theme_800_600,
    .ratio = USPLASH_4_3,

	/* Background and font */
	.pixmap = &pixmap_usplash_640_480,
	.font   = &font_helvB10,

	/* Palette indexes */
	.background             = 0x0,
  	.progressbar_background = 0x7,

	.text_background        = 0x15,
	.text_foreground        = 0x31,
	.text_success           = 0x171,
	.text_failure           = 0x156,

	/* Progress bar position and size in pixels */
  	.progressbar_x      = 212, /* 640/2-216/2 */
  	.progressbar_y      = 291,
  	.progressbar_width  = 216,
  	.progressbar_height = 8,

	/* Text box position and size in pixels */
  	.text_x      = 160,

  	.text_height = 100,

	/* Text details */

};

	.version = THEME_VERSION,
    .next = &usplash_theme_1280_1024,

	/* Background and font */
